records.englishTranslation:
Wednesday, 30 OCTOBER 2024
FIRST SESSION
Opening Time: 10.03
CHAIRMAN: Mehmet MUŞ (Samsun)
VICE CHAIRMAN: İsmail Faruk AKSU (Istanbul)
Spokesperson: Orhan ERDEM (Konya)
CLERK: Nilgün ÖK (Denizli)
PRESIDENT MEHMET MUŞ - Dear members of our commission, our esteemed Vice President, our Head of Strategy and Budget, our esteemed President of the Court of Accounts, esteemed bureaucrats of public institutions and organizations, esteemed representatives, distinguished members of our press; I salute you with respect. I wish you a good morning.
I am opening the 4th Meeting of our Commission.
II.- LEGAL PROPOSALS AND OTHER BUSINESSES
A.- Bills
1.- 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal (1/278) and 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal (1/277) and Court of Accounts memorandums ()
CHAIRMAN MEHMET MUŞ - Our agenda includes discussions on the 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal, the 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal and the Court of Accounts reports in general.
Our Vice President made the overall budget presentation last week, so we will move directly to the groups.
The first word belongs to Mr. Rahmi Aşkın Türeli from the Republican People's Party Group.
Mr. Türeli, come on.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (İzmir) - Thank you, Mr. President.
Dear members of the Planning and Budget Commission, esteemed Vice President, President of the Court of Accounts, esteemed bureaucrats, esteemed members of our press; I greet you all with respect.
Of course, we received a presentation last week, Mr. Vice President made a presentation to us on the entire budget. We are starting these budget negotiations as of today. This will be a long process, we always compare it to a marathon.
Of course, we always say that, last year, in the budget, even on the first day, when you made your presentation, I took the floor on the procedure, and in the speech I made on the procedure, I said that the fact that this General Economic Targets and Investments table and the report containing the tables were not distributed to us on time created a great deficiency. The same thing actually applies to the 2025 Presidential annual program because there is a lot of detail here. So, if we were to talk about all of it today, if we had had the information here at least a few days ago, we could have had a healthier discussion system by looking at the numbers here. Of course, we will talk about these and use them in the next ministry budgets, but as I said, I think it is important for the health of the negotiations that these should be distributed to us in advance. We want and demand from you, especially as the Chairman of the Planning and Budget Commission, that this should happen in the upcoming budget negotiations.
Dear MPs, yesterday, October 29, we celebrated the 101st anniversary of the establishment of our republic. On the anniversary of the founding of our Republic, I commemorate Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, his comrades in arms, members of our Veteran Assembly, our martyrs and veterans with respect, mercy and gratitude. Keeping the Republic alive and defending it forever is extremely important for all of us, we are aware of this, we always act within this framework and will continue to act.

Now, of course, within the presentation of the Vice President, we actually opened a procedural discussion this year and said: We said and revealed that both the final account bill and the general compliance declaration of the Court of Accounts prevented us from discussing the final account bill in a healthy way. Because frankly, the difference between the initial appropriations and year-end appropriations of the 2023 budget was neither included in the final account bill nor in the general compliance notification of the Court of Accounts. In other words, if the appropriation was increased here, on what basis was it increased, under what laws was it increased, what kind of decisions were taken, where were they spent? We may see the expenditures, but we do not see in which appropriation increase they are used or spent. That's why they actually need to be healthy. We said it last year, let's repeat our suggestion this year, it is actually appropriate for the final accounting bill to be discussed in a separate commission, and this is the right thing. Of course, this requires a change in the Internal Regulations, but our proposal last year was this: Establishing a sub-commission. Because, as you know, the arrival of the final accounting bill to the Parliament is earlier, at the end of June or mid-September, and the Court of Accounts report is accordingly. In this sense, a sub-commission can be established, and within the current structure, this sub-commission needs to be discussed in a healthy manner, within the framework of both the final account bill and the Court of Accounts reports, the basic reports of the Court of Accounts - the 4 basic reports - and also the reports prepared by the Court of Accounts by institutions. I think we will get information about this issue today because it is a very important issue. In other words, we said that the Court of Accounts' general compliance notification prevented us from seeing the 2023 budget implementation results in a healthy way. In this sense, knowing in detail where this was spent, if there was an increase in appropriations, where they were increased, where these appropriations were spent, why they were not included in the general compliance notification of the Court of Accounts; We want to know all of these.
Now, of course, today we will talk about the economy and the budget. The Turkish economy is in a serious economic crisis. That the growth rate of the economy has slowed down and unemployment rates remain high, I do not only say this in terms of narrowly defined unemployment, but also in terms of broadly defined, idle labor force. On the other hand, we are experiencing a process in which income distribution is deteriorating, poverty is increasing and inflation cannot be prevented. In fact, all of these show us that, of course, the problems experienced in the economy are important, but I think the economy should be moved beyond the framework of a stand-alone analysis. When I look at it this way, there is a serious collapse not only in the economy, but also in the economy, social and political areas. There is a serious collapse of institutional structure in Turkey. We have seen that basic public services, education and health are increasingly marketized and monetized, and we also saw this in the most recent incident that we call the "private hospital gang, newborn gang".

In other words, we see that this system, a correct system, has not been established, there are major gaps in the system, the necessary inspections have not been carried out, and as a result, Turkey is in a serious institutional collapse. Problems in the employment market continue, crime rates are rising, gangs and mafia formation are everywhere, violence has become increasingly common in every aspect of life; From violence against women to violence against children, child abuse, we are in such a structure today. So, actually, while we are talking about this whole budget, we need to talk about these as well. Why is Türkiye in such an institutional collapse? Why did Türkiye face a situation, a portrait in which all values ​​were worn and eroded? This is an issue that needs to be addressed in detail. This collapse, this corruption, this decay continues to show its serious effects today. That's why, of course, we will talk about the budget, but all of these need to be discussed. Of course, the reasons for their emergence are not only economic, but we need to reveal why Turkey is in this institutional collapse today by looking at it from all economic, social, political and sociological frameworks and perspectives.
Today we will talk about budget, budget is a resource distribution mechanism. Budgets are technical texts, of course, but they are also political texts, political economy texts. Taxes on the use of resources. What are we doing in the budget? As a result, the state receives a part of the citizen's income through taxation through budgeting, confiscates it and allocates it to certain expenditures. Here, taxes, from whom taxes are collected, what types of taxes they are, in which areas the expenditures are made, and who benefits from them, are also a political economy issue, a framework. In this sense, budgets are also seen as a secondary distribution mechanism, causing secondary distribution of income. Of course, the choices here are how governments approach the issue and what kind of Türkiye they design, and who they choose within this Türkiye. This is also valid in all these areas, in terms of labor-capital relations, in terms of sectors, in terms of regions, in terms of class. In other words, the problems that Turkey is experiencing today, the structural problems that cannot be solved and come to the fore again and again, are not fate, but ultimately, as I said, they are issues that emerged as a result of the conscious choices of the governments and powers that rule Turkey. That's why we will always ask these questions here. Do the Medium Term Program and budgets in front of us today - which also forms the framework of the budget, for three years - have a perspective that can solve these problems? Or, as I said, was it created to protect and maintain the current situation by increasing the same things as the budgets of previous years, by increasing expenditures, revenues, at a certain rate of inflation, without seriously changing or intervening in these structures? you need to see this.
I would like to say this at the beginning: The budget does not have a perspective that can solve these problems, the institutional collapse that Turkey is in, the structural problems of Turkey that I mentioned.

The medium-term program is a stabilization program; It is not a development program and what is very interesting is that its relationship with the Twelfth Development Plan has been completely cut off. If you remember, while the budget was being discussed last year, it was squeezed in right before the budget, we also talked about the Twelfth Development Plan and there I made a comparison between the development plan and the medium-term program because the Twelfth Development Plan covered the years 2024-2028 - five years - but the medium-term program covered its three years, 2024-2026. There I said that the medium-term program, that three-year framework and the next two years were actually disconnected from each other. I even made an analogy like this, you may remember, I said: I guess another team prepared the MTP and another team prepared the development plan because the two have nothing to do with each other. This year we saw that this is true, it is even more true because in the past, three years of those five years were MTP, now this year it has gone forward one year, it has four years, there is a serious discrepancy, a serious disconnection between those four years and the next year.
Look, in the development plan, the five-year average growth rate is 5 percent. We look at the MTP, if the targets in the MTP are achieved in four years, which do not seem very possible, in terms of growth, the growth will decrease, to reach 4.25 percent, that is, 5 percent. Let me even put it this way: A very high growth rate is required. In its newly announced report, the IMF reduced the growth rate of 2024 for Turkey to 3 percent and 2025 to 2.7 percent, so when we look at it this way, then 2024, 2025, 2026, 2027 are a separate framework, and in 2028 there should be a growth of around 10 percent, but only if the plan meets the target, that is, the growth rates are downwards, below the plan. But what is interesting is that when we look at it in terms of national income, in terms of gross domestic product, it is exactly the opposite, because according to the plan, in 2028, that is, in the last year of the plan, the gross domestic product would be 1 trillion 589 billion dollars, but now we look at the MTP, it will be 1 trillion 774 billion dollars, in 2027, a year before that. National income per capita would be 17,554 dollars in 2028, now we look at the MTP, a year ago, it will be 20,420 dollars in 2027. Now how can this happen? What is the explanation for this? Hot money, entering into a new policy based on hot money, returning to those policies again, the appreciation of the Turkish lira because, as a result, we calculate the national income in TL and then convert it into dollars. If the dollar exchange rate there is sufficiently below the inflation rate if the dollar does not increase, then seriously, the numbers here seem so inflated, that is, the growth rate slows down, but both the national income in dollars and the national income per capita increases. Let's say that the employment increase was 4 million 984 thousand people in the development plan, that is, the average is 997 thousand people, that is, approximately 1 million people. We look at the MTP, 3 million 309 thousand people in four years, an average of 827 thousand people; Employment is going down, employment growth is going down. In order to reach the figure in the development plan in 2028, employment must increase by 1 million 675 thousand people, exactly twice the average, which does not seem possible. In the current account balance development plan, there is a deficit of 2.8 billion dollars in 2028.

Let me say it again: A current account deficit of 2.8 billion dollars is predicted. We look at the MTP, in 2027, there is a deficit of 22.6 billion dollars a year ago, the current account deficit. So what will happen? So will there be a $20 billion deficit from 2027 to 2028?
Now, of course, I said these for this reason: Development plans are important, they are a five-year perspective. In a sense, MTP also constitutes its medium-term segments and there are annual programs, but the relationship between the two has been broken, meaning that the development plan has actually been thrown away. It is also clear from here that the upcoming medium-term programs are not programs that will ensure the development of Turkey, but programs that are only a stabilization program. So, what do we see when we look at the OVP now? A process of disinflation. The main aim is disinflation; Reducing the inflation rate and keeping inflation under control is within a program. It is not a stability program or a development program, but Turkey needs development. I looked at the plans of the past years. Starting from the first plan, in almost all plans, Turkey's growth target was 7 percent, even 8 percent in some years, but when we look at it here, it is much lower. So, when we look at this disinflation process, how did we get to this point? So, out of the blue, Türkiye said, "A disinflation process, let's reduce inflation in the medium-term program." He did not say this, and this was a conscious choice. This is the President's statement: "Interest is the cause, inflation is the result." It is a result of his thesis. Let's not deceive ourselves, it's not an external dynamic or anything. Of course, the external dynamic, the Russia-Ukraine War, rising oil prices and commodity prices in the world had an impact, but their impact was extremely low. The crisis that Turkey is in and today's high inflation are caused by the wrong policy implemented by Turkey. Wrong or right, maybe it was given consciously to speed up the economy, but the cost was very high; I call it "lost years", that is, a five-year period has ended. Look, it is a period of approximately two and a half years until after the elections, between September 2021 and May 2023. Now, in this structure, we see that a period of approximately two and a half years is required to reduce inflation to single digits again. What is the basic perspective of the MTP to achieve disinflation? It focuses on two things: One; In terms of demand inflation, reducing total demand means eliminating that inflationary process, you say tight monetary policy, tight fiscal policy and tight income policy, you say that all of them should be in harmony, policies that will reduce demand, so that people will not be able to spend, thus inflation will come down. Secondly, high interest rates create an economy based on hot money from abroad; As a result, it makes foreign exchange inflows to Turkey attractive. As a result, the inflow of foreign currency is based on suppressing the foreign exchange, thus eliminating cost inflation in terms of costs and introducing a disinflation process between the two.

But as I just said, this whole process, the implementation of the President's "interest is the cause, inflation is the result" thesis by the Central Bank, and the point reached today is the rise in inflation in Turkey - it was up to 85 percent, it was 19 percent - the rise in the exchange rate, the deterioration of income distribution, the increase and prevalence of poverty, and also began to seriously affect the real sector. The slowdown in the economy, the slowdown in the growth rate, and the negative figures in industrial production show us that this is the case, but this will continue during the MTP period. The MTP does not intervene in this way because the main perspective of the MTP is to carry out the disinflation process; It also has certain stages, and we try to proceed according to those stages. OVP is inconsistent within itself, for example, which is very interesting; Turkey's growth structure is based on domestic demand, a demand mostly led by private consumption. Now, we look at the MTP, the economy for the years 2025-2026-2027 is again based on domestic demand, because foreign demand or net exports have almost no contribution, it is at zero level. Current account deficit, inflation; All of these are internally inconsistent and there is no serious structural reform in the medium-term programme.
Now, let's get to the budget. I mentioned the MTP because ultimately, the budget will constitute a one-year slice of this, although there is also a three-year perspective here, but it would be right to focus especially on 2025. What is interesting is that the budget deficit is decreasing, from over 2 trillion to 1 trillion 931 billion lira. Year-end CPI is 17.5 percent, I am saying the year-end figure. Of course, here we need to take the average figures, not the end-of-year figures. When we look at that, there is the gross domestic product deflator.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN MEHMET MUŞ - I am opening your time again, Mr. Türeli.
Here you go.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (İzmir) - Thank you.
The average of all prices in the economy is 33.9 percent; Let's say 34, round calculation. The increase in spending is 31.4 percent, below the inflation deflator. In fact, when we look at it, non-interest expenses are lower by 28.9 percent, and there is a 50.2 percent increase in interest expenses. One result of this policy is that Turkey faces high interest rates again. Personnel expenses are increasing by 29.8 percent, capital expenses, that is, investment, are increasing by 8 percent; Look, this is a very serious problem because the growth of the economy and the realization of its potential depend on increasing Turkey's capital stock, that is, on investing for this purpose. The fact that investments have reached almost zero level here creates a serious problem.
The PPP model continues. 204.2 in 2025; Money to be given to bridges, highways and city hospitals built with the public-private partnership model; The total for 2025-2026-2027 is 689.1 billion lira. Agricultural supports: 135 billion lira, 2.2 per thousand; As you know, Article 21 of the Agricultural Law stipulates that the support given to agriculture from the budget must be at least 1 percent of the national income.

Mr. Vice President, actually, you know, you made another calculation there and showed these figures higher, but there are agricultural investments in it, investments are not included there, that is, those investments are counted separately, what we are talking about here is actually a transfer, that is, direct support from the budget, and this was 91.6 billion last year, 135 billion this year, 2.2 per thousand of the national income, not 1 percent. So include credit subsidies, include agricultural investments.
ERHAN USTA (Samsun) - 0.22
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (İzmir) - 2.2 per thousand.
For this reason, when you put these numbers, this does not actually happen, so you know this, both the employees here and the friends in the bureaucracy know this. It would be wrong if we sit here like this and put credit subsidies, agricultural investments that are counted elsewhere - they are already included in capital expenses - in order to make these figures seem high.
Social aid is 1 percent of the budget. We have been saying for a long time that social aid is actually inadequate in Turkey; The OECD average is 2.5 percent; This year it was 1 percent again. You give the numbers, but they need to be proportioned.
The damage caused by exchange rate protected deposits continues. The last time the Governor of the Central Bank made a presentation here, we asked there, I could not get the exact figures, but when we look at certain figures in the Central Bank's balance sheet, we see that it has exceeded 300 billion liras as of now. Last year, the Central Bank showed a loss of 830 billion liras. It is very clear why the Central Bank made this loss in a place where all public banks, public private banks and all financial institutions made very high profits, because the introduction of the thesis "Interest is the cause, inflation is the result" brought Turkey face to face with the exchange rate protected deposit system in September 2021, at the end of December 2021, and as a result, very high losses and this continues. In a way, we said at the time that this is a ticking time bomb, a very serious problem, and this is also a transfer of wealth, because when we look at the deposit structure in Turkey, the rate of those holding large deposits is very low, and the share of those with over 1 million deposits in the total is even less than 1 percent.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - Technically, please explain this wealth transfer so we can learn.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (İzmir) - It is a transfer, I just said it, let's remove it, BRSA's responsibility.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - I want to hear it technically.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (İzmir) - I will say it, Mr. Yegin, we say it, I will say it.
Look, BRSA has numbers, in a five-fold division, here are those with deposits up to 10 thousand, here are 10 thousand-50 thousand, 50 thousand-250 thousand, 250 thousand-1 million and above 1 million; When we look at it this way, the percentage of people with large amounts of money is extremely low.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - I say this with all my heart.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (İzmir) - Let me finish my speech, we will talk later.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - This capital transfer technically, scientifically.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (İzmir) - I said it, I said it.
AHMET VEHBİ BAKIRLIOĞLU (Manisa) - The Central Bank lost 833 billion liras, where did this money go?

RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (İzmir) - Mr. Yegin, let me finish my speech.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - Explain the wealth transfer to us, I will be really happy, I want to understand.

Budgets should be put forward to eliminate this. Budgets should change these distributions. Budgets are opportunities to intervene in the economy and social life; How does he do this? As I said, it ensures the growth and development of the country and increases the national income. Solving the problems in the employment market and making the remanufacturing industry effective in Turkey because that manufacturing industry is the locomotive sector. In other words, budgets show who a political power is on the side of, living in that society. For example, "Is it on the side of capital, on the side of workers, laborers, the poor? Is it on the side of conflict, war, armament in the international arena, or is it on the side of peace?" In fact, budgets are documents that determine many issues such as. In this respect, the right of citizens to make a budget has been a very sacred right for centuries. Unfortunately, the system that exists in Turkey today limits citizens' right to budget. We talk and debate about this every time, because the Presidential government system is a system that actually bypasses the Parliament and does not allow the Parliament to prepare the budget, but sees it appropriate to make the budget in the palace; We will continue to criticize this, this system needs to change as it is.

It is truly a country of injustice and when we look at those budgets, when we look at the budgets that have come to date, we see that this feature comes to the fore in all of them. Look, I want to touch on some data. What do I mean when I say that you have turned into a country of injustice? In other words, the share of labor in the gross domestic product, which was 33.5 percent in 2018, decreased to 26.3 in 2022, that is, a decrease of approximately 7 points, 7-odd points in five years. On the other hand, when we look at the share of net operating surplus mixed income, that is, the share of capital in national income, we see that there is an increase, that is, we see that these increases continue in 2020, 2021 and 2022. Now, this means that this gap has grown, the difference has grown, that is, the share of capital has increased and the share of labor has decreased. What does this mean? Your governments and the budgets you prepared have increased the inequality between labor and capital; This is clearly seen from the numbers, and you are aware of it. This is actually a choice, a political and economic choice.

You know, it doesn't naturally develop like this, this inequality emerges as a result of these choices. This is the first point I want to point out, when we look at the budgets you prepared for the last six years, this is the seventh year.

Yes, today we are faced with a very serious situation, both in terms of trade and energy lines and in terms of international developments. Therefore, it is essential and a great need to take the steps that should have been taken much earlier.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Here you go.
HAKKI SARUHAN OLUÇ (Antalya) - I am wrapping it up, President.
In terms of Turkey being both a regional power in economic, social, political and cultural terms and a country of peace and creating a model of peace; We truly think that it is of great importance for it to be a model country in terms of building democracy among countries with different identities, cultures and beliefs in the Middle East and to spend its efforts, opportunities and resources in this regard. We hope that in the coming days, steps will be taken internationally within the framework of this awareness and Turkey can truly become a country that not only ensures social peace within itself but also serves as an important peacekeeper and peace model in its region. Otherwise, we are in a period and region where the interests of global powers are colliding with each other. This has been the case for a long time, but now it has increased once again, we are going through a period where discussions about the third world war are being made, there are such great interests in trade and energy that I really think that if the right steps are not taken here, if the foreign policy is not constructed correctly, then the consequences of this will not be encouraging for all of us.

Last sentence, if I go back to the budget, the budget needs to be built and shaped with this understanding of foreign policy and the region, but as far as we can see, such a thing does not seem to be reflected in the 2025 budget, especially when we look at some expenditure items.
My last sentence is that we hope we can all see the days when we will make budget choices that will meet the needs of workers, laborers, retirees, and all people trying to survive with great difficulties in Turkey, because in a country where the hunger line is 20 thousand liras and the poverty line is around 70 thousand liras, if the needs of workers, laborers, farmers, villagers, tradesmen, small producers are not met, the social, political and social problems that this will create will be extremely serious. Let me state this once again.
Thank you.

But it does this with a current account deficit of only 0.6 of its national income, that is, by using foreign resources, that is, by using savings. Now, what to grow? You need financing. What is financing? It is savings. If your own savings are not enough, you use foreign savings, that is, if you use foreign savings well, you will do good things. I cannot say that the current account deficit is absolutely bad. Current account deficit occurs when that resource is not used well. If you use that resource to make cars, it's bad; You use it in housing construction, it is bad; If it leads to corruption, shamelessness and theft, it is bad, but if you use it to build factories or develop technology, it is good, it pays off many times over.
Now, look, during your period, the growth rate increased from 4 to 5.4, but when we look at it with developing countries, our performance is bad, but when we look at it on its own, yes, but while your predecessors were doing this with a current account deficit of 0.6, you did this with a current account deficit of 3.8, and this is the basis of today's problems. We have a chart called "Periods in which the current account deficit decreases and increases". Just growth. The story of the current account deficit in Turkey is this, friends: In periods when growth decreases, Turkey contracts or growth slows down, our current account deficit decreases. Other things are needed to transform this structurally. You need to transform your industry, you need to transform your agriculture. We've had these discussions here before. When it had a current account surplus for three months in 2021, the ministers said in unison that Türkiye would have a current account surplus. Right after that, Türkiye had the highest current account deficit in its history, and I said this. Well, I said three months, let alone fourteen months, there were periods when Turkey had a current account surplus, but since you did not make a structural transformation, when you start growing again and your economy enters a growth trend, your current account deficit increases. Now, here it is. This is my current story, but my friends must be very confident that - of course, we did not have the opportunity to review it on an annual basis - they no longer included the current account deficit issue in the macroeconomic objective of the 2005 program on page 81, that is, Türkiye corrected the current account deficit issue. Our friends did not include the issue of correcting the current account deficit in the macroeconomic purpose of the program. I am very sorry about this. Politicians say, "We are running a current account surplus, just like that." But if the bureaucrat also believes this, then woe to us. This would be a very sad thing.

There is nothing structural about the current improvement in Turkey's current account deficit. Oh, does it make us comfortable in terms of finance? That is a separate “case”, but you need to do something else to permanently reduce the current account deficit.

Of course, there have been improvements in our credit score and the country's risk premium, and hopefully, with these improvements, the public will gradually reduce this burden from this trouble of interest burden, which is our trouble - I want to go into more detail a little later in the budget. without getting here. Because the world experienced some things, and so did we; There has already been a taxation regarding the earthquake. We need to state this clearly: At the end of these twenty-three years, the last seven years were under the rule of our friends from the AK Party and we said, "I wish we had not lived." He said - I'm talking in terms of budget - "I wish these years had never happened and Türkiye had not been so financially ruined." The years we mentioned, that is, the budget of the last six or seven years, tell us this. Based on this, we talk a lot about the natural budget right of the Parliament, that is, we call it "budget right". I have this perspective: Whatever happened in this country in the last seven or eight years was due to a change in the system, the system destroyed Turkey. I hope that the revision of this system in the coming period is the most important work of the Parliament, we will probably have the opportunity to talk about this with our Parliament Speaker tomorrow, but in the coming period, we must eliminate any doubts about the removal of the budget right from the nation's Parliament. It is not right to look at the fundamental issues of our country from a daily or party affiliation perspective. We should all be able to act together on the principle that is right for the country and the nation.
Now, with your permission, I would like to start with a sentence about the budget. Reliability and transparency Dear Vice President, I mean this: We personally have very close relations with Mr. Cevdet Yılmaz or many of our friends here, we do not have a personal trust problem with anyone, but we are talking about a budget, in fact we are talking about a financial basis. I mean this country. In your presentation, you can include retirees, young, old, working people; You said many things and said that the target of the budget is their welfare and happiness. Now, in order to achieve such happiness, we must first discuss the reliability and transparency of the budget that we built on numbers; I think it's something we need to talk about a lot. I will say two things about this.
Reliability. First of all, what did we build the figures on? For example, regarding inflation, we would really like this, that is, after the elections in 2023, we would like the discussions about TURKSTAT to end. I hope we will talk about this with our Minister of Treasury and Finance. Well, open this inflation basket, let's put an end to these discussions, let's finish the double-headed inflation explanations. What's in this basket? How are these calculated?

How do those figures and prices found by TURKSTAT come about? Let people see this. Of course, we do this based on the figures announced by the state, but in a country where inflation and inflation figures are a matter of debate in terms of reliability, when we make a budget and base the interest on inflation figures, it is not possible for any of the figures in this budget to be realized.
The second issue of trust, Mr. Vice President - I expressed this to our Central Bank Governor - we need to put this issue of transparency on the back burner. Now, 2018-2021; As the opposition, we all talked about the 128 billion dollars, "What happened to 128 billion dollars, to whom did we sell it?" like. Well, now calculations are being made, it is said that this figure exceeds 400 billion dollars. Now, this is 400 billion dollars. If such a figure exists, it needs to be seen. Yes, due to exchange rate pass-through, let's take it from the beginning of the year, let's see how much the foreign exchange increased, let's look at inflation; There is something strange about this. Now, our Central Bank cannot provide transparency on this issue. Therefore, in an environment where we do not know who buys what and sells what in the exchange rate management of the Central Bank, in an environment where inflation figures are followed by the society and the people who follow this business, there is a very serious problem with the numerical reliability and transparency of this budget, because we will talk about its performance in the end, I will go into the figures in more detail a little later.
Of course, another important issue here, that is, the second dimension of reliability, in my opinion, is this: It was necessary to confront the nation, Mr. Vice President. Believe me, I am not saying this just as a party member, because if you remember our President, "What did you want that we did not give?" When he says, that's "FETO terrorist organization." What they said was that we lived through July 15 after that statement, but it came out, it was made good with the nation and the nation accepted it. Currently, this country, elected with 52 percent, has a President and a head of executive power. So what do I mean by saying goodbye?
Not only starting from 2018, but in 2021, we are talking about how we dragged this country into a financial collapse with those wrong steps we took during the periods when inflation and interest rates were at 18-20 percent. In fact, we are talking about the price of five or six years in the whole picture we have right now. Believe me, someone should have come forward and apologized. I would like to thank Mr. Mehmet Şimşek - I have told him many times here - that is, "It is necessary to return to rational policies." By saying this, a very serious criticism was actually made about the past. Therefore, if an apology and a confrontation had been made with the society on this issue, believe me, this budget would have been questioned, accepted by the nation and its credibility would have been much higher.
Now, of course, thank God we did not go to war, we did not experience any problems, but we have a worse budget picture than if we went to war. Now, I will tell you a little later, you already know some things numerically. Now, the current economic management has been really successful by using financial tools in tight monetary policy. So we have seen an improvement in the data on many issues. What happened? In other words, what happened in production, investment and employment?

There is a point of view on this issue: I think that a mistake has been made in the calculations made based on the gross national product in Turkey. This is something that is now also in the literature: We are growing, but how are we growing and who are we growing? How do we grow and who do we grow? What kind of growth is this? Is growth a fair distribution, a growth that ensures equitable distribution, income justice? To be honest, now is the time for us to ask these questions. Yes, monetary tightening has been made, what is being done in terms of fiscal policy, you have expressed them, we will probably listen to you and then our Minister of Treasury and Finance, but now our question is: How many factories did we open, how much employment did we provide? These are things we will talk about further.
Now, there is a World Economic Outlook Report recently announced by the OECD regarding this growth. Now, our Turkey grew in the top 3-5 places in the world for years, sometimes we even became champions in growth, and there were periods when we were truly a leader ahead of China. Now, in these announced figures, the world average is 3.2; The OECD brought us down to 3.2. In other words, the OECD is doing something like this: It is pulling down our growth, it is pushing the world average up because interest rates are falling in the world, there will be an expansion, there will be an increase in the world trade volume. Therefore, he talks about an increase and a growth in world trade, but when it comes to Turkey regarding the same growth, he says: "I am reducing Turkey's growth to 3.2 percent."
Of course, when we look at inflation in G20 economies, there is a very different picture. Again, it is shown as 3.3 percent in the OECD report and Turkey's inflation has been brought down to over 29 percent. We will talk more about this issue, the issue of inflation, in the budget of the Ministry of Treasury and Finance. As you know, we started with 33, then we put a band called 36-42, maybe there will be a realization between 42-44 this year, we will take your guess on that issue, but right now, medium-term programs are of course constantly revised, the latest is 17.5 percent. Now, there is an inflation forecast of 29.1 percent. Regarding the OECD's Türkiye, will we hear this from you for 2025? “17 and a half percent plus 5 percent, plus 10 percent.” So will we have such a margin inflation figure? Of course, in an environment where our President is so serious about geopolitical risks and threats to the country in the Grand National Assembly of Turkey, here is what we will do about what the price of a barrel of oil will be, whether it will rise above 100 dollars or not, and how this will affect Turkey; Of course, this is a separate issue. Therefore, measures are needed in this regard as well.
Now, “We have been growing continuously for 14 periods.” We said so much so that sometimes we became - as I just stated - one of the fastest growing economies in the world. Yes, we went down from 19th to 17th, you stated this in your presentation.
Now, speaking specifically about income distribution, I have the following objection to you in your presentation regarding purchasing power statistics: In other words, regarding electricity and natural gas prices, "We are the country that provides them cheapest in Europe." you said.

There was a similar statement in your presentation last year, it was a statement you took from EUROSTAT data and shared. Now, when we look at the purchasing power statistics of Turkey, we have a purchasing power problem that is 3rd and 4th from the bottom among 27 countries in Europe, that is, it cannot be compared with Europe, that is, it cannot be compared with Germany or France.
Therefore, I believe that we need to rethink the issue of who this growth causes and what kind of obese growth it causes. As I have just stated, in countries like ours in the world, where the rent and interest economy is ahead of the production and investment economy and is reflected in the budgets, there is now talk of an impoverishing growth concept. In the last seven years in Turkey, everyone should look at the budget and ask, "How is Türkiye really growing and who is it growing?" If he asks the question, Türkiye, unfortunately, has become an economy where a significant part of the society, the large masses of people, are impoverished and a very small minority thrives. In such a country, we cannot look at any data in terms of gross national product and say, "We are doing well." It wouldn't be the right approach to say that.
Look, we have a contraction issue here in the industrial and manufacturing sectors. Now, in June, July and August, our industrial sector shrank by almost 5 percent, and has there ever been a period in the history of the Republic of Turkey - we have financial advisor friends among us - when bankruptcies, concordats and enforcements were so intense?
Dear Vice President, how will we achieve this 4 percent growth, how will this 4 percent growth happen, while the industry is contracting so much - the OECD predicts 3.2 percent in 2025, we say 4 percent? If there is really going to be growth on the real economy side, we don't need to talk about these, hopefully these will happen.
There is something I noticed in your general presentations: You speak highly of the capacity to create employment. Now, “It is a success that the number of people employed is actually increasing every year, but is this enough?” TURKSTAT's latest answer to the question "We cannot answer this without looking at the idle workforce." He said and 27.2 percent of the working population in Turkey is currently not working, and our labor force participation rate is 54.3 percent. Now, Mr. Vice President, the labor force participation rate in Europe is around 75 percent, the EU average. Come on, we passed these; We look at Saudi Arabia, Mexico, these countries; We look at Russia and Brazil; The labor force participation rate in these is over 60 percent, so I want to say this: Yes, we ensure participation in employment, but when we take into account the increasing population in Turkey, we have a very high unemployment rate in terms of our employment rate and the issue of idle labor force, because, as you know, people are no longer considered unemployed in Turkey if they do not look for a job for a few weeks.
So why did I bring this here?

Again, when we look at the budget data, let's be clear, of course states are not going bankrupt, but if it continues like this, that is, if a Turkey that cannot increase the employment participation rate, grow the real economy, grow production and investment thinks that it grows in this way with financial instruments, interest and rent, and cannot increase the number of working people, the Social Security Institution will collapse in Turkey, God forbid, this will make Turkey pay a very heavy price. The numbers we currently allocate to the Social Security Institution in the budget are reaching appalling proportions.

Now, in such a situation, it is necessary to reconsider how the current economic model is perceived as a model that encourages consumption in foreign trade and the suppression of the exchange rate.
Again, in our presentations - I am going through your presentation, we talked about this a lot in the Twelfth Development Plan - you always said this: You talked about high value-added products, Turkey's R&D, increasing its innovative, innovation-oriented production capacity, and you said that the 2025 budget is a budget that will increase and support this; I hope this goal will be realized, but regarding these high-tech products, there is a contraction of more than 4 percent in their exports, Mr. Vice President. There is something like this about exports: Look, between 2002 and 2012, we have an export figure that we increased from 36 billion dollars to 150 billion dollars, we have increased it 4 times in ten years; It's an incredible number.
Now, "High-tech products will support exports." If exports of high-tech products fall by 4 percent and we keep the 1-2 percent growth in exports. Look, I'm saying this by comparing it to the last decade. Okay, let's not grow 4 times, but let's grow 25-30 percent. Hence, we consider this a success.

Of course, in this economic model - let me underline it again - when we look at TÜİK data, whatever we have implemented in the last two years increases income inequality in the country. Because Türkiye is a country that consumes more than it produces, Türkiye is a country in debt. Therefore, let me remind you about 2017: In 2017, the debt stock of this country was 870 billion TL, and the total debt stock of the Republic of Türkiye in 2017 was 870 billion. By the first half of 2024, the total debt is 9.1 trillion, 9.1 trillion. I also have banker friends on the other side, I am also a former banker, so someone needs to explain this to us: How can the country's total debt increase 10.5 times in six and a half years? What did we do? Well, at the beginning of the budget evaluation, it is necessary to say goodbye to the nation and confront them by saying, "Mistakes were made on some issues, we saw them and now we will do them like this." I have to say.
Now, therefore, what does this debt bring us? It imposes an interest burden. Now, what is this interest burden like? In other words, we were saying that we need to reduce this interest to 50-55 billion TL, last year we had an interest budget of 1 trillion 254 billion, that is, there was a figure to be paid, we paid 97 billion more interest - we see in the current reports. Now, we are a state that discussed the savings measure package of 100 billion TL in the Plan and Budget and paid 1.2 trillion, 1.3 trillion in interest, that is, giving that savings to that interest, so we need to be aware of this, but let me come back to this year. 1 trillion 254, 1 trillion 300, this year we will pay 1 trillion 950 billion TL in interest. Our friends have stated that there is a 45 percent increase in interest payments. Well, now it is 14-15 times the amount of agricultural support.
Dear Vice President, we have known each other for a long time, we know what you think, we do not think differently than you about the development and development of this country, but we need a determination, that is, such a small share of the budget is allocated to agriculture and education, and we turn to "Fair distribution" and "We will support all segments of society with this budget." When you say this, we have the right to ask the following question: Whose budget is this budget, and who will benefit more? Therefore, unfortunately, the situation here is deplorable, a fact that hurts us as someone coming from the real sector. In my opinion, this is what summarizes the budget. The state will collect 1 trillion 637 billion taxes from companies and institutions in this country, but all the taxes it collects cannot reach the 1 trillion 950 billion TL it will pay in 2025, and cannot keep up with the interest.
Now, of course, we would like to hear these from you in more detail from now on. Clearly, I still see that society will buy this: "Yes, we made these mistakes, we did it wrong, and we really ruined the country." We have just seen in the data that this country has reached economic figures worse than if it had entered the war; This country does not deserve this, I think the history of the AK PARTY does not deserve this either; Almost like an inheritance, what was done in fifteen years was completed in seven or eight years. I rebel against this as a citizen of the Republic of Türkiye.
So, this interest, this debt, what is the reason for this? We are running a budget deficit. The budget deficit is really interesting.

Well, like this, the budget deficit in this country in 2022 was 142.7 billion. In some television programs, I say, "Well, this shouldn't exceed 40-50." I was criticizing.

year 2017; I always give examples from 2017, I always do this here because this country urgently needs system change right now. A year later, we may not see these current figures. MUSTAFA KALAYCI (Konya) - Mr. President, esteemed Commission members, Mr. Vice President, esteemed representatives of our public institutions and organizations, non-governmental organizations and the press; First of all, I would like to greet you all with respect and thank those who contributed to the preparation of the budget.
Our Republic is 101 years old. The Republic of Türkiye is a legacy that will last forever, a sacred trust that will be defended under all circumstances, of a just cause and a nationalist consciousness that does not bow to oppression. The Turkish nation and the Republic of Türkiye will exist as long as the world continues.
I once again celebrate the 101st anniversary of our Republic, and commemorate the founding heroes, especially the founder of our state, Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, our beloved martyrs and our ancestors, with mercy, gratitude and gratitude.
Mr. President, dear MPs; The world economy has recently been going through a difficult process due to pandemics, war, geopolitical risks and global crises. The post-pandemic recovery process of the global economy was disrupted due to the Russia-Ukraine war that started in 2022, trade disputes, intensified political struggles and inflationary pressures, and the massacre that turned into a genocide initiated by Israel in October 2023 further increased the tension and geopolitical risks in our region. While the world economy grew by 3.3 percent in 2023, the world trade volume increased by 8 percent. The world economy will grow by 3.2 percent in 2024; It is expected to maintain moderate growth at 3.3 percent in 2025.
In addition to the slowdown in the global economy, despite the costs imposed on the economy by the earthquakes experienced in our country in February 2023 and the elections that caused uncertainty, the Turkish economy became the fastest growing country among the OECD countries with a growth of 5.1 percent in 2023.

Growth has been positive on an annual basis for fourteen years. Our national income exceeded 1 trillion dollars for the first time in 2023, reaching 1 trillion 130 billion dollars, and our per capita national income increased to 13,243 dollars. Employment increased by 880 thousand people in 2023, reaching 31 million 632 thousand, and the unemployment rate decreased by 1 point to 9.4 percent. Our exports grew by 6 per thousand in 2023 and reached 255.6 billion dollars. Turkey's share in global goods exports increased to 1.07 percent in 2023 and reached its historical highest level. Annual inflation was 64.8 percent in 2023.
In May 2023, Turkey ended the expansionary monetary policy that it had been implementing since the third quarter of 2021 and joined the global trend and switched to a tight monetary policy to prevent rising inflation and ensure price stability. The interest rate, which was 8.5 percent in the middle of 2023, was first increased to 15 percent and then to 50 percent with gradual increases. Turkish economy will grow by 5.7 percent in the first quarter of 2024; In the second quarter, it grew by 2.5 percent annually, thus growth in the first half of the year was 3.8 percent. The contribution of private consumption expenditures to growth continued to decrease in the second quarter of the year, reaching 1.2 points, the lowest level in the last four years. In the same period, the net export item continued its positive contribution to growth with 1.3 points into the second quarter.
Machinery and equipment investments, which have been growing continuously for eighteen quarters, that is, four and a half years, contracted by 5.6 percent in the second quarter of 2024. Industrial production was 5.3 on an annual basis in August; It decreased by 1.6 percent on a monthly basis. The manufacturing industry capacity utilization rate was 74.9 percent in October. Manufacturing Industry Purchasing Managers' Index decreased to 44.3 in September, and a monthly slowdown was recorded in production for the 6th consecutive time. Although the contraction in industrial production shows the effects of tight monetary policy on production, these effects are estimated to be temporary. As a matter of fact, the seasonally adjusted Real Sector Confidence Index increased by 3 points compared to the previous month in October and reached 102.2, thus exceeding 100 after four months and shifting to an optimistic outlook. It is observed that the trend in favor of those reporting a decrease in production volume also turns in favor of those reporting an increase. Additionally, the Consumer Confidence Index increased by 3 percent in October and reached 80.6. Consumers' financial situation and general economic situation expectations have improved significantly.
The economic program is being implemented decisively and macro-financial indicators are improving significantly. The disinflation process continues, growth has started to stabilize, the current account deficit has narrowed, our country's risk premium has decreased, its credit rating has increased, its outlook has turned positive, the gray list has been removed, external resource inflows have increased, and the Central Bank reserves have improved.
Annual inflation decreased to 49.38 percent as of September. However, inflation and the high cost of living rightfully disturb all of our people. Our main duty is to eliminate complaints on this issue.

As of the second quarter of 2024, employment increased by 1 million 189 thousand people compared to the same period of the previous year and by 205 thousand people compared to the first quarter, and the unemployment rate decreased to 8.4 percent, the lowest level of the last twelve years. Erhan Bey is gone. Although Erhan Bey said, “Nobody should talk about the increase in employment.” he said, but I'm talking specifically about it. As of August, 1 million 100 thousand additional jobs were provided annually, and the unemployment rate decreased by 0.7 points compared to the same month of the previous year, falling to 8.5 percent.
Yes, the issue of unemployment is constantly brought up. It is obvious that there is an increase in employment, the data shows this. Now, when we look at the statistics, employment dropped to 26 million during the pandemic period, so to speak, but today it is 32 million. In other words, there is an increase of 6 million jobs in four years. I don't know if I want to ignore this, I leave the interpretation to you. Not only that, the labor force participation rate increased to a historical level, 54.3 percent, that is, labor force participation increased.
When we look at it, yes, there is an increase in the idle workforce rate. But when we look at its details, we see this: There is a decrease in those who are not normally included in the workforce. When we make a one-year comparison, there is a decrease of around 500 thousand people who are not included in the labor force. There is a significant increase in the number of those who have no hope of finding a job, whom we call "potential workforce", those who can do a job but are not looking for a job, and those who look for a job but cannot start working. In itself, there is also an increase in the idle workforce rate, as there is an increase in the number of people entering the workforce who were not included in the workforce before. Surely that's okay, right? Problem. But I will also say that this is not a new calculation, that is, the data of the Turkish Statistical Institute has been making these distinctions in this way for many years and in accordance with the standards in Europe. Now, those who can start work but are not looking for a job. This is debatable; Is he unemployed or not? Yes, he is a potential workforce, but he is not looking for a job, he is able to do it. For this reason, it is not right to only highlight the increase in the idle labor rate and, on the other hand, overshadow the success achieved in increasing employment. In other words, there is both a very significant increase in employment and a high increase in the labor force participation rate.
Another issue was brought to the agenda: "The share of these labor payments in the gross value added is constantly declining." It was said. In fact, an invoice was even issued to the Presidential government system on this issue. I have a series in front of me since 2009. The share of labor payments is increasing every year. Look, while it was 26.7 percent in 2009, it increased to 27 percent, 28 percent, 29 percent in the following years; It increased to 32 percent in 2016, 30 percent in 2017, 30 percent in 2018, 31 percent in 2019; Then there is a decrease, it decreased to 26 percent in 2021 and to 23 percent in 2022. We went through a pandemic period, that period definitely had a significant impact. In 2023, the labor force share increased to 32.8 percent. Yes, the EYT regulation was made in 2023, severance pay payments definitely have an impact, but when we look at this year's data - both the first quarter and the second quarter of this year - the share of labor payments increased to 42 percent in the first quarter and 40.8 percent in the second quarter, that is, it reached a historical level. So, is EYT the issue here this year?

No, so there is no severance pay payment. Here, we actually see an indicator of employment growth. In other words, the increase in employment also provides an increase in labor payments.
Yes, another issue is poverty. Yes, I agree, there is a deterioration in the inequality in income distribution, but when we look at poverty rates, we see that there is a decrease in TÜİK's relative poverty rate, material or social poverty rate, permanent poverty rate and the rate of those at risk of poverty or social exclusion. There is 2023 data.
Additionally, if you look at the World Bank's website, it has its latest assessment dated October 25, 2024 regarding the Turkish economy. The exact statement he used there is as follows: "The poverty rate, which was over 20 percent in 2007, decreased to 7.6 in 2021." says and 7 for 2022; It predicts a poverty rate of 6.6 for 2023. How does he calculate this? His calculation based on a daily income of $6.85 does this for the entire world. As you know, Turkey is a country listed in the upper middle income group by the World Bank, and we are close to being among the high income countries. Hopefully, we will transition to that this year or next year. When we look at other rates, we see that the rate in Turkey is gradually decreasing, including the World Bank's figures of 3.65 dollars per day or 2.15 dollars per day.
Yes, my dear friends, while exports increased by 3.2 percent in the nine months of this year, imports decreased by 7.9 percent. As of September, our annual exports increased by 3.4 percent to 261.6 billion dollars, and our annual imports decreased by 7.3 percent to 340.3 billion dollars. Foreign trade deficit increased by 31.5 percent to 27.6 billion dollars in the nine-month period of this year; The annual decrease was 31 percent, by 35.3 billion dollars. The current account deficit decreased by 75.2 percent by 29.3 billion dollars in the January-August period. As of August, the annualized current account deficit decreased by 40.6 billion dollars, at a rate of 78.3 percent, to 11.3 billion dollars.
Our tourism revenues increased by 9.3 percent in the six-month period of 2024, reaching 23.7 billion dollars. The Central Bank's gross reserves reached a new historical record level of 159.4 billion dollars. Turkey's EU-defined general government debt ratio decreased to 26.1 percent in the second quarter of 2024. As it is known, the Maastricht Criteria is 60 percent, and this rate is 86 percent on average in EU member countries. In our country, household debts increased from 12.6 percent to 10.2 percent compared to the previous year, debts of non-financial companies increased from 55.6 percent to 42.8 percent, public debts increased from 35.6 percent to 29.2 percent, debts belonging to the financial sector increased from 21.2 percent to 21.2 percent. It decreased to 16.7. In other words, Türkiye is among the least indebted countries in terms of public indebtedness, real sector indebtedness and household indebtedness.
The capital adequacy ratio of the banking sector is at 17 percent and it has a solid structure. The banking sector's non-performing loans ratio is 4.14 percent in 2020; 3.67 percent in 2021; 2.38 in 2022; While it was at 1.61 percent in 2023, it is at 1.69 percent as of August this year.

Mr. President, dear MPs; In 2023, the budget deficit was realized as 1 trillion 380 billion liras and the ratio of the budget deficit to the gross domestic product was 5.2 percent. 960 billion liras were spent in 2023 for the reconstruction and reconstruction of the earthquake area and the rapid elimination of the effects of the earthquake. Excluding earthquake expenses, the ratio of budget deficit to gross domestic product is 1.6 percent. The ratio of budget deficit to gross domestic product in 2024 is projected to be 4.9 percent. It is estimated that this rate will be 2.5 percent, excluding earthquake expenses. An allowance of 1 trillion 28 billion liras has been allocated in 2024 for the rapid elimination of earthquake damages and the needs of our citizens living in the region. While budget expenditures for 2024 were predicted to be 26.9 percent of the gross domestic product, this rate is expected to be 25.4 percent. This indicates that significant savings have been achieved in public expenditures thanks to the measures taken.
In the 2025 budget, expenses are 14 trillion 731 billion lira, revenues are 12 trillion 800 billion lira, and the ratio of the budget deficit to gross domestic product is expected to be 3.1 percent, excluding earthquake expenses, this rate will be 2.2 percent. An appropriation of 584 billion liras is foreseen in 2025 to improve the earthquake zone and increase resilience against disasters.
Mr. President, dear MPs; Türkiye has reached a level where its economic breakthroughs can be achieved in political stability until 2028. This period, in which separatist terrorism is ended and national unity and brotherhood are strengthened, should be a period in which efforts are made to achieve the goals of the Turkish and Turkish Century. Public resources should be used to create production structures that are globally competitive, have high R&D and innovation capabilities, have a strong capital structure, and can benefit from economies of scale to the maximum extent. An industry that carries out Digital and Green Transformation in a healthy way and has international competitiveness should be rapidly created. The ability and capacity to produce science and technology should be increased, and this should be transformed into development and social welfare. In this context, we consider and support the HIT-30 High Technology Investment Program, introduced by our President in August, as very important. With the HIT-30 High Technology Investment Program, 30 billion dollars of resources, including grants, taxes and market development supports, will be directed to high technology areas. Türkiye aims to become a global center in high technology production by 2030 with the HIT-30 Program.
With its national technology move, Türkiye has reached a point where it can develop and produce advanced technologies with its own means and capabilities. It should be aimed to fully mobilize the domestic and national production potential, further strengthen the giant breakthroughs in the defense industry, further increase the domestic contribution to energy, and obtain the advanced technology captured in the defense industry in other areas.

According to the 2024 Global Innovation Index announced in September, Türkiye achieved a significant success by rising to the 3rd place in the upper middle income group. In the general rankings, it ranked 37th, rising 2 places compared to 2023. Türkiye ranked 16th in the "creative outputs" category. It has risen to the first place worldwide in the field of trademarks and industrial designs. This situation shows that Turkey has successfully commercialized intellectual assets.
Undoubtedly, the production economy based on technological development, innovation, efficiency and employment should be expanded, and Turkey should be among the leading determinants of regional and global developments by integrating with the world economy in an open and competitive structure.

PRESIDENT OF THE COURT OF AUDITORS METİN YENER - Mr. President and esteemed members of the Planning and Budget Commission; As I begin my speech, I greet you all with respect.
As it is known, in accordance with the 161st article titled "Budget and final account" of the Constitution of the Republic of Turkey and the 42nd article titled "Final account law" of the Public Financial Management and Control Law no. 5018, the final account law proposal was prepared by the Ministry of Treasury and Finance in accordance with the form of the Central Government Budget Law, according to the accounting records, and was prepared by the Ministry of Treasury and Finance in accordance with the form of the Central Government Budget Law, on June 28, 2024, together with the justification containing evaluations showing comparatively the results of one-year implementation. It was presented to the National Assembly and a copy was sent to the Court of Accounts.

As a result of the evaluation of the audits and activity reports carried out by the audit group directorates in accordance with Article 41 of the Court of Accounts Law No. 6085 titled "General compliance notification", it has been observed that there is no incompatibility between the results of the implementation of the 2023 Central Government Budget Law included in the 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal and the results determined based on the accounts of public administrations.
As is known, extra-appropriation spending and appropriation addition are different from each other. Over-appropriation expenditures are expenditures that exceed the appropriation amounts provided in the budget law. Appropriation addition is the addition of appropriations to the budgets of public administrations within the budget year, based on the authority given by law.
To date, in general compliance notifications, the Court of Accounts has presented the above-appropriation expenses included in the final account law proposal to the Grand National Assembly of Turkey, and in appropriation additions, it has examined whether the authority given by law and the limits, if any, are complied with and stated whether they are in compliance with the legislation.
In the 2023 general conformity notification, there is essentially no different method and practice from the general conformity notifications of previous years. In the 2023 general compliance notification, it is stated that "The appropriation additions were examined in terms of their compliance with the laws and limits authorizing them, and it was seen that they were in compliance with the legislation. As stated in the previous years, the general compliance notification - on page 12 - found that as a result of the examination of the Court of Accounts audit reports, there was no determination that caused a change in the amounts of appropriations and budget expenses." It was presented to the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye by saying: Since there is no over-appropriation expense in the 2023 Final Account Proposal, a table regarding this is not included in the general compliance notification.
In the 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal, page 33, general budget administrations; The total of the added appropriation columns in the summary tables of appropriations and budget expenses of special budget administrations on page 147 and page 229 of regulatory and supervisory institutions is 1 trillion 318 billion 264 thousand 519 liras and 82 kuruş, and the usage information of this amount on a program basis can be obtained when Table 9 and Table 13 of the 2023 General Compliance Declaration are examined together. The difference between the total appropriation amount in the 2023 central government budget expenditures and appropriations - in table-9 - on a program basis in the general compliance declaration is 7 trillion 290 billion 788 million 49 thousand 519 lira 82 kuruş and the initial appropriation of 4 trillion 808 billion 267 million 645 thousand lira, is 2 trillion 482 billion 520 million 404 thousand. 519 lira is 82 kuruş. The basis for the added appropriation in the amount of 1 trillion 318 billion 264 thousand 519 liras and 82 kuruş TL is included in the budget laws and various laws.

Based on the State of Emergency Law, it became necessary to add an appropriation of 223 billion. Additionally, with the decision taken by our Supreme Assembly, personnel expenses were increased. As our esteemed MP stated, it was presented as an impact analysis of 794 billion lira in the Planning and Budget Commission due to the increase in the minimum payment limit of retirees, increasing it to 7,500 lira, holiday bonuses being increased to 2 thousand TL, and also the regulations regarding retirement, known publicly as EYT, and 692 billion lira was added here. Other issues are actually transactions carried out every year. This is the 6th article of the Budget Law and the additions we made with the regulations in the relevant laws. Law No. 5018 states that the Budget Law will be made on a gross basis. Therefore, we prepare the budgets of general budgets, special budgets and regulatory and supervisory institutions. Here, when we give an allowance from the general budget to the special budget administration, this number increases repeatedly. In other words, when I give an allowance to the Ministry of Transport and the Ministry of Transport makes this allowance available to the Highways Department, this is added as income and also as an appropriation to special budget administrations, so our gross budget grows.
Again, some of our institutions and special budget administrations have income. We take these as an estimated income basis when drafting the Budget Law.

Depending on the realization during the year, we add these revenues of special budget administrations as appropriations, to enable them to use them within that year.
Again, conditional donations and aid. This is not something we can predict, it depends on what happens during the year. Therefore, if a conditional donation or aid was made to any administration during the year, we add it as an appropriation on the condition that they use it exclusively in accordance with the purpose of that donation.
