Dear President, Dear Vice President, valuable members of our Planning and Budget Commission, representatives of our public institutions and organizations, valuable members of the press; I greet you all with respect, love and affection.
Before I begin my words, I congratulate our Republic Day, which celebrated its 101st anniversary yesterday, and wish God's mercy to our heroic martyrs and veterans who lost their lives, especially Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk.
I hope that the discussions on the 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal and the 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal will be beneficial for our country and nation.
As AK PARTY, we have worked to increase our service quality by producing services in every field from education to health, from transportation to energy, from industry to agriculture, from sports to social support. We continue to work to develop, grow and strengthen our country, increase the welfare of our citizens, make their lives easier, and also ensure their security.
The 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal has an approach that supports investment, employment, production and exports and increases social welfare. Our budget is based on sharing the opportunities provided by growth with all segments of society. The 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal has been prepared in line with our policies of maintaining fiscal discipline, ensuring economic stability and supporting sustainable growth.
There are many developments that threaten trust and stability in different parts of the world, especially in our nearby geography. The successive events that took place in this historical period indicate that humanity, societies and economies have reached a point of radical transformation. The current conjuncture dominated by increasing uncertainty and risks makes predictions about the future very difficult. This challenging process in the world and regional economy was carefully taken into account in the 2025 budget preparations, and our budget was created to minimize global risks for our country.
Dear MPs, growth slowed down in 2022 with the withdrawal of the extraordinary incentive policies implemented after the epidemic in the global economy, and the inflationary wave that occurred in this process brought about a financial tightening that spread throughout the world, especially in developed economies, and increased the expectations for a slowdown in economic activities. Since this date, the pressure of tight monetary policies on economic growth and risk appetite has become evident, while geopolitical tensions have maintained their weight on the agenda.

In the 2020-2023 period, the world economy grew by a cumulative 10.7 percent despite the difficult conditions, and compared to the world, the Turkish economy overcame these difficult conditions and displayed a more impressive growth performance, growing by a cumulative 25.9 percent. When evaluated in terms of average annual growth, while the world economy grew by 2.6 percent in this period, the Turkish economy managed to grow by 5.9 percent, more than twice the world average. These results clearly reveal Turkey's economic power and resilient structure. With the positive effects of our transparent and predictable policy steps that ensure strong coordination between monetary and fiscal policies, our top priority will be to reduce the inflation rate to single-digit levels during the program period and ensure that it stabilizes at these levels, in the continuation of the disinflation process that started as of June 2024.

We see that the 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal has been prepared in accordance with the Public Financial Management and Control Law No. 5018 and in line with the following objectives: Strengthening macroeconomic and financial stability, maintaining fiscal discipline, ensuring price stability by reducing inflation to single digits in the medium term, improving R&D and innovation capacity, ensuring technological transformation with a focus on green and digital transformation, strengthening human capital, further activating the labor market, improving the business and investment environment, and reducing informality in the economy; To continue the determined implementation of monetary, fiscal and income policies in a mutually supportive manner in the fight against inflation; To compensate for the damage caused by the earthquakes that occurred in February 2023 and continue to take the necessary measures to reduce disaster risks; To increase voluntary compliance, predictability and transparency in taxation, to strengthen tax justice, to redistribute income fairly and to increase revenues with an approach that spreads the tax to the base; To carry out the fight against informality with the active participation of all parties by making better use of technological opportunities and to continue auditing activities aimed at reducing informality; To act in line with the policies, measures and priorities determined in the basic policy documents in public resource use; To ensure that public administrations use their resources effectively, economically and efficiently, without exceeding the allocations allocated to them; To carry out public services with an understanding of efficiency and savings, to comply with and monitor savings measures; Implementing education and employment policies that will direct human capital to the jobs of the future in the medium term; To ensure the appropriate use of service levels by strengthening demand control and integration between service levels in health products and services; To support agricultural activities on the periphery of cities, which are important consumption centers, to ensure that consumers can access agricultural products at affordable prices by reducing logistics costs; To ensure that reserves are brought into the economy with high added value by increasing domestic and international exploration and production activities for oil, natural gas and mineral resources; It aims to achieve the goals I have mentioned above, especially to strengthen the financial sustainability of the system by implementing regulations that prioritize equity and actuarial balance, which encourage people to remain more employed in the social security system.
Budget appropriations are determined by taking into account the macroeconomic indicators, policies and priorities, measure targets, previous years' spending trends of the administrations, the important activities, projects and needs they carry out in the medium-term program; It is seen that budget revenue estimates are created by taking into account the revenue policy targets and priorities determined in the medium-term program, macroeconomic indicators, previous year revenue collections, the effects of revenue policies on budget revenues, and developments in national and international markets.

While 2025 central government budget income and expense forecasts were being made, it was assumed that the gross domestic product would grow by 4 percent in real terms, the year-end CPI would be 17.5 percent, exports would be 279.6 billion dollars, imports would be 369 billion dollars, and it is also envisaged to allocate sufficient reserve funds in order to carry out the urgent and priority services that will arise during the budget implementation.
Mr. President, dear members of parliament; There are budgets of 227 public administrations within the scope of the 2025 central government budget. I would also like to give some figures regarding the 2025 budget proposal: In the 2025 central government budget, it is anticipated that budget expenses will be 14 trillion 731 billion liras and budget revenues will be 12 trillion 800 billion liras. The ratio of the budget deficit to gross domestic product is expected to be 3.1 percent. After the increase in budget deficits due to the earthquake, it is seen that the budget deficit is approaching its average level again during our governments' period.
It can be seen that, while our governments continued to maintain fiscal discipline with determination, on the other hand, the budget was transformed from an interest budget into a service budget. Interest expenses, which were 14.3 percent of the national income in 2002, were reduced to very low levels, as a matter of fact, it is seen that interest expenses are at 3.2 percent in the 2025 budget.
In order to eliminate the damages caused by earthquakes in 2023, 960 billion TL was spent in the budget, and it is anticipated that 1 trillion 28 billion TL will be spent on earthquakes in 2024. In the 2025 budget, a total of 584 billion TL is allocated for the reduction of disaster risks, the rapid elimination of damage caused by earthquakes, the needs of our citizens living in the earthquake zone and the construction of earthquake-resistant cities. All authorities and institutions of our state continue to work uninterruptedly to ensure that life in the earthquake zone returns to normal quickly.
The distribution of 2025 budget appropriations according to economic classification is envisaged as follows: A total of 3 trillion 911 billion lira for personnel expenses, 1 trillion 24 billion lira for goods and service purchase expenses, 5 trillion 813 billion lira for current transfers, 1 trillion 102 billion lira for capital expenses, 338 billion lira for capital transfers, 306 billion lira for lending expenses, reserve appropriations. 287 billion lira, interest expenses are 1 trillion 950 billion lira.
Dear MPs, we have considered education, one of the most important elements of development, as the most important area in our budgets since 2002, and we have increased the investment made by the Ministry of National Education over the years. With our budget proposal, we increased our education budget to 2 trillion 181 billion lira in 2025. Thus, we allocate the largest share of the central government budget to education, which received a share of only 9.4 percent in 2002, with a rate of 14.8 percent in 2025. In addition, it is seen that the investment budget of the Ministry of National Education has been increased by 41.2 percent compared to the 2024 appropriation, from 100 billion lira to 142 billion lira.

Again, one of the issues we attach most importance to during our governments' term is health services. In order to ensure that our citizens benefit from health services in the best way, it is seen that 1 trillion 106 billion lira resources have been allocated for health services in the central government budget in 2025. Considering the health expenditures from the Ministry of Health, higher education institutions and the Social Security Institution, the total amount allocated from public resources to the field of health reaches 2 trillion 435 billion liras. Again, while our number of manpower in health services was 379 thousand in total in 2002, 257 thousand of which were within the Ministry of Health, it increased to 1 million 448 thousand in October 2024, 872 thousand of which were within the Ministry of Health.
The Turkish economy maintains its strong structure not only with investment, employment, exports and growth, but also with the solidity of its financial structure. We are determined to spread the prosperity of our country to all layers of society with the principle of social state. As one of the countries with the best functioning and most comprehensive social support system in the world, we are increasing the social aid budget to 650.9 billion lira in 2025; This amount corresponds to 4.4 percent of our budget.
I would like to briefly share other social resources we provide from the budget to increase the welfare of our citizens. A resource of 472 billion lira is foreseen in the 2025 budget so that our citizens can use electricity and natural gas more cheaply. In 2024, approximately 60 percent subsidy is applied for electricity and natural gas to residential subscribers. As of January 2022, we have kept the minimum wage exempt from tax, and all our employees benefit from this opportunity. In this context, a tax exemption of 810.4 billion lira is foreseen in 2025.
Dear MPs, the resource allocated to agriculture from our budget in 2025 is being increased to 705.6 billion lira. In this context, 135 billion liras are allocated for agricultural support programs, 166.3 billion liras for agricultural sector investment allowances, and 204.6 billion liras for agricultural credit subsidies, intervention purchases, agricultural kits and export supports. Agricultural sector investment allowances are also being increased significantly, and in 2025, the agricultural sector investment allowance will be increased to 166.3 billion lira, an increase of 64 percent compared to the previous year. While the agricultural irrigation investment allowance was at the level of 23.1 billion lira in 2002-2022, this figure is increased to 51.4 billion lira in 2023, to 60.3 billion lira in 2024, and to 108.1 billion lira in 2025, an increase of 79.3 percent compared to the previous year.
As of 2003, our railway line length of 10,959 kilometers has been increased to a total of 13,919 kilometers, 2,251 kilometers of which are high-speed lines, by the end of September 2024, our signaled line length of 2,505 kilometers has been increased to 8,046 kilometers, an increase of 221 percent, and our electrified line length of 2,122 kilometers has been increased to 7,274 kilometers, an increase of 243 percent.

An allowance of 561 billion lira is allocated from our budget for real sector supports, in this context, 222.8 billion lira for Social Security Institution employer premium support payment, 160 billion lira for agricultural loans subsidy support, 60 billion lira for Halk Bank tradesman loan subsidy support, 29.6 billion lira for state contribution paid within the scope of vocational training, and 56.9 billion lira for other real sector supports, especially exports. is leaving.
In addition, while only 62 defense projects were carried out in 2002, the number of projects has increased 16 times to over a thousand. With the contribution of the resources we allocate to defense and security, the localization rate in the defense industry has exceeded 80 percent. As of the end of 2023, the total defense and aviation sector turnover, which includes all defense and aviation sales of our defense companies and shows the size of the sector, reached 15.1 billion dollars. Our defense and aerospace exports, which were only 248 million dollars in the past, grew more than 22 times and reached 5.54 billion dollars in 2023, and are expected to reach 6.6 billion dollars by the end of 2024.
2024 has been a year in which a more positive picture was seen in foreign demand conditions and global trade in terms of our exports, and 2025 will also be the same.
We would like to thank our Vice President Mr. Cevdet Yılmaz and our Strategy and Budget Presidency, our Ministry of Treasury and Finance and all public institutions and organizations that contributed to the preparation of the budget proposal, non-governmental organizations and professional organizations that contributed with their opinions and suggestions, as well as our Commission members for their contribution to our work with their evaluations, and wish that the 2025 budget and all the work we will carry out with this budget will be auspicious for our country and our beloved nation. I wish.

CEVDET AKAY (Karabük) - Dear President, Dear Commission members, Dear Deputy President, very valuable bureaucrats, representatives of valuable non-governmental organizations, valuable press members; I greet you all with love and respect.
I sincerely hope that our budget period will yield beneficial results for our country. We will express our findings and opinions here so that the budget can be healthier.
When we look at the size of the budget, I want to talk more about the budget because our other deputies, including our spokesperson, touched on general issues in detail.
The budget size of 2025 is 14.7 trillion. Again, when we look at public revenues, a revenue of 12.8 trillion is targeted. Naturally, the Ministry of Treasury and Finance, which has the largest expenditure size, is 6 trillion 598 billion, which corresponds to 44.78 percent of the budget. It is followed by the Ministry of National Education with 1 trillion 451 billion and the Ministry of Health with 1.20 billion; I'm talking other than the appropriations to be transferred. Of course, there are problems with these two ministries, the Ministry of National Education and the Ministry of Health. We will address this issue in more detail when their budgets come.
Now, when we look at it, we already foresee a deficit of 1 trillion 931 billion in the budget. Although there is a 10.1 percent decrease compared to the previous year, it is still a significant size in terms of budget deficit. We have not yet been able to achieve this control regarding this deficit and this budget discipline. Now, when we look at it, the ratio of this budget deficit to public expenditures is around 13 percent and to tax revenues is around 15.08 percent. Here too, although there is of course a proportional decrease compared to previous years, there is a significant increase in the rates. When we look at the details of the budget here, we see that the most important problem is interest expenses; There is an interest expense of 1 trillion 950 billion - this is interest expense - again, its ratio to the budget size is around 13 percent, and its ratio to revenues is around 15.23 percent.
Now, when we look at the world economy in general, the global problem here is about the size of global debt. According to the IMF's statements, public debt in the world - I mean all public debts - is around 100 trillion dollars, and when we include the private sector debt, this reaches 300 billion dollars, very serious and high figures because the world's total income is around 105 trillion dollars. Let's call it "gross national product", I'm talking about the total income generated. Therefore, the borrowing rate is very high and there is a debt payment problem.

In the past, developing countries would borrow money and developed countries would undertake it; Now the situation is reversed, and China has been added to this. This means that this debt needs to be dissolved and reduced within production by creating income. When we look at our country, this problem is also valid in our country because this budget; Debt, interest and - I will now also specifically emphasize the exchange rate difference expense - exchange rate difference expenses - they are very important for us, I will give detailed figures in a moment - have entered the spiral of these three. We cannot produce net debt revenue, that is, we need to channel the debts we borrow and use into projects that increase production, employment and improve exports, and pay the interest and principal with the income obtained and reduce it; We see that this debt burden has increased over the years, we see that we cannot manage this, and this is something that will strain our country's economy in the coming period. “Let's increase exports.” We say, but on the one hand, when we look at the share allocated to exports in the real sector in 2024 and 2025; While it was 69 billion, we see it at 57 billion in 2024 and 2025. Here too, there is a decrease of 17.4 percent. This shows that we are having trouble generating revenue and paying debt. Now, I just mentioned the exchange rate difference expense, I looked at the figures for 2023 and 2024, and this issue has been examined and examined in detail in the Court of Accounts reports and the general compliance declaration. Now, when we look at it in terms of short-term domestic debt and long-term domestic debt - I'm talking about short-term as exchange rate difference expense - there is a total exchange rate difference expense of 374 billion 45 million in 2023, that is, 32 billion 806 million of it has been paid and accrued, but the paid part is 32 billion 806 million in 2023, the total domestic debt size is 3 trillion 211 billion in 2023, this is 100%. It corresponds to 11.64; I would like to draw particular attention to these exchange rate difference expenses. Now, I wanted to express the fact that if these amounts have reached this size in an environment where the exchange rate is suppressed, this debt burden will be much higher with a slight increase in the exchange rate and, in fact, the borrowing rate will increase more as a country.
So, what is the situation with foreign debt usage? When we look at it here, we are talking about a total of 1 trillion 348 billion foreign exchange difference expenses, and a figure of 1 trillion 722 billion when we add up the domestic and foreign debt exchange rate difference expenses. The debt stock carried over from 2023 to 2024, Mr. Vice President, is 6 trillion 700 billion carried over debts. Currently, Mr. Temurci also stated in his speeches, "It has reached 9.1 trillions." He expressed and said. It's a really big number; Even in 6.7 trillion, the exchange rate difference expense is 25 percent, even 25.7 percent. We need to take precautions for this.
Now, I have some questions here, I would be happy if the necessary friends would take notes, study and answer them during this process. The total of treasury guaranteed debts carried over to 2024 is 656 billion 435 million; There are some that are guaranteed by the treasury, transferred to 2024, and used in 2024.

Now, I am curious about where these treasury guarantees are used, that is, if they are external sources, from which external financial institutions they are used, the interest rates and maturity structure, and for which projects they are used. Who are the contractor companies used in these projects, the completion rates of the works there and the recycling of these used loans, are there any in default, are there any overdue ones, are there any unpaid ones; I would be very pleased if information was given specifically about these issues, and I wanted to emphasize this here.
Again, when we examined, there was an exchange rate difference of 251 billion 519 million regarding the part of these guarantees transferred to 2024. 17 billion 887 million of this was paid in 2023, that is, we paid it, it came out of our treasury, it came out of the state's coffers. Now, when I examined the final account and budget for 2023, something caught my attention. There is an amount paid from the treasury risk account called "treasury guarantees amount", a figure of 357 million 831 thousand liras paid from the risk account. Now, what has this figure reached, what has it reached in its final form in 2024, and to whom and for what was this amount paid? Is there a loan or a debt guaranteed by the Treasury or any unpaid portion of anything? I wonder if the treasury is paying this instead of that person, that institution. I would be happy if you could answer this too.
Again, an ongoing question in the same context is the guarantee and on-lent loan agreements signed in 2023, 4 billion 109 million US dollars; Details of these as well... The questions I just asked are also valid for this loan usage. I would be grateful if you could give me specific information about this.
Now let's get to the revenues. We have just talked about income, and in the income part, we said that we will ensure justice in taxes in terms of direct and indirect taxes. When we look at the distinction between direct and indirect taxes, nothing has changed over the years; In this budget, the ratio continues between 65 percent and 35 percent, indirect taxes continue at 65 percent, and direct taxes continue at around 35 percent. Therefore, while less taxes should be collected from those who earn less, we collect higher taxes from those who earn less and the same taxes from those who earn more. Therefore, the tax burden continues on wage earners and low-income earners. If we were to add rejections and refunds to this - because refunded VATs are also indirect taxes - we see that this rate is up to 70 percent. Here, there are tax exemptions, exceptions and deductions in order to ensure justice in taxes, that is, I am talking about the taxes whose collection is given up, which we call tax expenditures. Between 2010 and 2020, only some of the companies that carried out these PPP and build-operate-transfer projects were given tax, duty and fee exemption certificates 128 times. I can list the details: Here, 30 times to Cengiz İnşaat, 36 times to Kolin İnşaat, 24 times to Makyola, 19 times to Limaka, 19 times to Kalyon Holding. There are also some additions between 2020-2024 that I can detect. Kalyon Holding has the company “Electrical Vehicle Enerji Yatırım AŞ”; For example, this one received another incentive.

Again, on February 16, 2023, just a week and ten days after the earthquake, Cengiz İnşaat received incentives for an investment of 3 billion, it has 85 percent state contribution, it will not pay SSK premiums for ten years, it has 200 million lira energy support, 75 million lira employee support. So, on the one hand, these institutions make donations for "earthquake aid", but on the other hand, they receive an investment incentive certificate a week or ten days later and benefit from very serious government opportunities.
These PPP and build-operate-transfer projects are a bleeding wound for our country. From 2005 to 2024, 79.5 billion dollars were paid, and 162 billion dollars are to be paid this year. Another figure of 673 billion will be paid this year, in 2025 and in 2026. We said let's increase their taxes, we increased them from 25 to 30 percent - regarding the tax package - in the report presented in the impact analysis, it will have a collection impact of 557 million since it is only valid for 2025, but on the other hand, the tax burden of these institutions varies between 3 percent and 6 percent. You will also appreciate that although a 10 percent minimum corporate tax has been introduced due to the incentives they receive, we will actually collect very little from here. Precautions for this collection must also be taken. These guarantee payments need to be stopped somewhere, the exchange rate must be fixed and the country must be relieved of this burden. I think this is one of the important problems in the budget.
Again, I think there is a refund problem in our country regarding the domestic value added tax, regarding VAT refund. In 2024, 50.52 percent of this rate will be subject to VAT refund - I am telling you what I deduced from your reports - that is like a figure of 844 billion. You know, you carried out an Iron Fist operation, and here you made important findings regarding the fake invoice, around 105 billion TL; It was reported that the public loss was around 25 billion. These unfair VAT refunds... When we look at the figures for 2025, domestic VAT amounts to 2 trillion 884 billion, 48.12 of which will be subject to refund. I express this for this reason: If we could prevent 25 percent of these unfair refunds, we would be able to obtain 1 quarter of it, an income of 347 billion. You were receiving notices about this issue, and they were also coming to our e-mails; Special examination and attention to this issue will provide significant resource input to our state, treasury and finance. Companies in the sector that see this being inspected and do this work will not dare to do it. I think it is also important in terms of deterrence.
Also, the figure regarding the treasury portfolio and participation income caught my attention; While there was an income of 33 billion 382 million in 2024, we reduced the income in 2025, there is a decrease of 17.6 percent, 27 billion. Here too, there are SOEs, state economic enterprises and banks. Is there a decrease in these incomes, have their losses increased and are there no dividend payments? I would be happy if you share its details. This is information that you presented in detail on page 78 of the 2025 balance sheet, but we can see it globally, we could not see the details; I would be happy if you could give me detailed information.
I would also like to touch upon the problem regarding the Treasury's debt assumption commitment.

Here, a debt assumption of $16.6 billion was committed for 18 PPP projects between 2012 and 2021, meaning these loans were used and the treasury became a guarantee and used for the relevant projects. I would be glad if the usage efficiency in the projects, the completion rates of the works - are there any unpaid amounts here - are stated to us as an internal item within the global amount of these treasury guarantees. Again, most recently, a treasury debt assumption commitment of $173.5 million was made to FERNAS in September 2023. As I have just told you, the exchange rate difference expenses of foreign loans, such as the treasury debt assumption commitments here, are 1 trillion 348 billion.
I would like to touch upon another issue: Regarding increasing the revenues of the Defense Industry Support Fund - Mr. Ağbaba also mentioned, let's say "deferred", as our President put it - we talked about an income. There was a bill here, targeting an income of 70-80 billion; Well, there were issues such as 750 TL regarding the credit card limit, and there were figures such as 3 billion, 1,500 TL, 3 thousand TL regarding the purchase and sale of vehicles from the notary. It has been withdrawn for now, but while we are making efforts to generate 70-80 billion in revenue for the defense industry, on the other hand, a bill regarding electronic commerce and license fees was presented to the General Assembly in the same week and became law. Despite all our efforts, both in the Commission and the General Assembly, we could not prevent it. Dear Vice President, I would like to emphasize it again, especially because we want you to be informed about this issue here. Look, in this bill, normally 1 times the export amounts were deducted from the transaction volumes of e-commerce companies and calculated and accrued as a license fee at a rate similar to the remaining tax brackets, but with the passed law, 4 times the exports and 4 times the investment are deducted from the transaction volume.
I will show you a table like this, I don't know if it is visible or not. Look, when you calculate the current transaction volume of a company with a transaction volume of 350 billion - let's say 50 billion - from 300 billion, based on the table you see the rates here - there is a gradual rate system, there are rates with a progressive tax bracket - a license fee of 31 billion will be paid, but with this bill, a license fee of 1.4 billion will be paid. This is just a company and we are talking about the realized turnover, we can talk about even higher figures.
Now, an expansion has been made here regarding its size in the sector, with the addition of 20-23 percent and 15 percent, and a home delivery application has been made; This is a very wrong practice. I wanted to give you some detailed information here, although I think you probably already know this. If something is to be done about this issue, if exports are to be supported, we are not against it. Just as a calculation is made in certain bases and increasing rates in tax brackets, it is possible to regulate the amounts here in a transparent manner and in accordance with real market conditions.

This issue needs to be specifically addressed, especially since the relatives of the two former ministers work in the export units of the companies mentioned here and the fact that they are managers of an American company that invested here also raises a question mark. While we are trying to save so much from public resources, the fact that this issue is being passed by the Parliament makes us all sad. I hope the mistake will be corrected.
I will also touch upon a couple of issues regarding austerity measures. It was stated that there would be no vehicle purchases for three years, but we see that 3,423 vehicles will be purchased in the 2025 budget. Last year, this number was 3,059, there are 364 more vehicles, an increase of 11.89 percent. On the one hand, while we are trying to save money by selling and renting these vehicles and reducing expenses, on the other hand - of course, there is this authority in the budget law in case of a force majeure or different situation - I wonder if the existing vehicles are not enough and these vehicles are being purchased? Because there are around 116-117 thousand vehicles in the public sector, both owned and leased, and we see that these are not sold at a sufficient level. We can say this again from here: Again, when I look at the figures, there is a figure of 69 million in securities sales revenue. Are the sales of vehicles tracked in a separate account? I'm wondering about this especially from here.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Mr. Akay, please get it together.
I'm adding two minutes to your time.
CEVDET AKAY (Karabük) - Thank you.
Of course, we need to take many measures in terms of taxing the unregistered economy, providing the necessary savings measures, reducing the tax burden of wage earners, collecting more taxes from those who earn a lot, taxing capital, reducing the tax burden on all these facilities, including mines, to a real level and ensuring that they are operated by national companies, supporting tradesmen, farmers, SMEs, and adjusting the wages of retirees and minimum wage earners as they deserve. These must be implemented in order to eliminate the disorder in income distribution.
The defense industry is important for all of us, but on the other hand, we see that the balance sheet structures of the distinguished companies of our defense industry are deteriorating. I would like to ask a specific question about this here. When we evaluate the Presidency of Defense Industries within the Presidency - we will go into detail in its budget - ROKETSAN's production
There is a decrease of around 40 percent, a very serious decrease. There are departures in the engineers, technical teams and technical staff working here. Where and how did they start their business? Is there a private sector company using this technology? I would appreciate it if you could give us information about this. We all want ROKETSAN to recover because we are an industrial enterprise that can manufacture MAM guided missiles, a very important industrial enterprise, but we see that its balance sheet structure is falling and ROKETSAN is weakening.
Again, we are hearing some rumors about the Mechanical and Chemical Industry Institution, that it will be privatized. Is there any possibility of privatization of the Machinery and Chemical Industry institution?
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CEVDET AKAY (Karabük) - I am finishing, Mr. President.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Here you go.

CEVDET AKAY (Karabük) - We also receive notices and hearings that the products produced by this company are sold abroad, especially to Ukraine, through Europe, through the Czech Republic, that is, the product sold for 2 liras is sold from the Czech Republic to Ukraine for 2.5 liras, and the profit is transferred to the established company there. Is this true? Who is the company here in the Czech Republic, who is responsible for it? We will request information on this issue.
I don't want to make my words too long.
Thank you, Mr. Vice President.
Thank you.
PRESIDENT MEHMET MUŞ - Thank you.
Now, Ms. Gülcan Kaçmaz Sayyiğit from the DEM PARTY Group.
If you're ready, I'm turning on your microphone.
Here you go please.
GÜLCAN KAÇMAZ SAYYİĞİT (Van) - I am ready, President, thank you.
Mr. President, is it twenty minutes or ten minutes?
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Twenty minutes.
GÜLCAN KAÇMAZ SAYYİĞİT (Van) - Twenty minutes.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Yes, but you can also use it for ten minutes.
GÜLCAN KAÇMAZ SAYYİĞİT (Van) - OK. Today there is generosity in terms of time.
CHAIRMAN MEHMET MUŞ - No, it's like this: Twenty minutes for the general public, twenty minutes for group spokespeople when we move on to the ministries, and we will reduce the others to ten minutes.
GÜLCAN KAÇMAZ SAYYİĞİT (Van) - OK, thank you.
Dear Vice President, valuable members of the Planning and Budget Commission, valuable workers and bureaucrats; I greet you all with respect and wish you success in your work.
We all know that the budget process is as tiring as it is vital, because we are talking about a budget that touches everything and affects everything in some way, especially workers, women, children and ecology. Of course, we have to add that the privileged classes and the wealthy conglomerates were never negatively affected. But those of good conscience, I would like to underline once again clearly that, as DEM PARTY, we will stand by labor and workers and continue to raise our voice against poverty and deprivation. If the budget is a right, it is our duty to be the voice of the owners of that right. Therefore, we made our opposition yesterday on behalf of the people of Türkiye against the numerical tricks and number rounding skills of the AKP government, and we will continue to do so today. Unfortunately, we did not and do not see the people in any of the budgets prepared during the AKP's rule. We are faced with a system that acts with a holding mentality even when the economy is relatively good. Those who see themselves as bosses have always seen citizens as subjects. Unfortunately, the only promise that stood out in large print in every budget presented to us was poverty and impoverishment; While the country's resources were given away to a narrow group, the broad masses of the people were condemned to suffer in the grip of unemployment and poverty.
Now, especially after soft power was replaced by pure violence in the Middle East, unfortunately the country has never been able to recover. Security policies in the Kurdish issue both took lives and impoverished all peoples.

Unfortunately, democracy, freedoms, justice, peace and human rights have never been the fundamental pillars on which the budget is based; On the contrary, inflation, unemployment and foreign exchange crises were always tried to be manipulated through these security policies and simple violent tendencies. This needs to change now because the people of Türkiye no longer have to be victims of wrong political choices and displays of power. In summary, I want to say this: The political government has clearly violated the citizens' right to budget this year as well. Unfortunately, we see once again how the AKP government turned a deaf ear to what we said last year, because the people own the budget, but unfortunately there is no institutional mind that consults them and includes them in the budget process through non-governmental organizations. If the budget belongs to the public, we want to ask why this budget is being prepared in the mysterious rooms of the palaces? If the purpose of the budget is to solve problems, why can't we find a solution to the problems of retirees and minimum wage earners? There is no solution because we are faced with an AKP mind that sees itself as the owner of the budget. Its sole purpose is not to spread the prosperity to be built, but to create a rosy picture based only on inflation rates adjusted with abstract growth figures.
Now, in this sense, we are going through a period where peace is talked about a lot, as you all know. We think that an economic peace should also be built in the process where this peace is talked about a lot. This is only possible by acting on the axis of bread and justice.
Dear guests, the history of the budget is also the history of democracy. When we look at the past, the right to budget has developed significantly as administrators internalized democracy. Because, to the extent that citizens can have a say and be held accountable, they also have the power to control where the taxes paid by the people are spent, whether they are used for their intended purpose, the balance and appropriateness of income and expenditure, and similar issues. Therefore, the control mechanism is an important brake in contemporary democracies. Unfortunately, we see and learn every day how vital control is, especially in weakened governments like the AKP. To put an end to this, we need a modern, democratic monitoring tool in the light of the principles of accountability and responsibility. We are currently talking about the reports of the Court of Accounts. We can say that it exists for this very reason, but unfortunately it does not seem possible for anyone to breathe easy considering the fact that institutions have been emptied and politicized over the years. Technically speaking, the Court of Accounts appears as an independent and free institution. The Court of Accounts is truly an important institution that derives its power from the people and is responsible for conducting audits for the public interest on behalf of the Parliament. Because from an ideal perspective, how public expenditures are realized, irregularities and corruption, and even how institutions can demonstrate a better budget performance depend on the Court of Accounts' reports. When we look at the world examples of the auditing phenomenon, we can clearly see that auditors are far from political influence and that the institution has the power against political intervention.

In fact, the name of the institution equivalent to the Court of Accounts in the United States is "government accountability office". Frankly, I like this naming, too, because control is not just algebra; There cannot be multiplication or subtraction. If we get to the heart of the matter, it means that state institutions must account to the citizens for where and for what they spent the taxes they collected from the citizens.
Mr. President, dear audience; When I examined the external audit reports of the Court of Accounts, I frankly felt that the state was avoiding being accountable to its citizens. I think that the problems and irregularities are not fully reflected, especially when we compare the details of past findings with today's summary of findings. The Court of Accounts reports created serious excitement whenever they were published, and the results of these reports made a big impact, but "the fish stinks from the beginning." Unfortunately, the process that started with the saying "has now reached the point where we say its tail has gone bad." It should not be thought that we have completely eliminated the Court of Accounts. Of course, it is one of the most valuable institutions, but in recent years, the Court of Accounts has given the impression of being an institution that limits itself. There are dozens of institutions that have been found to not provide information about the sources used in their annual reports. There are more than 200 public administrations that are said to not provide information about the reasons for deviations between budget targets and realizations. Again, it is stated that 258 public administrations do not include basic financial statements and explanations regarding these statements in their annual reports. There is a serious structural problem. While the constitutional authority of the Court of Accounts is obvious, which institutions can do this by getting power from where? We want to ask this while you are here. If it is a matter of budget compliance, we think that the Court of Accounts reports should be handled on their own.
We all know that at this point today, society has lost its faith and trust in state institutions. We call it the Court of Accounts, but we are aware that the audits carried out on municipalities are very limited. In particular, the average inspection rate of provincial and district municipalities is around 50 percent. Therefore, which municipalities are not inspected is an important point. Look, when we had municipalities, auditors were not leaving the municipalities, but during the period of trustees, there were either no audits or very few audits. So much so that there are municipalities whose audit report has been published only once in the eight years they have had trustees. In the published reports, there were illegal recruitments, money used for purposes other than their intended purpose, etc., but the political power did not even bother to move against this. Unfortunately, we did not receive a response to our research proposals based on the findings of the Court of Accounts, and our research proposals were rejected.
I would like to conclude my remarks by drawing attention to a few more points. Now, it is not possible to mention all the external audit reports of the ministries within this period, but let's look at the audit report prepared for the Presidency, which attracts a lot of attention with its expenditures and practices today. There is not a single finding. Frankly, it is surprising for us that there is no evidence of those who do not save their reputation and travel with aircraft convoys.

In such a situation, we should not be expected to believe that the Court of Accounts is independent and impartial.
Another issue is that the Turkish Wealth Fund is not audited by the Court of Accounts; We think it is against the public interest. In addition, thanks to some findings and findings of the Court of Accounts, we can clearly, unfortunately, see how citizens' taxes are wasted, how they are used for the benefit of the political power, how they are transferred to companies in treasury-guaranteed bridges and hospitals, and how they are used in the arms race under the name of "procurement of goods and services".
As a result, the classic "I did it and that's it" approach of the AKP government in the 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal unfortunately made itself felt in this period as well. The structural problems of institutions that have moved away from competence and merit are not taken seriously because AKP has built a system in which everything will be reflected from its own side. However, as a requirement of democracy and people's participation, it would have been possible to carry out a more transparent budget process and even to have the discussions delivered live to the whole society; Nothing like this happened. Despite this, our duty as the DEM PARTY is to ensure that the voice of the people resonates here, and to be the voice of the people from now on, as we have done this in the strongest way with our words; We will continue to be the voice of the laborer, the poor and the worker.
Thank you.
PRESIDENT MEHMET MUŞ - Thank you.
Now Ayyüce Türkeş Taş from İYİ Party Group.
Mr. Taş, if you are ready, please come.
AYYÜCE TÜRKEŞ TAŞ (Adana) - Mr. President, Mr. Vice President, esteemed bureaucrats, my esteemed fellow MPs, our valued guests; I salute you all with respect.
I also hope that our 2025 budget negotiations will be beneficial for our country and nation.
Before I start my speech, I would like to congratulate the 101st anniversary of our republic. On this occasion, I wish mercy to Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, his comrades in arms, and all our martyrs. I pray that God does not force us to struggle for liberation once again. How happy we are to be here as representatives of the great Turkish nation under the blessed roof of the Turkish Grand National Assembly. Let the number of those who serve by realizing the responsibility of the seats we sit in increase, so that the sovereignty belongs to this great nation unconditionally.
I would like to state here that I absolutely reject the expression "Kurdish geography" used by Mr. Oluç during the budget negotiations. The Republic of Türkiye is a whole with its 7 geographical regions. There has never been any positive or negative discrimination against any region or segment of society at any time in the history of the Republic of Turkey. Of course, there were wrong policies implemented and mistakes made, but this situation affected the entire geography, that is, all these 7 regions and the entire Turkish nation. It is absolutely not good-intentioned to define the wrong policies implemented or mistakes made in Turkey specifically for a segment or a region, and I reject this.

We, as a nation, experience every problem, problem or positive development in common, work together to eliminate it, or share our happiness together. Starting from Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, the Republic of Türkiye has made its development plan across the entire geography. In fact, I say that the Southeastern Anatolia Project, which dates back to the time of Ataturk and started in the 1970s, is the largest development project undertaken by the Republic of Turkey, and I would like to draw attention to some issues that I consider important in the 2025 budget.
The most striking point in the 2025 budget is the total population of Turkey, which is used in the calculation of gross domestic product per capita. Turkey's population for 2024 is calculated as 85.6 million, but it is clearly seen in this population that illegal immigrants in our country are not taken into account. According to Turkish Statistical Institute data, there are 3.1 million registered immigrants in our country, but when we consider illegal immigrants, this figure is around 10 million. This means that the real population of our country is around 95 million. It is very important that economic data reflect reality in achieving economic targets and creating confidence in the market. As the İYİ Party, we say: Let the general population census, which was held every ten years until 2000, be conducted again as a traditional closed census. As you all know, after 2000, Turkey has been collecting population data with the address-based population registration system, but this system only tracks the registered population and does not provide clear information about the unregistered population and temporary immigrants. We all know that the demographic structure of our country has changed significantly due to immigration and emigration, especially after 2011, but the extent of this change is not fully known since a comprehensive census has not been conducted. Closed general census does not only provide demographic data, but also provides us with important socioeconomic data such as income status, education level, unemployment rate. That's why regular censuses are vital. The most important problem that will arise from not having up-to-date and detailed population data is that it will lead to serious deficiencies in the planning processes, as we see in the upcoming budget proposal. In addition, in a country where so many illegals and criminals roam freely outside, a closed census with a curfew is not an option but a necessity. We say that we are willing to stay at home for a day every day in order to be safe, for our state to make correct plans, and even to have healthy economic data. On this occasion, I present our closed census proposal to your attention.
In addition, I would like to draw attention to the need to urgently re-conduct the agricultural census, which was last held in 2001, regarding agriculture, which is one of the important topics of the budget and is also on the agenda of my constituency, Çukurova, Adana and of course all farmers in Turkey. The pandemic and the wars in our region have recently shown us once again the importance of food safety.

The difficulties experienced by our farmers in recent years have once again demonstrated that the first step of development and income generation in agriculture is planned agriculture. An indispensable part of planned agriculture is agricultural census.
When considering the 2025 budget, I think it is important to look at the previous year's achievements and current economic indicators. The 2024 budget was a summary of the difficulties faced by low and fixed income citizens, tradesmen and industrialists. Fundamental problems such as insufficient income, the cost of living caused by inflation, and inadequacies in public services made themselves felt deeply in 2024. I regret to see that the 2025 budget is unfortunately far from solving these problems. If we need to evaluate the budget in 2024, the budget realization reports published monthly by the Ministry of Treasury and Finance show us that, at the end of ten months, there is no change in the Government's policy, which started last year, of turning a blind eye to the oppression of low-income, fixed-income, blue-collar and white-collar workers in the face of inflation and purchasing power. In our country, where the hunger line is approximately 19 thousand TL and the poverty line is 65 thousand TL, the minimum wage is still 17 thousand TL, so low income people are crushed. Due to the pressure on exchange rates, exporters who tried to produce and export despite inflation and cost increases were crushed because exchange rates were kept at the level demanded by the capital, not at the level they expected. Regardless of the amount of salary they receive, the incomes of those with fixed income, that is, blue and white collar workers, will not be enough to meet their needs in 2024. Therefore, this segment was also crushed because there is a significant difference between actual inflation and announced inflation, and wage increases are made according to the announced inflation, which puts fixed income earners in a difficult situation.
If we take a look at the general picture, the prices of rent, education, health and food, which are the main expenditure items on an annual basis, have all increased by more than 50 percent, but while these increases are taking place, the increase that citizens will receive will be calculated at approximately 18 percent, which is a great injustice. Unfortunately, food inflation is breaking records, especially in our country, which is supposed to be an agricultural paradise. Turkey has the highest food inflation among OECD countries, and we rank 1st in Europe and 5th in the world in the same category. Since food expenditures are among the top expenditures, it is important how much trouble our citizens are in.
If I don't misunderstand, my deputy friend from the AK Party had just stated that rents in Spain have increased a lot. I know that he and all my friends here know it very well, but I want to emphasize it again. When looking at rent increases, what is important is the percentage of our total income that we allocate to rent.
İSMAİL GÜNEŞ (Uşak) - Minimum wage is 1,800 euros, rent is 2,800 euros.
AYYÜCE TÜRKEŞ TAŞ (Adana) - Anyway, you do the division, I won't divide it now.
This should be 20-25 percent worldwide, its share in total income. Unfortunately, in Turkey, this figure is as high as 50 percent on average.

The Ministry of National Education and its budget, which was increased by 32 percent, is one of the factors that caught my attention. Of course, education is one of the indispensable parts of a nation and a state, and of course it should receive a strong share from the budget, but at this point, it is obvious that our national education system has collapsed and needs to be addressed urgently. If in 2024, schools in the capital of Turkey cannot employ staff, cannot ensure hygiene, and the Minister of National Education says that he cannot do these things because he does not have enough funds, I think there is no need to comment here.
Again, if bussed education services are being abolished under the name of "saving measures" and the Minister of National Education is transferring huge amounts of resources from his own budget to what he calls NGOs - but it is very clear where they are, everyone knows them - this is also an unacceptable reality. Here too, there is nothing left to talk about or defend, but it is necessary to take action in order to do business.
While we are on the subject of savings, I would like to draw attention to one more thing, as a mother of two children. Because of this permanent daylight saving time, our children have been going to school in the dark for years, security problems occur, their biological rhythms are disrupted, and psychological problems are increasing. “Why is this being done?” When we ask the Ministry and our Minister, the answer is that it is energy saving. When we look at the budget of the Ministry of Energy, it is 45 billion TL, while the budget of the Presidency of Religious Affairs is 130 billion TL. The budget of the Presidency of Religious Affairs is almost more than the budget of 5 ministries, and it seems that the total budget of 2 important ministries such as the Ministry of Internal Affairs and the Ministry of Foreign Affairs is only as much as the budget of the Presidency of Religious Affairs. It is called "energy saving", the Diyanet's budget is more than Energy. “We are in a difficult region geopolitically.” It is said, the Israeli threat is constantly mentioned, and even - last week - an attempt was made to collect taxes from credit card limits for this reason, but the budget of the Diyanet is more than the budget of the National Intelligence Organization. We say that tradesmen are closing their shutters, SMEs are bleeding, the budget of the Diyanet is more than the budget of the Ministry of Commerce. We say that we cannot protect our women, children, elderly and animals in the country, and the Presidential protection expenses are breaking records. I think there is something that really needs explanation here. I would like to say that such a distribution is not acceptable when citizens have such difficulties in accessing security, education and health services.
Again, the situation of the Ministry of Health, whose budget has increased by 39 percent, is unfortunately deplorable. Even the newborn baby gang, which has been on the agenda of recent days, is valid and sufficient to say that this Ministry is bankrupt, but unfortunately, since our managers have never taken responsibility, everyone is still on duty. Even the repairs of the hospitals damaged after the earthquake could not be completed.

I would like to give another example from my own constituency: Çukurova University Balcalı Hospital is still trying to provide service under very poor conditions; Patients recovering from surgery are kept waiting in the emergency room, our doctors' call and duty payments are delayed, and incentive payments have not been made for two months. It seems that the citizens of the Republic of Turkey were not happy with the 2024 budget, which the ruling party presented and defended as a reform movement last year, and unfortunately, it is expected that they will not be very happy with the 2025 budget either.
I am very sorry to say that the 2025 budget will tell tradesmen to "Shut down", to SMEs to "Shut down their company", to exporters to "Slow down the business". and to the citizens, “Be thankful.” It doesn't go further than saying. We are faced with a budget that keeps low-income people constant and leaves fixed-income people in the grip of inflation.
I would like to say a few sentences about taxes, which are the main item of the budget. Unfortunately, justice in taxes does not seem to be achieved again in this budget. Mr. Şimşek “We will collect more taxes from those who earn more and less from those who earn less.” he said, but it is clear that this is not the case; The largest items in tax revenues are still indirect taxes, SCT and VAT. These taxes, paid equally by the rich and the poor, constitute 52 percent of the total income. Behind the rhetoric of "fairness in taxes", we were expecting a budget in which the declaration-based taxation system would be strengthened and the share of indirect taxes would decrease, but these expectations did not come true. If no one, no citizen, feels that money is coming out of his pocket, he does not go after that money, and this provides the government and administrators with the flexibility of not having to be held accountable for the taxes by the citizens. I guess that's why the AK PARTY Government continues determinedly with this strategy. At the same time, it is clear that corporate tax collection has failed. The government had stated that it would increase tax collection to 85 percent in 2024, but this rate remained at 69 percent in the first nine months of 2024, meaning that citizens can no longer pay their taxes or they refrain from paying taxes because they have lost their belief that taxes are spent on things that are good for the nation and the country, especially after the earthquake disaster we experienced. When we say "fairness in taxation", we mean the taxation of those who cannot be taxed; taxation of urban rents, capital gains, crypto assets; taxation of the ill-gotten gains of capital; He wants to see the removal of the capital gains exception. While we are waiting for this, we see that the bill has been increased to low-income and fixed-income people. When inflation rises, the public pays the bill, and when a bitter prescription is presented to reduce inflation, the public pays the bill again. The balance in the labor-capital equation is terribly distorted against labor.
As the Deputy of Adana, one of the provinces that experienced the earthquake, I would like to touch upon the issue of earthquake expenditures.

Our Vice President, Mr. Cevdet Yılmaz, announced the amounts of earthquake expenditures and said that an appropriation of 584 billion liras is envisaged for the improvement of the earthquake zone and increasing resilience against disasters, and most importantly, "Starting from next year, the share of earthquake expenditures in the budget will decrease." he said. I was surprised by this statement, Mr. Yılmaz, because currently, for example, the rate of houses delivered in Hatay seems to be 4.5 percent. Our citizens in Adana are still experiencing great grievances, education and health services are being carried out slowly in these regions, and there are serious problems with security. In addition, I believe and think that this budget should not be reduced while the investments needed to make Turkey earthquake resistant are obvious. While creating a budget and discussing the budget, we can never ignore the realities of the earthquake region and act with aphakic and imaginary discourses. Instead, it is our responsibility to take a prudent approach based on hard data. I think that each of us should act more sensitively and attentively, especially in matters concerning the earthquake region, taking into account our debt to the earthquake region and its people.
As a result, the 2025 budget should be structured in a way that takes into account the real needs of our citizens and ensures social justice. It is necessary to adopt a production-based economic model, reduce poverty and unemployment rates, and ensure justice in income distribution. Increasing the budget resources allocated for basic services such as education, health and social security is critical for the peace and welfare of the society. It should not be forgotten that the future of a country is shaped by the individuals in that country living in an educated, healthy and safe environment.
Thank you very much, I offer my respects.
PRESIDENT MEHMET MUŞ - Thank you.
Now Mr. İsmail Faruk Aksu from the Nationalist Movement Party Group.
Dear Aksu, come in please.
İSMAİL FARUK AKSU (Istanbul) - Thank you.
Mr. President, Mr. Vice President, esteemed members of our Commission, esteemed bureaucrats and members of the press; I salute you with respect.
I hope that the 2025 central government budget will increase the peace and prosperity of our nation and increase the efficiency of public services. I would like to thank our President, especially those who contributed to its preparation, the Strategy and Budget Presidency, the Ministry of Treasury and Finance, and our Vice President, Mr. Cevdet Yılmaz, for his comprehensive presentations.
At the beginning of my speech, I congratulate the 101st anniversary of our republic and the Republic Day, which we celebrated yesterday, and commemorate with mercy and respect all the soldiers of the War of Independence, our dear martyrs, and our esteemed ancestors, especially the founder of our republic, Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk. The Republic of Türkiye, which was born with the blood of martyrs and the sacrifices that we cannot repay, will survive forever. Long live the Turkish nation, long live the Republic of Türkiye!

Mr. President, dear members of parliament; Before moving on to my budget evaluation, I would like to bring to your attention, not as an answer to Mr. Temurci, but for the sake of record, an issue that is brought up here from time to time: With the transition to the Presidential government system, some expressions such as the budget system was changed in the Constitutional amendment made in 2017 and the budget right of the Parliament was taken away are used from time to time, and this was partially mentioned today. However, in reality, while the executive previously presented the budget to the Turkish Grand National Assembly, today the executive presents it again. There are two main changes made. One of these is that if the budget is not accepted in the Turkish Grand National Assembly, an interim budget is envisaged. This was essentially an issue that existed in the Public Financial Management and Control Law, and was also included in the Constitution. If the temporary budget could not be made, it was envisaged that the country would not be left without a budget by increasing it at the rate of revaluation of the previous year's budget. There is no transfer of authority here. From where? Because the previous year's budget was also a budget accepted by the will of the Grand National Assembly of Turkey and us, it consists of the rationalized implementation of the systems that exist in other countries, countries governed by the presidential system, in Turkey by evaluating that budget at a revaluation rate, so that the country does not remain without a budget.
The second issue is actually the issue that concerns our Commission. In the Constitution, as you know, during the parliamentary system, the Planning and Budget Commission was a power commission. According to the Constitution, 25 members of the 40-member Plan and Budget Commission had to be from the government. With the change, this place was made more democratic, and political parties were allowed to take part here according to their representation rates in the Parliament. Therefore, with the transition to the Presidential government system, I would like to state for the record that the statements that there was a negative regulation in the budget system that took away the budget right of the Parliament are not true.
Thank you.
Mr. President, dear members of parliament; With the negotiations we started today, discussions will be held on the budgets of the Grand National Assembly of Turkey and the Presidency, as well as the budgets of 17 ministries and a total of 227 public institutions, the final accounts of 226 public institutions and 229 Court of Accounts reports. Budget and final account laws regulated in Article 161 of the Constitution are among the basic legislative activities of the Turkish Grand National Assembly. The budget, which is authorized by the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye to transfer resources to the public and distribute these resources in the next one year, is also an important tool of the economic and social policies implemented. The 2025 budget, presented as a stability, enforcement and development budget, is defined as a budget that makes the gains sustainable and prioritizes economic development and social welfare, and it is seen that it was prepared in line with the medium-term program and the Twelfth Development Plan targets.

Budget expenditures are increased to 14.7 trillion Turkish liras, an increase of 32.8 percent compared to the previous year's budget law, and the ratio of budget expenditures to gross domestic product is expected to be 23.9 percent. It is aimed to increase budget revenues to 12.8 trillion liras with an increase of 51.7 percent, the budget deficit is estimated to decrease by 27.2 percent to 1.9 trillion liras, the ratio of the budget deficit to gross domestic product is expected to be 3.1 percent, and when earthquake expenses are excluded, this rate is predicted to be 2.2 percent. While budget income and expenditure forecasts are made, it is estimated that the gross domestic product will grow by 4 percent in 2025, the unemployment rate will be 9.6 percent; inflation rate 17.5 percent; It was estimated that exports would be 279.6 billion dollars and imports would be 369 billion dollars. The budget proposal also included year-end expectations regarding the 2024 central government budget realizations, and accordingly, the ratio of the 2024 budget deficit to national income was estimated to be 4.9 percent.
Dear MPs, it is important to show the objectives of the budget in which direction and for what purposes the budget resources are used. When we look at the shares allocated from the budget over the years, it is seen that the rate of social security, social aid, economic support and incentives, especially education and health, is in a steady increase. The largest share in the budget, approximately 2.2 trillion Turkish Liras and 14.8 percent, has been allocated to education, as an indicator of the importance given to education, education investments and education employees in order to ensure that education, one of the most basic public services, is widespread and accessible to every individual of our nation in every part of our country and to increase its quality. Along with education, the second largest share was allocated to health with 1.1 trillion liras. It has been stated that in this way, it is aimed to reach our citizens all over the country with health services through fully equipped hospitals and to support the health infrastructure.
Turkey supports the relatives of martyrs and veterans, the needy and poor, the disabled and retired, and shows that it is always on their side with the important steps it takes for social support and social protection. In this context, the resource allocated to social aid expenditures, which is expected to be 504.1 billion lira in 2024, was increased to 650.9 billion lira in 2025. This amount corresponds to 4.4 percent of the 2025 budget. In addition, in the 2025 budget, a resource of 472 billion lira was allocated for our citizens to use electricity and natural gas more cheaply, and a tax exemption of 810.4 billion lira was envisaged within the scope of excluding the minimum wage from tax. We believe that these resources will ensure that the needy, disadvantaged groups and the elderly live an honorable life by acting with the understanding of spreading the welfare of our country to all layers of society, as a requirement of the social state principle.
In our opinion, one of the strategic sectors of the future is agriculture. The resource allocated to agriculture in the 2025 budget was increased from 384 billion lira to 705.6 billion lira.

The resource allocated for agricultural support programs, which was 91.6 billion lira in the 2024 budget, was increased by 47 percent compared to the previous year, reaching 135 billion lira, and agricultural sector investment appropriations were increased by 64 percent compared to the previous year, reaching 166 billion lira. A resource of 204.6 billion lira has been allocated for agricultural credit subsidies, intervention purchases, agricultural SOEs and export supports.
In the 2025 budget, a total investment expenditure allowance of 1 trillion 569.2 billion lira is envisaged, 337.9 billion lira for capital transfers, 242.2 billion lira of which will be used in the construction of disaster residences and infrastructure expenses, and 128.9 billion lira for the investment acceleration allowance. In addition, a significant share of the budget was allocated for real sector supports, and an appropriation of 561 billion lira was foreseen.
When the resources allocated for the Defense Industry Support Fund are included in the defense and security sector, an appropriation of 1 trillion 608 billion liras has been allocated, which corresponds to 10.9 percent of the budget. With the contribution of these allocated resources, our defense industry will be strengthened to minimize its foreign dependency, and the determination to ensure the peace and security of our nation will continue uncompromisingly.
As a result, it can be seen that the budget proposal includes programs, supports and allowances that aim at the effective delivery of basic public services, focus on the needs of our citizens, protect all segments of society, and that the targets are determined in accordance with the upper documents.
Mr. President, dear members of our Commission; At a time when historical developments are taking place in our region, despite the ring of fire around us, Turkey continues to stand out as an island of peace, strengthen economically, develop national unity and solidarity, and strive to achieve its goal of becoming one of the largest economies in the world. The Turkish economy maintains its strong outlook despite the weak foreign demand conditions caused by global anti-inflation policies and the negativities caused by the earthquake disaster. Macroeconomic data indicate a growing and strengthening economy, and our economy is showing significant developments as a result of the economic program being implemented.
The Turkish economy, which continued its uninterrupted growth process for fourteen years and grew by 5.1 percent in 2023, grew by 2.5 percent in the second quarter of 2024 and achieved uninterrupted growth for sixteen consecutive quarters. Thus, the growth rate in the first half of 2024 was 3.8 percent, and Türkiye became one of the major economies with a national income exceeding 1.1 trillion dollars. The increase in employment shows that growth continues on a sustainable path. In the first eight months of the year, there was an employment increase of over 650 thousand, and the unemployment rate decreased to 8.5 percent in August. The employment increase in the last year reached 1 million 155 thousand people. Of course, there have been discussions here about idle labor a while ago. These discussions are undoubtedly true, but I think another remarkable point that should be emphasized is that the desired personnel cannot be found in many sectors at the moment; We follow and see this closely both in the manufacturing industry and in other sectors.

I think that an effective measure regarding this should be put into effect quickly.
With the disinflation process that started in June, inflation decreased by more than 26 points in four months, falling below 50 percent as of September and reaching 44.3 percent. The success achieved in the foreign trade balance and current account deficit has been another pleasing development showing that the program targets are being realized. Our exports reached a historical peak, exceeding 261 billion dollars. In August, the annual current account deficit decreased by 44.4 billion dollars compared to May 2023, at 11.3 billion dollars. Confidence in the Turkish lira has increased significantly and the share of Turkish lira deposits in total deposits has increased significantly. The share of TL deposits in total deposits increased to 54.1 percent as of September, and this increase showed that our citizens' tendency to invest their savings in local currency has strengthened. On the other hand, outflows from exchange rate-protected deposits have accelerated recently, and the KKM balance, which reached approximately 144 billion dollars in 2023, decreased by 98 billion dollars to 47 billion dollars as of the beginning of September 2024. While Turkey's risk premium decreased, portfolio inflow increased, and the Central Bank reserves exceeded 159 billion dollars in the week of October 18, reaching historical high levels, showing that our financial stability is strengthening.
Along with the determination shown in the fight against inflation, effective fiscal policies and social policies are also implemented to improve income distribution justice. Ensuring income distribution justice and increasing social welfare is undoubtedly the main goal of economic policies. For this purpose, measures such as creating a fair tax system, preventing informality, preventing waste and irregularities by increasing public savings have also been put into effect. We consider that continuing the measures taken with determination will contribute to ensuring income distribution justice along with policies to grow the economy and increase welfare. At this point, we consider that we have entered a period in which the problem is behind us, the improvement in macroeconomic data will continue in line with the program targets, and the downward trend in inflation will be reflected in the welfare of our citizens. Türkiye continues to gain the trust of domestic and foreign economic circles as one of the least indebted countries that produces, creates employment, increases its exports, reduces its current account deficit. Undoubtedly, the administrative stability established with the Presidential government system, the political stability provided by the People's Alliance, strengthened democracy and predictability are also the guarantees of economic stability.
As the Nationalist Movement Party, we foresee the establishment of an economic and social order that produces, creates employment, enables everyone to receive a fair share of the value produced, makes income distribution fair, and is based on the equitable sharing of blessings and burdens by all segments.

In this regard, the state should pay taxes, which are the main source of income to meet public revenues, according to everyone's financial ability; We find it necessary to build a fair tax system that makes it possible to collect less tax from those who earn less and more from those who earn more. We find it necessary to continue the steps taken for this purpose, the reform efforts and inspections initiated until a fair tax system is established. As stated in the plans and programs and included among the priorities, we wish that structural reforms that will eliminate the vulnerabilities of the Turkish economy are carried out quickly. We consider that it is the responsibility of politics to our nation to use the time until 2028 effectively and efficiently by focusing on development goals with stability, national unity, which is an important chance for Türkiye. It is time to make the gains achieved in macroeconomic and financial stability permanent and move them forward through structural reforms. It is time to take into account global and regional developments, to walk determinedly towards development goals and to strengthen our national unity in order to build the Turkish and Turkish Century in stability in line with national ideals.
Dear Commission members, I would like to briefly touch upon the 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal and the Court of Accounts reports. In 2023, the ratio of budget deficit to gross domestic product was 5.2 percent. Earthquake expenditures worth 960 billion Turkish lira were effective in increasing the budget deficit in 2023. Excluding earthquake expenditures, the ratio of budget deficit to gross domestic product is 1.6 percent. According to economic classification, current transfers and capital transfers took the first two places in the 2023 budget appropriations with a rate of 49.1 percent, and personnel expenses and social security state premium expenses took the first place with a rate of 22.9 percent.
