Within the scope of the regularity and performance audits carried out in 2023, the Court of Accounts identified a total of 8,755 findings in public administrations, 2,266 of which affected financial reports and tables, 6,216 of which were related to compliance with the legislation, and 273 of which included errors related to the financial management and internal control system of public administrations; The highest number of findings were related to local administrations with 6,315, as in 2022.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
İSMAİL FARUK AKSU (Istanbul) - I am finishing, Mr. President.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Here you go.
İSMAİL FARUK AKSU (Istanbul) - It has been stated that one of the most common mistakes is not making accounting records for the immovable properties allocated or used by public administrations or making them incorrectly. One of the most important duties of the parliament is undoubtedly the supervision of public expenditures.

I would like to express that the investigations of the Court of Accounts, which is a constitutional audit institution and exercises its audit authority on behalf of our Parliament, have been carried out seriously, that we see this as important for the function of protecting public interests and improving the administration through audit, and that it is gratifying that it has published the activity reports of all public administrations within the scope of central government in 2023 and the reports have been shared with the public. However, we think that some of the current findings can be reduced by strengthening the communication between the Court of Accounts and institutions, enabling mutual discussions and planned training activities. Within the scope of the findings, the majority of which are in local administrations, we are also curious about whether there are issues that may indirectly cause public harm, what measures are taken against them, and whether sanctions are applied. Moreover, as we have brought to the agenda before, I would like to reiterate that the establishment of a final accounts examination commission in the Turkish Grand National Assembly for the audit of final accounts is necessary together with a Constitutional amendment.
With these thoughts, I hope that the 2025 central government budget will be beneficial to our country, nation and institutions, and I greet you with respect.
CHAIRMAN MEHMET MUŞ - Thank you, Mr. Aksu.
Mr Ocaklı, come on.
TAHSİN OCAKLI (Rize) - I respectfully greet our Vice President, our Commission Chairman and our members of parliament.
Actually, it would have been better if I had spoken a few minutes later, so you might probably lose your appetite now, Mr. Vice President, that's why I said this.
Yes, I don't have ten minutes now, right, President?
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Twenty minutes, but you don't have to use it all.
TAHSİN OCAKLI (Rize) - I'll stop when I say what I can say.
First, let's celebrate October 29. Yesterday, Republic Day celebrations were held all over Turkey in a befitting manner. We really need to congratulate our citizens, the participants and everyone else. May the Republic be with us for many more years. Those who gifted October 29 to us, the fearless heroes, really founded this country because they were fearless when establishing this country, and those who founded the country actually took a huge step in paving the way for this country by not involving us in the Second World War.
I can't remember exactly whether he was in the Grand National Assembly of Turkey or outside, but - it doesn't really matter - Mr. President came out and said: "Israel threatens us. Therefore, we need to create a budget that will strengthen our defense against this threat." You tried to impose an additional burden on citizens in Turkey by creating a new tax bracket under the name of "Defense Industry Support Fund".
As you know, the Planning and Budget Commission is a Commission formed to reconcile bill proposals before they go to the General Assembly. While those negotiations were being held, unfortunately, before we could even express our opinion, that proposal was withdrawn on the grounds that it was not examined sufficiently, probably with a phone call from the President, without considering the opinions of the Commission and the Parliament.

Here, I am also protecting the President of Mehmet Muş, by the way, not only ourselves. In fact, a proposal that was canceled without our having any idea, on the grounds that it was not sufficiently prepared, appears before us as a reflection and response of the one-man regime imposed on us by today's system.
Turkey has a new constitutional debate. There is a need for this constitutional debate, but there is also a need for Turkey's democratization. Unfortunately, Öcalan's request to stop terrorism, which started with Mr. Bahçeli's call from the Parliament rostrum, was also expressed as a new initiative by Erdoğan, but the democratic part of the matter is not addressed at all - the truth of the matter is - it is just a project where arrangements are made or work is carried out in order to be able to take office again in the elections, otherwise the issue is not the democratization of Turkey. How can I say this? Dear President, dear members of parliament; In order to make a new constitution, we must first comply with our governing provisions in the existing Constitution. Proposing a new constitution while we have a member of parliament who has not yet been extradited to the Parliament and who is detained in prison actually makes us think about other things.
Now, unfortunately, this central government budget has no equivalent in the eyes of the public, that is, this budget is not made for the people, this budget is made for the capital. I regret to say that this budget has been prepared for public-private cooperation projects, bridges, hospitals and highways, in order to fulfill the debts to the companies that create the current account deficit. Approximately 14.3 billion TL has been allocated for this in 2025, allocated to contractors. There is another remarkable place in the budget: The budget is actually an interest budget, that is, we pay interest equal to half of the salaries of everyone working in Turkey. I think it was 15 million, there should be such a number. The amount of interest we pay corresponds to half of the salaries of all employees in Turkey, and should be 1 trillion 590 million. Therefore, we are paying a budget, and therefore interest, that is half the salary paid to 15 million people.
Dear MPs, for example, there is an item in the budget of the Ministry of Transport, it says: "Transfer to households and businesses." Now, I am asking our citizens living in households in Turkey: Have there been any transfers to your households? No, on the contrary, your burden has probably become heavier with heavy tax burdens, but 14 billion 385 million has been allocated for this transfer issue; Again, these go into the pockets of the contractors. A support of 138 billion lira will be required for contractors from the Treasury aid.
Now, Turkey's problem is poverty and unemployment; Unfortunately, there is nothing about them in this budget, but I repeat once again, as I see a significant danger for Turkey: Particularly regarding the wild mining issue, by opening the way for multinational companies in Turkey, preparations are being made for Turkey to be employed with cheap labor, like a colonial African country. Now, look, in the Eastern Black Sea Region, there are places that have been declared as mining areas and licensed as mining areas, with 70 percent in Rize, 71 percent in Artvin, 65 percent in Giresun, and so on.

How can you give licenses to 70 percent of a province? Among these, of course, quarry mining is acceptable, provided that it is rehabilitated, but we will allow the companies doing business in Turkey a situation that we call the "fourth group mining activity", that is, where the European Union countries prohibit the regulation that allows the separation of gold through cyanide; This is not something that will happen.
Again, another price of nepotism here is the delivery of the copper mine enterprise, which was made through a home delivery tender in our Zonguldak province, to a company for 3.68 billion, but has a reserve of 456 billion; It looks like this: You create a specification for the office of a Vice President, it starts with "C" and ends with "Y", or for the Presidency of the Budget Commission, it starts with "M" and ends with "S", or if that's not enough, you say that the city starts with "S", and the IDs delivered to the address are determined for the vice positions. Tenders are delivered like this in Turkey, unfortunately, you deliver like this, and the deficits that arise because of this are the deficits that we cannot cover, such as the deficits in the Northern Marmara Highway, which we cannot cover even if we use all the vehicles in Turkey. Unfortunately, as many of my fellow MPs give an example, perhaps 80 percent of the society pays the burden that arises in the budget; Even though 20 percent of them are rich by benefiting from equal opportunities, they are put in the same situation by the unfair tax system.
Now, what needs to be done here is to make arrangements to ensure that this budget receives more public benefit; For example, when we look at what is not included in this budget, we see that there is no effect in this budget to alleviate the financial difficulties in an environment where acts of violence and harassment against women and children are growing, which are reported in the newspapers, where occupational murders are increasing, where high inflation and the cost of living continue, which are on our agenda and accepted by all of us these days - in fact, most of you probably accept this. Therefore, something is needed that will eliminate our concerns, there is no regulation regarding this. Well, the terror of companies that engage in counterfeiting and adulteration practices on food products cannot be stopped completely. Look, a month or two ago, when a tea factory made inappropriate news about tea, access to the "website" that made the news was blocked. However, can there be anything more sacred than information? Of course, citizens will know this, but unfortunately such things happen.
There are very important scandals in public health, health, especially in our hospitals, there are serious problems, drug prices. We have citizens with SMA who will come to the Parliament on Monday, whose medications are not covered by SSI due to their pediatric diseases; There is no solution to their problems. There is nothing for young people to address their despair about young people.

Here, while taxes are collected from citizens, taking into account revaluation rates and paying taxes in that way, paying salaries or wages to citizens based on target inflation can be shown as the most concrete example of your unfair management.
In summary, I propose to revise this budget and make it more useful for society by establishing a sub-commission; We will present these with a proposal. I don't think there is much hope of changing this, but I say that even if we cannot change this budget, we still hope to change this government for Türkiye.
It may not be related to this topic, but let me tell you something else: This morning, our Esenyurt Mayor was detained at seven in the morning as if he were a human trafficker, and was taken from his home early and detained without an invitation. You've probably all heard the reason. Ten years ago, in the Imrali minutes, he was claimed to be a name discussed in the peace talks, that is, a professor who was a dean at a university, and now he is an elected Mayor who served for one and a half years, but as part of the Istanbul plan, he is detained from his home at seven in the morning without any invitation. Here, in another example, we have seen the reflection of political tutelage or the power of the government over the judiciary. We do not see the possibility of improving these issues in your government, so we consider it our duty to remove you from power by holding early elections as soon as possible.
I would also like to thank our bureaucrats for their presentation, our Vice President, and our Commission President for giving me the floor, and I offer my respects.
PRESIDENT MEHMET MUŞ - Thank you Mr. Ocaklı.
Now Antalya Deputy Mr. Cavit Arı.
Mr. Bee, come on.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Mr. President, Mr. Vice President, dear participants, my fellow MPs; First of all, I greet you all with respect and love.
Yesterday, we celebrated the 101st anniversary of our Republic with enthusiasm throughout the country. Once again, I commemorate our Great Leader Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk and all his comrades with respect and gratitude.
We are negotiating the budget for 2025. In the 2025 budget, 12 trillion 800 billion was envisaged as a revenue budget, 14 trillion 731 billion was envisaged as an expense budget, and at the very beginning of the work, we are starting with a budget deficit of 1 trillion 930 billion 689 thousand. Although “Sir, the budget figures have increased, the budget deficit has increased accordingly.” Although it is said that this budget deficit - let me put it this way - increased from 142.7 billion in 2022 to 2 trillion 652 billion in 2024, that is, more than 10 times, even up to 18 times. So, it is obvious that there is actually an economic problem here.
First of all, I would like to make a brief evaluation about the 2024 budget: In the 2024 budget, there will be 11 trillion 89 billion as expenditure budget,
The budget revenue started with 8 trillion 437 billion and - as I stated - the budget deficit is 2 trillion 652 billion.

In this budget presentation, our Vice President again made the following statement: "The 2024 budget is the budget of resilient cities, resilient economy and resilient society." "The 2024 budget was prepared by taking into account all segments of society and all regions of our country, including workers, civil servants, retirees, tradesmen, farmers, children, young and old." A sentence like. Let me express this: There is the same sentence in the 2025 budget presentation booklet, you used the same sentence here again. The budget essentially tells us how the state's resources, that is, the revenues and taxes collected from all of us, will be prepared by the government, and the understanding of the governments reveals itself here. Where that collected money will be spent shows the thought of that government. Although it is mentioned here as "workers, civil servants, retirees, tradesmen, farmers", all segments of society, especially workers, civil servants, and especially retirees who know that 2024 has been declared the Year of Retirement, tradesmen and farmers who are experiencing great difficulties, children who are under threat every day, young people who are worried about the future, see and know with experience that they can no longer benefit from this budget and that this budget does not bring any benefit to them. I would like to express that we hope that the 2025 budget will find a solution to the problems of these segments. However, if we continue like this: In the 2024 Budget Law Proposal, you stated that the budget was prepared in 2024 with a people-oriented and social welfare-enhancing approach that supports investment, employment, production and exports. Since you think that you could not fulfill all your promises in the observation I just made and the observation I made with this sentence, I guess you wrote the same sentences in this year's presentation. If you had been able to do these things last year, I'm sure you wouldn't have written them in this year's budget presentation. “We will overcome the high cost of living with realistic policies determined by common sense.” You said, but stop overcoming the high cost of living today, our citizens are groaning under this high cost of living, and this has happened with the budget you prepared and the budget method you applied.
Yes, the budget exists to meet the needs of living citizens and the public, to solve problems, and again, as you put it, "We will ensure that each of our citizens always looks to the future with confidence." You said, but this fancy words remained only in that booklet. Look, our citizens' concerns about the future continue to increase. Our retiree has now given up hope on the future, and if he can buy a loaf of bread and go home in the evening, he is considered to have saved the day. Our farmer has given up hope for the future; If he can replant his field with support and debt, his day has been saved. 3 out of 4 young people are considering going abroad at the first opportunity. All of these developments, which we can list innumerable, stem from this budget approach you prepared and the budget method you applied, because the methods applied make the rich get richer and their supporters get richer; As for our tradesmen, farmers, workers and unemployed, "There are no resources." It was a budget application called.

I would like to express this again: While defining the budget in the 2025 budget, you stated that you prepared the budget with an understanding of "stability, execution and development budget". As a political party that has been preparing a budget and being in power for twenty-three years, you bringing these goals to the agenda today, in my opinion, is a demonstration - in a sense - that you, too, aim to provide the stability that does not exist in this country, which we have mentioned many times before. Yes, there is no stability in this country, there is no stable government in this country.
Again, look, we call it a development budget, but we are in a period where you cannot go beyond just public-private cooperation investments. “We developed exports.” You say, "We will improve." you say; You shared data about imports for the first time this year. We have always criticized it in the past years. Your target for exports last year was 254.2 billion dollars, and you stated that you achieved an export of 255.6 billion dollars in 2023. For the first time, a figure regarding imports has been revealed, our imports are at the level of 345 billion dollars. In other words, despite everything, our export items are still below import expenses.
When evaluating the budget of this country, I think it is more accurate to evaluate the current situation of the country. I really want to express that there is no hope for the future in society anymore, everyone is worried, and we are living in a period where there is no assurance of tomorrow, thanks to you. As I just said, the farmer is hesitant about whether or not he will be rewarded for what he has planted. Cultivation areas have now shrunk and farmers have given up on production. The number of our farmers is decreasing day by day. Our tradesmen are worried about tomorrow. Look, the tradesman who opens his shop is at a loss even if he doesn't sell, and at a loss even if he sells; We are living in such a period. Our young people's hopes for the future are gone, and when we ask, 3 out of every 4 young people know that there is lack of merit in this country, that there is discrimination, and that they cannot get a job if there is no political influence due to discrimination in interviews. You have created such a country. That's why I stated that unfortunately, 3 out of 4 young people are waiting for an opportunity to leave this country. We really need to fix these.
Look, yes, the economy is important, but one of the most important data with which we will improve the economy is the judiciary. Unfortunately, it is not possible to say that the judiciary is fair, impartial and independent. We asked questions here in the past and were told in a written answer that trust in the judiciary was around 38 percent - it was two years ago. If the trust in the judiciary in a country drops to 38 percent, it means that there is no judiciary in that country; It means that the judiciary has no impartiality or independence. Yes, today the judiciary has become, in a sense, a tool for the political power to design the opposition; I say this clearly here. The judiciary has come to swing like a sword over political actors. The impartiality and independence of the judiciary must be ensured as soon as possible. If we cannot achieve this, how much do you say "We brought a budget, we made a budget, we spent here, we spent there?" Even if you say so, they will all remain idle.

You stated that 1 trillion 452 billion TL was allocated to the budget of the Ministry of National Education among the budget items and that reasons such as quality education, improvement of the physical conditions of schools and school construction were provided. First of all, let me say this: Look, no matter how much money you allocate to the National Education budget, unfortunately you have collapsed education today. We are living in a period where education has become of poor quality. Unfortunately, there is a big problem in the education our young people receive today. There are serious problems in both physical conditions and the quality of education. I would like to express that there is a great problem in the education of young people, especially with the current education model that has developed with the understanding of the government. Look, there are interview results announced about teachers, there is a government that cannot even come out and say what the results are exactly, and our party has shown the necessary reaction and is taking action.
Besides, let me give you a few examples regarding National Education from Antalya alone: ​​We have Karşıyaka Neighborhood in Kepez district, one of the largest districts of Antalya. I brought it up in this hall in the past period, in 2022 and in 2023. It is a central district with a population of 15,500 and there is no school or primary school in this neighborhood. As we said the previous year, "We included it in the investment program for 2023." came the answer. The year 2024 is about to end, there is no school or foundation. At least nearly a thousand primary school children go to other neighborhoods. Again, I would like to talk about only one region of Kepez: There are not enough primary schools, secondary schools, or high schools in the 11 neighborhoods called "Göçerler Kütükçü District" region, which covers 11 neighborhoods with a population of 130-150 thousand. In Göçerler District, sir, “It is not durable.” He broke down and said, "We'll do it right away." Four years have passed since the demolition of the so-called school, and there is no foundation yet. There is a problem-free parcel of 5 thousand square meters reserved as a National Education school area in Yavuz Selim District. The headman of the neighborhood and the citizens have been fighting for years; There is no school. "We have finished dual education in this country" in the center of Antalya. During this period of power - the Minister of National Education will come here and I will tell him too - let me tell you, I am telling it for those who know Antalya, and for those who do not know, there is Melahat Faraçlar Primary School and Secondary School in the center of Antalya, in the area called Kırcami, right at the intersection of Portakal Çiçeği and Perge streets; There are 850 students in the secondary school and 900 students in the primary school and there is dual education in this school in the center of Antalya, Mr. Vice President. I'm talking about the center of Antalya. This is perhaps one of the most expensive neighborhoods in Antalya, I pass by it every day. This year, before the end of the academic year, we brought it to the agenda in May and June, and met with the relevant Deputy Ministers. "Look, there is such a problem. Primary school children go to school in the dark of the morning, secondary school students have to leave school and go home at dusk. There is a problem with the performance of teachers, parents are in trouble. Just 200 meters away, there is Martyr Muhammet Oğuz Kılınç Anatolian Imam-Hatip High School, and this school has a capacity of perhaps 2 thousand students, but It has 250 students; we decided to move Melahat Faraçlar Primary School or Secondary School to part of this school.

While it was going positive at first, the Imam-Hatip Schools Association stepped in and stopped it. What is it sir? It will remain as imam-hatip. Well, the school is yours, brother, but until the new one was built, for two years or three years, at least until the capacity of that school reached a certain number of students, why did we not send these children to this school? Is it because it is called Imam-Hatip that new students were prevented from enrolling there?
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN MEHMET MUŞ - Mr. Bee, your time has expired.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - I am completing it, Mr. President.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Come on, get it together, please.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - This is how you manage National Education.
Apart from that, let me say this: Look, there is a project related to the Antalya-Alanya highway, the ring road, so it has become a story like this, "The tender has been made." It was said. For example, I asked and said: What is the warranty scope of this project? How many years of warranty is there? How many vehicles are guaranteed to pass per day? How much will the transit guarantee fee be per vehicle? When will the tender be held? If so, when will it be approved? What are these conditions? I read the answer given: "It is carried out within the framework of the provisions contained in the implementation contracts." That's a mocking answer.
Look, loans were distributed to our tradesmen before the election - let me finish by saying this - the day after the election, that is, March 31, the election ended, the Republican People's Party won 411 municipalities, and on the morning of April 1, Halk Bank withdrew the money from the accounts of all cooperatives, that is, it punished the tradesmen. You may or may not know this, but look, the five-year maturity amount of our tradesmen was reduced to three years, the one-six month payment plan was reduced to one-three months, and the interest rate was increased to 29 percent, the very next day after the election. I want to ask what this means.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - President, let me complete it for the sake of health.
Look, health-related problems are at their peak. We make at least two or three calls a day to find a place for the patient in intensive care or to make an appointment. We have to take care of at least 2-3 patients.
There is a Provincial Health Directorate problem in Antalya. The Provincial Director of Health was changed 3 times in one year and 6 times in three years; "Good luck, I hope you will be permanent" to the new one. we said. In a city where health problems have increased so much, the Provincial Director of Health does not stand firm and I would like to share the answer given to the question we asked here: "As the Ministry, the only criterion we consider in the appointment of managers is the principle of merit. The appointment and assignment procedures carried out by our Ministry are carried out entirely in accordance with the rules of law, and the allegations in the question in question are not true."
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN MEHMET MUŞ - Mr. Arı, come on.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Am I the one appointing them, friend? Did I appoint these 6 Provincial Health Directors or did we appoint them as a party? You appoint the Provincial Director of Health yourself, sir, and you take back the Provincial Director of Health you appointed after six months, eight months later. If merit is important, why do you appoint unqualified men, that's what I'm asking you.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Mr. Arı, the Minister of Health will come.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - One of them is a professor.

In conclusion, this budget, when we look at the general total, are the issues that should solve the problems of the citizens with all its applications.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - I would like to express that the budget is not sufficient in terms of numbers, the expenditure items are not transferred to the right places, the spending method is not correct, and we are living in a period where the problems of citizens are increasing in every field.
Thank you.
PRESIDENT MEHMET MUŞ - Thank you.
Dear MPs, we will discuss the ministries one by one. Let's ask our Minister of Health about this Provincial Director of Health issue, I'm curious too, they will definitely have an answer.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - The Minister of Health will say: I became the new Minister.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Continuity is essential in the state.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Let's ask, I asked 3 times.
CHAIRMAN MEHMET MUŞ - We will also ask you questions live here.
We thank you.
Dear MPs, now, we will continue with those who requested to speak from our members of the Commission, Mr. Heval Bozdağ, Mr. Vehbi Bakırlıoğlu, Mr. Mustafa Erdem and Mr. İsmail Güneş. After these MPs' request to speak is met, we will continue with our MPs who are not members of the Commission. First of all, we start with the political parties that do not have groups, Ms. Sevda Karaca Demir from the Labor Party, we will give her the first promise. Afterwards, after meeting the request of the deputies who are not members of the Commission, such as Mr. Mahmut Tanal, Mr. Sevilay Çelenk, Mr. Rıdvan Uz, Mr. Ömer Fethi Gürer, Mr. Zülküf Uçar, Mr. Hüsmen Kırkpınar, Mr. Hasan Toktaş, Mr. Rüştü Tiryaki and Mr. Yasin Öztürk, we will move on to the question-answer part and finish the day.
Dear MPs.
ELİF ESEN (Istanbul) - President, if possible, I would like to ask too.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Yes, the DEVA Party also came, we will promise the DEVA Party after EMEP for five minutes.
Dear MPs, please inform our party groups MPs because we are not going back after passing this ranking.
Once our deputies who are members of the Commission surpass those who are not members of the Commission, we will not go back again; Everyone should plan accordingly.
Thank you very much.
Now Mr. Heval Bozdağ from DEM Party.
Mr. Bozdağ, come in please.
HEVAL BOZDAĞ (Ağrı) - Thank you, Mr. President.
Dear Vice President, esteemed bureaucrats, press workers, esteemed Commission members; I greet you all with respect.
Now, regarding this budget, of course, as everyone, all our friends and MPs have mentioned, the right to budget needs to be asserted, the concept of "budget" needs to be discussed. It is necessary to discuss whether this budget is really what the society deserves, how much the society contributed to this budget, whether their demands were met, whether the items needed compared to the previous period budget were really the items that were met in this budget. We all know how the right to budget emerged, the year 1215, the period when a royal king could increase or decrease taxes as he wished, and at the same time create his budget by applying force - of course. From that day until today.

The barons objected, interestingly enough, they fought, of course, this is also important, rebellions broke out, and somehow they were able to ensure their own participation, at least in determining the taxes they paid to the king. Frankly, is the society at that point today? We need to look at it a little bit, that is, can the society rebel - in quotation marks - while these budget items are being determined? So stop participating in the debate - right - can he find areas where he can object? This is very important because we make decisions about society. We allocate the right shares to the society and distribute those shares through our own bureaucrats and administrators, and we carry out the process by foreseeing that there is justice in this distribution and that there is a correct payment. Here, we operate a process that is very easy and conscientious. Frankly, I don't think it works very fairly this way.
While I was browsing the internet about budget rights, after a few clicks, a research article came across me and I took a look at it. Let me mention the name Ali Gökhan Gölçek. He was a finance master's student at Dokuz Eylül University in 2014. Now, when I read it briefly, there is something I see there - it's only 2014, let me draw attention to this - it says: "If the politicians elected by the public turn to short-term, populist policies instead of long-term projects with the concern of retaining power, the state will face budget deficits and borrowing in the long term, and as a result, the increased debt and interest costs will fall on the society, that is, the tax-paying people." He mentioned Greece as "for example" and discussed the 2008 crisis. "The effort to retain power by intimidating the people with expansionary monetary and fiscal policies that stimulated domestic demand led to a huge crisis in the following years. Severe economic conditions brought the country's economy almost to a halt." Afterwards I gave up scanning, I thought this was enough. Frankly, I got a share from this as well.
Now, Mr. Vice President, there was your presentation, in your presentation. Actually, I will summarize my speech as follows, I will also focus on the health aspect, I will focus on budget preferences, that is, why were these items preferred? And I will make a few comments about what should be preferred and prioritized regarding health.
Now, you have made evaluations about the 2025 central government budget and said, "For example, we allocate 1 trillion 106 billion liras for health services." You said, you said, and then you immediately added SSI and said that with the health expenditures from the Social Security Institution, this would reach 2 trillion 435 billion lira. Here, of course, the share has suddenly grown a lot. However, the budget we discussed here was the central budget and the shares allocated to the ministries from the central budget, but the 16.52 percent mentioned in the speech here - also in your notes - appeared as if a share of the budget was allocated to health, and such a perception was created. Why do I dwell on this perception?

Because it was discussed; It was critically discussed and evaluated in newspapers, articles and columns. It was said like this, and frankly I say the same: If a share of 16.52 percent is allocated to health today, if we include SSI, which is almost the same as in the previous period, it means that the 15 percent share, which is the average of OECD countries and is predicted as the share that should be allocated for health expenditures, has been achieved. If that were the case, we need to discuss why these health indicators exist. Then, it is necessary to discuss why there are these health indicators, or this figure is not really this, that is, the shares we spend on health. We may be making mistakes due to our choices, and it seems that way. Preferences are important here, I will focus on preferences a lot anyway. We have to discuss this.
Now, of course, we wanted to hear this sentence, and everyone wanted to hear this sentence: SSI and public out-of-pocket expenses will be reduced. Differences such as contributions, co-payments and additional fees will not be collected from the public for health services. Why is this so? Because there is a rising household healthcare expenditure; It is expected to increase from 18 and a half percent to 20 percent this period. Households' out-of-pocket health expenditures such as treatment, medicine and the like increased by 98.8 percent in 2022 compared to the previous year and reached 112 billion 18 million liras. Its share in total health expenditure was 18.5 percent in 2022; I haven't seen the year 2023 yet, it will probably be published in the Health Statistics Yearbook in December 2024, I will see it there; It is expected to be around 20 percent. I would also like to get a figure from you if you have one. What would we like to see? Frankly, we would like to see this rate decrease, this amount to decrease. Maybe you didn't need to explain, but there is one more pen, maybe you could have included one more paragraph in your presentation. What was that paragraph, what was it supposed to be? Because we discussed a lot last term, and both while discussing the Ministry of Health budget and in the general presentation, there was a request for HPV and flu vaccines to be free; It was mentioned a lot, almost all opposition parties expressed this, even some opposition municipalities took steps on this issue, and it was very good. We were expecting you to make a surprise here about HPV and flu vaccines and at least include a statement in your presentation that these will now be free; Unfortunately, we couldn't see it. While the share allocated to health in the central government budget was 6.6 percent in 2024, it increased to 7.5 percent this year; There is some increase. We are 3rd from the bottom in OECD health expenditures, this increase does not change that. The ratio of health expenditures to gross domestic product is 9.2 percent on OECD average; Turkey's place in this figure is 4.3 percent.
Well, we took a look at the items that increased the most in the budget in general. I specifically focused on three items: "Here are our preferences." I said. We have made our choices very clearly. The budget allocated for defense and security expenditures is 47 billion dollars. It is not clear what the dollar exchange rate will be, I have rounded it; 1.5 trillion. Interest expenditures increased from 11 percent to 13 percent, 1 trillion 950 billion liras.

Tax expenditures were increased from 2.2 trillion to 3 trillion. Now, why these three pens? These three items make approximately 6.5 trillion. If we consider that the total budget is 14.7 trillion, it is almost half of it. So, where are these interest expenses made? To debts. Where are tax expenditures made? It starts from public-private partnerships, abbreviated as capital. Defense and security expenditures require a very intense discussion about why we do what we do; In fact, it is an item that we should discuss where these expenditures should be made in terms of our future, in terms of democratization, in terms of freedoms, and in terms of the future of the Middle East, before it is too late. Why have defense and security expenditures increased? Especially considering that defense projects increased after 2002, considering that defense companies were in the 800s and 2000s, and assuming that these places generally kept themselves afloat with the support of capital, I honestly think that the expenditures made here should be discussed.
Now, as a result, it is seen that the budget allocated to health - we will discuss it more easily through health - is transferred to private capital as a resource. We know about city hospitals, city hospitals seriously eat up the health budget. This period, we are talking about 83 billion liras for the items that will go to city hospitals. Let me also tell you what is for 2025 and 2026: 104 billion 500 million lira and 115 billion lira. Now, when we look at it this way, between 2024 and 2025, city hospitals will be paid almost as much as the amount you allocated and increased to the health budget; At least it didn't fall short of that, which is good in that respect.
Now, here, where are the resources being transferred? The marketer is transferred to health. Without seeing this. Because if we are transferring a budget here, does this budget - that's why we said it - go to the society, does it extend, does it reach the society? Because we will discuss that in a moment. We think that it has not reached the public because the latest health data is very problematic. If it does not reach the society, then we need to ask why all this budget is being transferred here and where it is going. Frankly, there needs to be accountability for this as well. We think that it is going towards market-oriented health, neoliberal policies, that is, the Health Transformation, which is a World Bank project. There were 1 billion patient applications last year, 1 billion patient applications. If we were creating a healthy society then this number had to decrease. Economy, numbers etc., everything is very obvious, we discuss this a lot, everyone is talking about numbers here too, I think this is the simplest number, also in terms of health indicators. If you go to the hospital a lot, you have a health problem. No one visits the hospital for social activity, for sightseeing, or to see doctors. 1 billion and this number is increasing. Therefore, we think that the choice should be made from therapeutic expenses to preventive health services. We think that primary health care services should be supported.
Well, this is the share allocated to health in the budget.

When we look at whether it is allocated more to therapeutic expenses or primary health care services, unfortunately we see that this continues to increase as a share of therapeutic expenses. While it is around 26-27 percent, we see that a share at that level remains in primary care and the rest is allocated to therapeutic services.
Now, why doesn't capital invest in primary healthcare - very important? Why doesn't capital invest in primary healthcare? Because it is about preventive health services, there is no profit there, there is no need for therapeutic services, medical supplies or anything like that, there you prevent the disease before it occurs. For this reason, the person who is not sick does not participate in this trade, the health trade, as a subject or even as an object. That's why capital does not invest in primary health care. Then whose job is this, whose duty is it? The duty of the public, the duty of the state; must invest in primary healthcare. Why has to? There were no measles cases; last year, 76 local and 27 foreign measles were seen; total 103. 0.12 per hundred thousand. It is 2023, a year has passed, the number of domestic cases is 3,571, the number of foreign cases is 1,517. Look, the number per hundred thousand increased from 0.12 to 5.96. Under-5 mortality rates in 2023: See, while it was 11.1 per thousand in the previous year, 2022 - these figures are per thousand - it increased to 14 per thousand. It was written as "Southeastern Anatolia", the Kurdish geography was stated as "Southeastern Anatolia" in the statistics. While it is 16.5 in 2022 - see, it is above the Türkiye average. Türkiye's average has increased - 14 in 2023. In 2022, it is even higher than its increased rate, 16.5, and in 2023, this figure has increased to 21.6. We see the deaths of children under 5 in all weeks. Because in 2022, total health expenditure: Hospitals will consume 50 percent, retail sales and medical supplies will consume 22.8 percent, and city hospitals will consume 22.8 percent. So how much is devoted to public health? 9.2. When? In 2021. So, what is the share allocated to public health in 2022? 6.2 percent. There is a regression there too. In other words, we see that the share allocated to public health continues to decrease even within primary health care services. We do not make our choice in favor of public health, protecting society, or public health; This is seen very clearly. Why do we support capital, why do we support therapeutic services?
There were some numbers, I wanted to focus on them, about the number of newborn beds and the number of intensive care beds. You mentioned the number of ambulances in your presentation, their number has increased considerably; 5,696. Two or three days ago, a traffic accident occurred very close to Ağrı Taşlıçay and the person was transported to the hospital on a stretcher and the person died. It has indeed increased greatly, 618; 5,696 is a big number.
The number of newborn beds and intensive care beds has increased, you are right, you are right, we are also much above the averages compared to the European and OECD averages, but look, 54 percent of the number of newborn beds are private. The newborn issue has just come to the fore. I don't want to use newborns side by side with the gang, it was just on the agenda.

54 percent; There are so many beds that our lives have been taken to fill them - there is no need to explain that process.
Well, I would like to end my speech with a few questions. Now, do you think that the issue of profit and rent is more valuable to capital than human life? This is my question, Mr. Vice President. Or would you agree with the war industry's claim that wars are started by capital and people are brought into conflict with each other in order to sell more guns, tanks, planes, UAVs and SIHAs? If so, wouldn't leaving preventive health or peace of people in the background under the name of "treatment" or "defense and security expenditures" while making budget choices mean planning a mistake with the consequences? Or aren't we actually architecting the health or war conditions of the coming periods with our budget choices from the very beginning? So, after all, if we were to invest in health, the vast majority of diseases would be cured, and those that could not be treated would be preventable. If you allocate a budget for primary health care and public health services and do that job properly, you will create a healthy society.
Again, states can solve their problems without conflict if they really want to. After all, people know that their interests lie in compromise, and when the conditions for this are created, they give the strongest support to this compromise. What do you think about this? I'm asking very clearly: Why isn't a budget allocated and efforts aren't made to prevent society from getting sick? Don't we want a healthy society? Or why isn't a budget allocated and a continuous effort not made to make a social contract that will ensure reconciliation and peace sustainable? To get out of the economic crisis, it is always said that: "If structural problems are solved, economic growth will occur." Without ensuring justice, without democratization, without finding a democratic and peaceful solution to the Kurdish issue, which is perhaps our biggest structural problem, the economy cannot be improved and justice cannot be achieved in the budget.
Thanks.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Mr. Bozdağ, thank you.
Now, Mr. Vehbi Bakırlıoğlu.
Your time is twenty minutes.
Here you go.
AHMET VEHBİ BAKIRLIOĞLU (Manisa) - Dear Vice President, first of all, welcome.
Now, there is something I pay attention to. This is approximately my 6th budget. In all budget presentations, the presenters usually start like this: "Sir, this year we allocated the largest share to National Education." You also presented in the same way. I looked at previous years and there are almost the same words. In your budget presentation this year, you said, "We allocated the largest share of funds to National Education, with a share of 14.8 percent, a figure of 1.4 trillion." you said. Now, then, I want to ask this: If we allocate the largest share to National Education and education, why are our schools dirty today? If we allocate the largest share to National Education and training almost every year, why do we not have security personnel in our schools? Why do teachers have to face violence in our schools right now? Why can't we give a bowl of hot food to our students who go to school hungry?

Why can't we still solve the problems in mobile education, if we give the biggest share to National Education? The share allocated to the Ministry of National Education in the 2025 budget is 14.8 percent. You see, in 2015 this share was 17.2 percent; In 2016, it was 17.6 percent. When we look at it, we see that the share of National Education and the share allocated to education has decreased over the years. Oh, we see that 75 percent of these figures, 1 trillion 452 billion, are already insurance premiums and personnel expenses. How much is allocated for investment? You increased the share of sanctions from 100 billion lira to 142 billion lira, 66 billion of which is already in the earthquake zone. Let me tell you what I understand - other MPs have just mentioned it - today, not only in the earthquake zone but also in Manisa, many schools in many parts of Turkey have been demolished on the grounds that they are not earthquake resistant and new ones have not been built in their places, and our students have to do dual education and due to the summer time application you insist on, they have to go to school in the wee hours of the morning, they have to return home from school late at night, and even in Manisa, you reduced the lesson hours to thirty minutes just for this reason. So, when I look at these figures, I see that these problems in national education will continue.
What else is in the budget? We see that earthquake expenditures are decreasing. Earthquake expenditure will be 960 billion lira in 2023; It was 1 trillion lira in 2024, and it will be 584 billion lira in 2025. Well, we need to ask this: Have the wounds of the earthquake been completely healed in those 11 provinces in the earthquake zone, has the infrastructure been completely repaired, have the buildings to be given to the earthquake victims been delivered? Our orators told us that only 10 percent of it has still been delivered in Malatya. So, why is there a decrease in earthquake expenditures?
Agricultural supports, we have said it many times, and we will say it again. Here, agricultural supports are 135 billion liras, their share in the gross domestic product is 2 per thousand; In previous years, this was 6 per thousand, it dropped to 2 per thousand, but this year, we need to focus a little more on agricultural support, Mr. Vice President, because farmers are no longer able to produce. This year, tomatoes, peppers, melons, watermelons, all the fruits and vegetables you can think of were left in the field, and let me tell you this in summary: This farmer, our producer, has no chance of paying these debts anymore, you will see in the coming period. Currently, tractors have started to be sold, fields have started to be sold, people will not be able to produce, and we may face a greater food inflation in the coming period. This year, last year, you imposed a ban on the export of tomato paste to reduce the prices of tomato paste and to combat food inflation, but next year we may have to import tomato paste because our producers are really upset and have come to the point of quitting production.
We look at health expenditures. The amount spent on health from public resources this year is "2 trillion 435 billion lira." You mentioned in your presentation, 16.5 percent of the budget. However, in the 2024 presentation, this figure was 1 trillion 650 billion lira and the share allocated according to the initial budget was 19.5 percent; decreased, that is, the share allocated to the health budget decreased.

Our esteemed Central Bank President - it came across the public and the press - mentioned that there should be a 25 percent increase in the minimum wage. The figure of 25 percent is currently circulating in the public opinion, and the IMF is also talking about the same thing: "Don't raise the minimum wage too much." He said, and gave the following advice: "Instead, increase social aid." he said. Well, we look at social aid expenditures in the budget, it is 650 billion liras and its share in the budget is 4.4 percent in 2025. We are looking at 2024, I swear the IMF was more fair, that is, although it should have increased, it was 497 billion in 2024, its share in the budget is 4.5 percent. So let alone increasing or decreasing, it is almost the same.
Now, there are some really weird things in this budget. There are some weird things I can't figure out; I am strong in mathematics, I have a degree in statistics, but I really couldn't figure it out, let's see if we can get out together? Now, the ratio of gross personnel expenses to the budget is 26.5 percent; Last year it was 26.9 percent. This figure was 29.7 percent in 2016, meaning that the share of gross personnel expenses in the budget has decreased over time from 2016 to today. Well, we look at the number of civil servants and even the number of public personnel, it has increased by 46 percent since 2016. Now, the number of public personnel is increasing by 46 percent, but the amount allocated to public personnel is decreasing. So where is the devil in this? So look, a similar situation also applies to our retirees. There has been an increase of over 40 percent in the number of retirees since 2016, but you see, the share of pensions in national income is decreasing every year. Despite the EYT - these days, which are shown as a great disaster - despite the EYT, which is claimed to have disrupted the structure of the finance, the figures have improved and when the transfers made from the SSI are considered, their ratio to the national income has decreased, and when compared to previous years, it has decreased. The numbers are clear, SSI has its figures.
Now, there is a situation here. You are saying this, specifically you are saying: "Whether it is 16 million retirees or 20 million, the share I will allocate from the national income to retirees is certain. I will not give more than this." Likewise, your approach to your own officers and staff is more or less the same, it's really strange. So now, we see that education expenditures have not increased, health expenditures have not increased; earthquake expenditures are decreasing, personnel expenditures are decreasing, investments are decreasing. Something has to increase. There is something increasing here; interest. Look, Mr. Vice President, we have been in the same commissions with you before. Generally, MPs, that is, new term MPs, say the following in their budget speeches: "There is no room for workers in this budget, there is no place for farmers in this budget, there is no place for civil servants in this budget. This budget is the interest budget." Oh, I swear, this budget is really an interest budget. In other words, we will have an interest expenditure of 1 trillion 950 billion liras, its ratio to the budget is 13 percent. This is a record, at least there has been no such figure in the last ten years. Its share in the gross domestic product is 3.2 percent. In other words, when we look at these figures, we have been unfair to the previous AKP and Justice and Development Party budgets. In other words, its ratio to gross domestic product was 1.9 percent in 2016, 1.8 percent in 2017, and today it is 3.2 percent. Today, 17 lira of every 100 lira of tax revenue goes to interest.

Tax revenues, more than 85 percent of total revenues, 17 lira of which goes to interest. You know, how did we get here? You know, they were saying - you probably didn't say it - "Interest is the cause, inflation is the result." So we set out. At this point, we are breaking records in interest payments.
If we look at the revenues, we talked about the expenditures, as I said, a significant part of them consists of tax revenues and 67 percent of them consists of indirect taxes, 67 percent, nothing changes when you look at it. Again, taxes are indirect taxes, 13.4 percent of which is VAT and 19 percent is SCT. Injustice still continues. Well, let's look at direct taxes: 19 percent is income tax - a significant part of which is the tax deducted from the source, from wage earners - and 14.6 percent is corporate tax; 19 percent income tax and 14.6 percent corporate tax. In 2022, the opposite was true; 13.4 percent income tax, 19.4 percent corporate tax. Now suddenly the structure has changed.
Now, I want to ask you a question here: We made legal regulations. Like what did we do? We introduced a concept called "minimum corporate tax" and said: From now on, no one will pay corporate tax below 10 percent. And voila, we increased the tax rates, from 20 to 25, from 25 to 30. We even said: Even though we do not or cannot collect taxes, we increased the tax rate to 30 percent for companies that carry out public-private cooperation projects. Now, even though we remove these, how does the corporate tax rate decrease as a percentage of tax revenues? For example, last year, "1 trillion 314 billion liras of corporate tax will be collected in the 2024 budget." we said; 1 trillion 116 billion lira has been realized, that is 85 percent. There is more realization in all other tax types than the budget, only the realization rate in corporate tax is low. Why can't we collect corporate tax?
Now, to understand your government's or your Minister of Finance's fight against inflation, there is a Nasrettin Hodja joke, let's cheer up a bit. Nasrettin Hodja had a horse that he loved very much, but over time, the hay prices increased a lot and became too much for him, so he said: "Let me reduce the hay I give him by half and see what happens." He reduced it by half, there was no problem for the first week, there was no problem with the horse. He said, "Let me lower it one more time." In the second week, he lowered another half, the horse is standing again, no problem. He dropped another half in the third week. The fourth week: "I won't give it up completely." he said. He started not giving any; Three days later, he opened the barn door and saw the animal lying on the ground. "What a shame, he just got used to hunger, his end has come." he said. Now, unfortunately, there is such a policy that you are scared and scared: "The minimum wage earner gets a little more, the civil servant gets a little more, the retired gets a little more. They take the extra money, spend it like crazy, and then inflation increases." So that's it in a nutshell. So, if you could, you would say, “Let's not give anything, let's not demand anything, let's fight inflation.

"But there is something like this: We look at tax revenues, we foresee an increase of 43 percent, 43 percent, and I just mentioned, 65 percent of these tax revenues are indirect taxes, a significant portion of which is SCT and VAT, that is, the tax collected from expenditures. Now, on the one hand, you say: "Let's reduce expenditures." On the other hand, you say: “Let there be a 43 percent increase in SCT and VAT. In other words, the year-end inflation expectation is 17.5 percent; The most optimistic figure will be around 45 percent by the end of this year. If you look at it accordingly, BAĞ-KUR will give a 16 percent raise to SGK retirees, 11.7 percent to retired civil servants and civil servants; There will be an increase of around 12 percent, and there will be a 25 percent increase in the minimum wage. So, I wonder how you will achieve these figures, SCT and VAT figures without spending.
Of course, there is another dimension to this. Between January and September 2024, the number of bounced checks increased by 85 percent compared to the same period in 2023 and reached 197 thousand; This is a very serious increase. Well, the monetary amount of these bad checks increased by 245 percent; This is a very serious number. The number of bankruptcies is increasing, the number of concordats is increasing, the applications are increasing. In fact, today, a fifty-year-old fabric company, a well-established leader in its sector, has gone bankrupt with a debt of 500 million lira. Bankruptcies are increasing.
There was a research conducted, I said it during the budget negotiations last year; According to research, 13 percent of companies in Turkey are zombies, that is, they can no longer manage themselves, they survive with low-interest loans, and it was said that these companies would go bankrupt if interest rates increased. Now we see that bankruptcies are slowly starting to occur, especially in the textile sector. The industry has come to a standstill, everyone has said it, and we are facing a serious wave of bankruptcies.
Another issue that saddens me and makes me think is individual bankruptcies. Bad credit card debts have increased by 192 percent since the beginning of the year, our citizens are in a very serious debt spiral, especially there is a serious increase in credit card debts, they have started to be unable to pay and this will be a big problem. How do you foresee a 4 percent growth in this situation, when the industry has come to a halt, there are bankruptcies, there are credit card debts, and the number of bounced checks has increased? So this won't be easy either. In other words, it is bad, it is alarming, and even today the IMF has reduced the growth rate to 2.7 percent in its prediction for 2025. Well, you - our other orators also said - prepared this budget based on 4 percent. Therefore, when we combine all these figures, it seems difficult to achieve such growth with this view, and it seems impossible to collect these revenues. Therefore, this budget deficit seems like a very big figure, 1 trillion 931 billion liras, which is really big, and it seems impossible to make it to the end of the year with these figures.
If we look at something else, we need to look at borrowing; The debt stock of the treasury exceeded 8.6 trillion, 8 trillion 649 billion lira. There has been an increase of 1 trillion 926 billion lira since the beginning of the year, and this increase is due to the exchange rate difference of 795 billion lira.

When we look at the structure of the debt stock, 59 percent of it is borrowed in foreign currency, that is, almost 60 percent; There is an increase in 18.6 percent of our domestic debt and all of our external debt in foreign currency. This being the case, when we consider that the foreign exchange rate is going moderate, the exchange rate difference of 795 billion seems to me to be a serious figure. It is said that the foreign exchange rate is below where it should be, that is, it should be even more, it is said that the current figure of 34 is due to the hot money coming from abroad, and it is higher than it should normally be. Well, if we face a possible foreign exchange attack tomorrow or the day after, we will face a very serious exchange rate difference. The interest burden is indeed increasing significantly. All the previous speakers mentioned this, and you also mentioned this: “Yes, our debt is increasing, our debt stock is increasing, but among the criteria, we are in a better situation when compared to European countries.” Yes, the numbers tell us this, but there is a difference: In other words, the debt of the United States is 122 percent of its gross domestic product, but it borrows in its own currency and its debt rate is 4.2 percent; Germany also has a debt ratio of 62 percent of its gross domestic product, but it borrows in its own currency and borrows at 2.2; France also borrows at 3 percent and these are long-term debts. When we look at our structure, unfortunately we do not borrow in our own currency and we borrow at 45 percent, and the maturity of this...
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Mr. Bakırlıoğlu, please let's wrap up.
AHMET VEHBİ BAKIRLIOĞLU (Manisa) - I'm wrapping it up.
I see that we pay the debt with debt, that is, we have to borrow to pay the debt, to pay the interest; Well, this is not something foreign to us. Our farmers do it too, and people today do it too. What is he doing? To pay off his debt, he goes to pay off one credit card, withdraws money from another credit card, and pays it off. For example, I did it, I just did it today, I withdrew money from another bank, a private bank, to pay my debt to Ziraat Bank, I paid off my debt to Ziraat Bank; We are managing this way and we are on the verge of bankruptcy. Unfortunately, I see a similar situation in our budget, and I am worried about the future.
Despite everything, I hope our budget will be beneficial.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Thank you.
Now, Mr. Mustafa Erdem…
Dear Deputy, your time is twenty minutes. Here you go.
MUSTAFA ERDEM (Antalya) - Mr. President, Mr. Vice President, our valuable Commission members, our deputies, our valuable bureaucrats, our valuable workers of the press; First of all, I greet you all with respect and love.
Before starting my speech, I once again congratulate our Republic Day, the 101st anniversary of which we proudly celebrated yesterday, and on this occasion, I commemorate the Great Leader Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk and his companions with respect, longing and gratitude.
Again, I wish God's mercy to our citizens who lost their lives in the treacherous attack on our TAI recently, and I wish a speedy recovery to our wounded.

Even though it may not seem like it, I would like to start my speech by wishing that our budget negotiations will be beneficial for our country.
Dear MPs, as we say in every budget discussion, budgets give the executive branch of the legislature the authority to collect public revenues and make public expenditures. Beyond its legal nature, the budget sharing here reveals the government's choice, whether it is fair or not, and its social priorities. Unfortunately, the budget before us today does not surprise us. This is the 23rd budget of the AKP government and the 7th budget of the one-man regime. This budget, like the previous ones, is unfortunately a budget far from justice, investment, production and transparency, contrary to what was said in the presentations.
Mr. President, dear friends; The upcoming budget means more than hundreds of pages, figures and graphs. I want to call out to those who made this budget: You stubbornly do not hear the voice of this society. The situation you have brought to this country with your political choices and neoliberal economic policies for twenty-two years, with a system of profit and exploitation, incompetence and corruption, is now causing irreversible social decay and social destruction.
We will discuss this budget in the Parliament of this nation until January. In this picture you have created, the country is now where people laugh and kill babies for profit; Murder, aggression, harassment and rape, lawlessness, mafia and corruption have become ordinary. We have come to this situation as a result of economic policies that reinforce people's feeling of worthlessness, because everyone, from young to old, who are struggling with economic difficulties, cannot see their future in this country, and this state of uncertainty leads to a break from social values. He goes to school, he's hungry; He finishes school and cannot find a job; He works but cannot get paid for his labor; He retires and cannot feed himself; He gets sick and cannot find medicine; cannot receive proper health care. We can give thousands, tens of thousands more examples. While humane living opportunities are being eliminated for the majority of the society, impoverished people are trying to find ways to survive in accordance with the current conditions. The only survival strategy suitable for today's conditions in Türkiye, where poverty has become commonplace among large segments of society, appears to be piggybacking on others, joining gangs, corruption, and finding one's way, even if it is immoral; Consequently, in the face of impoverishment, a tendency to decay also emerges. So, unfortunately, you stole the hope of this society. In short, this budget expresses these beyond the numbers.
“What does the budget bring?” When we look at it, you don't have any suggestions that would change this picture. To understand the presented 2025 budget, it is necessary to look at the medium-term program announced about a month ago. We saw that you revised the inflation target for 2024 from 33 percent to 41 percent. Again, you increased the 2025 inflation target, which was set as 15.2 in the medium-term plan, to 17.5 percent. In addition, "In the Medium Term Plan, the compatibility of minimum wage increases with the disinflation process to prevent the wage-price spiral will continue to be taken into account." With your statements, you are showing wage increases as the cause of inflation.
Again, you have suppressed labor incomes as a result of tight monetary and fiscal policy.

In the Medium Term Plan for the period 2025-2027, the Government declares that it will continue these policies. Unfortunately, the 2025 budget was prepared in line with this spirit. No matter how much you deny it, you received the instruction from the IMF. Friends, look at the IMF Report announced in the past weeks, does it say anything different? You even go and make promises about minimum wage increases to capital groups abroad. Take the money from the citizens, condemn them to starvation; Unfortunately, this is the essence of this budget.
And again, tax, tax, tax. Dear friends, 2025 central government budget expenses are estimated to be 14 trillion 731 billion liras, budget revenues are estimated to be 12 trillion 800 billion liras, while the budget deficit is expected to be 1 trillion 931 billion liras. So, how will it happen? The AKP government, which does not make savings in expenditures, will create the resource with the taxes that will come from the pockets of these people in 2025. This citizen will again pay 12 trillion 651 billion liras in taxes in 2025, but it seems that this will not be enough. In the budget proposal, 45 percent of tax revenues consist of taxes collected from consumption. A total of 5 trillion 724 billion 862 million lira taxes appear as domestic goods and services taxes. 2 trillion 145 billion SCT revenue will enter the treasury coffers next year. This amount increased by 52.2 percent compared to last year. However, the domestic VAT amounting to 2 trillion 884 billion TL, which was increased by 72.7 percent compared to last year, stands out.
Now, dear friends, when you make a presentation, you use fancy words: "We are spreading prosperity." "Tax justice" or something like that. You will give workers a raise based on the 17.5 percent inflation target, you will increase taxes by 70-80 percent, and you will say "I am increasing welfare." you will say. Dear friends, the share of corporate tax in total tax revenues is only 12.78 percent. 1 million 100 thousand corporate taxpayers, including giant banks and companies, will pay only 1.6 trillion taxes. Since the bosses show expenses but do not pay them, most of them will be VAT paid by the workers and it will be 3 times more. Of course, the benefits for capital in the 2025 budget do not end there. Taxes higher than the projected budget deficit will be waived. In 2025, you are giving up a total of 3 trillion 5 billion lira of tax revenue from the capital sector under the name of "tax deduction, exemption, exception", and then you come and try to collect taxes from citizens' credit card limits - although you have withdrawn them for now.
So, where will this budget be spent? It is necessary to say a few sentences about this. A total of 3 trillion 911 thousand lira for personnel expenses, 1 trillion 24 thousand lira for goods and service purchase expenses, 5 trillion 813 million lira for current transfers, 1 trillion 102 billion lira for capital expenses, 338 billion lira for capital transfers, 306 billion lira for lending expenses, 287 billion lira for reserve appropriations, unfortunately 1 trillion 950 billion for interest expenses. lira Our esteemed Vice President says: "As AK PARTY governments, we have always seen education as our top priority." and “We increased the budget of the Ministry of National Education, which we took over as 8 billion lira in 2002, to 1 trillion 450 billion lira in 2025.” Citizens no longer believe in these, dear friends.

Millions of students are longing for a hygienic, safe education environment, clean water and a meal that will fill their stomachs in schools that do not have cleanliness in their buildings or security personnel at their doors. While the share of National Education in the general budget was 13.6 percent in 2016, its share in 2025 did not reach 10 percent. According to the 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal, the Presidency of Religious Affairs budget increased by 41 percent in 2025 compared to the 2024 budget, while the budget of the Ministry of National Education increased by 32.87 percent compared to last year. 1 trillion 30 billion of the budget allocated to National Education goes to personnel expenses, and 131 billion 441 million goes to insurance premium payments. In other words, more than 80 percent of the budget is for goods, services and capital expenses of the Ministry of National Education, and the total remaining budget is 291 billion lira. As a result, there is almost no money left to meet the cleaning and security personnel needs of schools and for the cleaning and stationery materials that schools need. In this country, you either have the parents clean the school or you collect money from the parents.
Dear friends, unfortunately you sacrificed education to savings measures that you did not implement yourself. You published an austerity measure on May 17, it is documented. You took away children's right to food, service and a healthy education. For some reason, dear friends, on May 28, you transferred 5 billion 702 million liras from the budget of the Ministry of National Education to a pro-government foundation. This is called "your savings measures." There is no saving for sects or communities; Unfortunately, it is so, don't fool anyone anymore, the public doesn't believe it either.
I am not talking about health here at all, we will talk about the ministry budget. As a result of your marketeering approach, you have already sacrificed health to the private sector and profit, and given it away to your supporters. The entire responsibility for what these babies, families, and citizens are going through today belongs to you.
While the laborers, retirees, young people and children working in the country are getting poorer day by day and are in need of a morsel of bread, unfortunately, the satisfaction of the government and its pro-contractors with this budget does not decrease. The burden on the budget of payments for public-private partnership projects continues to grow. The appropriation allocated here in 2025 is 202.3 billion lira, and in the next three years, 678.3 billion lira will be paid as a guarantee for these projects from the budget. Unfortunately, your love for these gangs of 5 and 10 does not seem to end in this budget either. I would like to ask Mr. Minister again: You increased the tax to 30 percent within the scope of public-private cooperation. 37 of 44 companies have no tax base, meaning they do not pay taxes. The Ministry of Finance expects 505 million tax revenue from here. Now, is this your justice in taxes? “Spread taxes to the ceiling, not the bottom.” I request.
Again, of course, the budget is open to the palace. Dear MPs, while citizens are crushed by the high cost of living, you are increasing the palace's budget by 37 percent. Presidential budget appropriation is 16 billion 928 million 146 thousand TL. 17 million lira will be paid for the purchase of the vehicle. A total of 20 vehicles, 5 of which will be official vehicles, will be purchased for the Presidency in 2025. According to the New Savings Measures Circular, the purchase of new vehicles in the public sector was restricted for three years. This nation is tired of carrying you on its back.

Neither your palaces nor your convoys nor your planes nor your vehicles are finished. It's a shame indeed.
The appropriation offered to the Presidency of Religious Affairs in its budget for 2024 increased by 41 percent from 91 billion 824 million to 130 billion 119 million. This is a budget that leaves 6 ministries behind. Money to be transferred to foundations, community associations and other ministries are not included in this budget. Will you govern this country with this understanding? Absolutely not. As if it were not enough, the pomp, expenses and extravagance of the Diyanet will unfortunately continue.
Dear MPs, there is also the aspect of paying interest on capital and debt. The interest to be paid in 2025 is 1 trillion 950 billion lira. As you can see, there are more taxes and more interest payments. So, how will it happen, dear friends, how will inflation be reduced? Let me tell you: With this budget, the fire in citizens' homes will grow even bigger, and the poverty caused by the economic situation you have created will deepen even further. According to the forecast of the Ministry of Family and Social Services, the number of children who cannot be cared for by their families is expected to increase to 200 thousand. The Ministry's prediction for the number of people who will benefit from electricity consumption support in 2025, 2026 and 2027 is recorded as 4 million 100 thousand households. Again, you say that the number of people with UHI debt will reach 9.8 million. And these friends are hungry, but unfortunately you are also sending enforcement to these debtors. These are the paintings you created. You are trying to cover up the poverty you have created with social aid and present it as something worthwhile. The important thing is not to increase social aid, but to eliminate or reduce those in need of social aid.
You say that agriculture is still the same, we will increase it according to the target inflation of 17.5 percent. You have vowed to break the farmers' backs and destroy production. As a result, we will enter a new year in which the tax burden, budget deficit, interest, current transfers, and public-private partnership payments increase, but our revenues decrease. This budget is far from being a solution to the problems of civil servants, retirees, low-income people, students, women and farmers. This budget contains plenty of taxes and few salaries. This budget is not the budget of the people, but of a handful of capital and your supporters, and I hope it will be the last budget you prepare on behalf of the people of this country. I respectfully greet all of you.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Dear Mr. Erdem, thank you.
