In this regard, when we look at occupational murders - occupational accidents in almost all fields, they are called "workplace murders" anymore, no one calls them "work accidents" - they have increased significantly in all other fields, from mining to the construction industry. What Turkey needs is a new development program, a model in which Turkey will grow, the economy will grow in a healthy way, investments will increase, employment will increase, exports will increase, but this is not the budget and medium-term program we are discussing that will achieve this, because as I said before, the budget is not designed to correct the already existing, distorted balances, those disturbed balances will be further disrupted in this process.
I just told you how high the tax increases are, and the structure in which the current 65 percent is based on indirect taxes, taxes collected on expenditures such as SCT and VAT, continues. This increases the existing problems in Turkey today.

When we combine this with the institutional collapse in Turkey and the fact that the state administration has moved away from merit today, it greatly increases the problem that arises when we look at it together, and this crisis that Turkey is experiencing today, this economic crisis, social crisis, political crisis, ethics and moral crisis, this is the reason for all of these. Budgets should be put forward to eliminate this. Budgets should change these distributions. Budgets are opportunities to intervene in the economy and social life; How does he do this? As I said, it ensures the growth and development of the country and increases the national income. On the one hand, it distributes the national income more equitably, highlights certain sectors, and does so regionally. When we look at it from the perspective of class balance, the tension between labor and capital changes this existing structure that is increasingly developing against labor. This budget does not change any of these, there is nothing in this budget.
They ask me, in many places where we meet with our voters, when we meet people, our citizens, "Is there anything for me in this budget?" they say. And I say: “There is nothing for you in this budget.” This budget is similar to budgets made in the past. As if we were not in a crisis, as if nothing was happening, we are presented with a budget almost similar to last year's budget. But let's put it this way: Even if you don't change it at all, this economic structure, which increases the existing imbalances, makes those existing imbalances even more seriously intolerable. Therefore, as I said, here are the structural reforms that will actually solve Turkey's problems - we did not see this in the MTP, nor in the budget - and the elimination of Turkey's production and export structure, which is dependent on imports; carrying out a serious tax reform and a spending reform. We said "savings measure" but there is nothing. Solving the problems in the employment market and making the remanufacturing industry effective in Turkey because that manufacturing industry is the locomotive sector. When we look at the technology density, the share of high-tech sectors in total is 3 percent. It was 3 percent when you came to power, and it is 3 percent now. There has been a slight upward trend over the years, mostly in low and medium-low technology sectors. A structure where these are not corrected and eliminated is of no use to Turkey. That's why we are already expressing our opinions here, we will express them, we will express our criticisms and we will also say what should happen. We said it here, we will say it again, and we will say it in these ministry budgets.
I think it is important to clearly state the problems that Turkey is experiencing today and how to solve them, both from a policy perspective and a measures perspective.
Thank you and my respects.

Now, we say it every time, we are talking about this budget right. First of all, I would like to state that: Budgets show the political, economic and class preferences of a government in that country. It is like this everywhere, in every country of the world, and political powers determine in what way and in what proportion they will collect resources from whom, from which social segments, from which classes, with the budgets they make, and they determine to whom and how they will transfer these resources, so they actually declare this. In other words, budgets show who a political power is on the side of, living in that society. For example, "Is it on the side of capital, on the side of workers, laborers, the poor? Is it on the side of conflict, war, armament in the international arena, or is it on the side of peace?" In fact, budgets are documents that determine many issues such as. In this respect, the right of citizens to make a budget has been a very sacred right for centuries. Unfortunately, the system that exists in Turkey today limits citizens' right to budget. We talk and debate about this every time, because the Presidential government system is a system that actually bypasses the Parliament and does not allow the Parliament to prepare the budget, but sees it appropriate to make the budget in the palace; We will continue to criticize this, this system needs to change as it is.
Now, you, as the government, love very much "The situation in Turkey in 2002 was this, this is the situation today." That's why I want to make some evaluations on a few points by looking at at least a period, not from 2002 until today, but a close period, because since you have always prepared a budget with the same mentality, for a long time - I do not mean the first periods, but for a long time now - you have actually turned this country into a country of injustice. It is truly a country of injustice and when we look at those budgets, when we look at the budgets that have come to date, we see that this feature comes to the fore in all of them. Look, I want to touch on some data. What do I mean when I say that you have turned into a country of injustice? In other words, the share of labor in the gross domestic product, which was 33.5 percent in 2018, decreased to 26.3 in 2022, that is, a decrease of approximately 7 points, 7-odd points in five years. On the other hand, when we look at the share of net operating surplus mixed income, that is, the share of capital in national income, we see that there is an increase, that is, we see that these increases continue in 2020, 2021 and 2022. Now, this means that this gap has grown, the difference has grown, that is, the share of capital has increased and the share of labor has decreased. What does this mean? Your governments and the budgets you prepared have increased the inequality between labor and capital; This is clearly seen from the numbers, and you are aware of it. This is actually a choice, a political and economic choice.

You know, it doesn't naturally develop like this, this inequality emerges as a result of these choices. This is the first point I want to point out, when we look at the budgets you prepared for the last six years, this is the seventh year.

So, as I said before, nothing has changed in the preferences, this budget still does not show a preference in favor of labor, and even when we look at the decrease in the expenditure item, it is obvious that the wage increases of public employees will be quite low in 2025. “Where is it cut from?” When we look at it, we see that it is cut from here. When we look at investments, we see that there is a very serious decrease in public investments. In other words, 2025 will be an extremely difficult year both in terms of public investments and the wages of public employees and public workers; First of all, it needs to be determined. So your budget preferences are in favor of capital in this budget as well.
The speaker just stated that tax expenditures, which mainly consist of tax exemptions and exemptions granted to capital, are determined as approximately 3 trillion in the 2025 budget, which is truly an incredible figure. We also discussed this for the 2024 budget; If you remember, when the savings measures and tax issues came, when these packages came, we discussed them and pointed out that this was an extremely serious problem. The resources allocated to tax expenditures continue to increase in this budget, we do not see a decrease. In other words, it was approximately 2 trillion 200 billion liras in the 2024 budget, and now it has reached approximately 3 trillion liras in 2025. So, there is no change in preferences, that's what I want to say.
Let's look at it in terms of taxes. So basically let's look at these preferences. We see something very clearly here. There is no decrease in indirect taxes. Look, the income from VAT and SCT is extremely high.

The income from VAT constitutes almost 32 percent of the total income, which means that indirect taxes do not decrease, they continue to increase. What happens, who pays an indirect tax? Here, workers, laborers and all segments of society pay VAT and SCT. Therefore, this means that citizens will continue to be robbed through indirect taxes - sorry for using this term. We discuss the injustice in taxes every time, we continue to argue. You also know that no steps have been taken to ensure justice in taxes.
Mr. Yılmaz, we have seen from your statement recently, you were not here, I said it behind your back, but let me say it now, you said at that time: "We do not consider taxes on crypto assets and the stock market." We always say that this is very wrong, that is, if justice is to be achieved in taxes, taxes should be collected from those who earn income above a certain rate, both in crypto assets and in the stock market - exchange rate protected deposits are now out of date, we have said it many times, but you did not listen - so this means unfair gain. Taxes are deducted from the workers' wages at the source, but those who earn high profits in crypto assets and the stock market - I am not targeting small investors or those who earn low profits - but this is not a correct situation. We are talking about a country where there is such serious injustice in taxation. You know, “We will collect more taxes from those who earn more.” says the Minister of Treasury and Finance every time, but we do not see that more taxes are collected from those who earn more, and we believe that this continues to create a serious problem.
Now, of course, we can discuss this at length, not today, we will discuss it in the next ministries, but we see that a very significant part of the resources of this budget, this country's resources, are allocated under the name of "defense and security policies", this is essentially a serious problem. In other words, last year it was around 40 billion dollars. When we add it up, the expenditures on defense, armament and security policies correspond to approximately 47 billion dollars this year; This is a very high rate. We see in every budget that this is not evaluated in any way and we continue to talk, we think this is wrong.
The interest budget, we discuss this once again in every budget, now it has appeared once again. The figure for interest payments for 2025 is almost 1.5 trillion - if I remember correctly - which is a very high figure, that is, it corresponds to a rate of 13 percent for this year in 2025. I think it would be around 11-12 percent in 2024, a figure in that range. It seems to have increased a little now, but this is a rate that shows that it is not unfair to call it the "interest budget", and in fact, we are faced with a government that is a friend of the interest lobbies in this regard, let's take note of this as well.

Now, there is a situation like this: So, when we look at this budget from these perspectives, as I said, we will talk about the ministries one by one when the day comes, of course, but I would like to mention two very glaring places: One of them is the field in which you work, the budget of the Presidency is really incredibly high, that is, the budget of the Presidency and the budget allocated to the Parliament are almost the same, when we look at it numerically, they are very close.
İSMAİL GÜNEŞ (Uşak) - There are current transfers.
HAKKI SARUHAN OLUÇ (Antalya) - Yes, now, we will discuss that when the time comes, but this shows that: In fact, the importance given to the legislative body emerges once again from this perspective. On the other hand, when we look at the Presidency, palaces and all expenses, it has expenses that reach very high amounts every day, and I also exclude the covert appropriation. When we look at it from this perspective, we believe that there is a serious inequality in this budget.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Dear Mr. Oluç, I am extending your time.
HAKKI SARUHAN OLUÇ (Antalya) - Thank you, Mr. President.
In other words, we criticize the Presidential government system, we will talk about it, but we believe that it is an extremely wrong choice for the Presidential government system to use such large resources, so we want to express our criticism on this issue as well.
So, we are faced with such a situation that today, there is a crisis in the economy and this country is trying to overcome this crisis, and of course, the bill falls on those who live on the wage of this society, those who live on their labor, laborers, workers, farmers and tradesmen. So how did we get caught in such a big crisis? You are looking for something in international relations, you are looking for something in the pandemic, etc. Okay, well, all of these may have an effect to some extent, but you know, and we know, that this is not the main reason. Therefore, from this perspective, let's say in advance that the year 2025 will be a year in which those who earn their living with their labor and wages in Turkey will experience great difficulties, both when we look at it in terms of wage income and also when we look at it in terms of unemployment due to the results of economic policies; It will be a year in which unemployment will increase. Since you do not evaluate broadly defined unemployment, you are trying to present it as if there is a decrease in unemployment, but when we look at broadly defined unemployment, we see that this is not the case.
Now, there is one last issue I want to touch upon.

Of course, we will talk about this in more detail when the relevant ministries come, but I think there is a need to briefly touch upon it here - and that is - I would like to briefly touch upon the issue in terms of international developments and especially regional policy: Now, of course, very serious economic problems are experienced in the world from time to time, and we see the consequences of these. But especially when we look at recent years, when we look at the years after the 2000s and the last seven or eight years, we see that, as in every century, there is once again a great struggle over trade and energy lines in the world. Actually, I say struggle, wars are taking place, that is, people are dying and all this is happening on trade and energy lines. So, on the one hand, we are watching what is said and done at the G7 and NATO summits; On the one hand, we see the decisions taken at the G20 summits; On the other hand, we see the decisions taken by China and the surrounding countries. But when we look at the result - I think - it was September 2023, the G20 meeting was held there with the main theme of "One World, One Family, One Future". At that G20 meeting, an IMEC - we can call it IMEC when we read it in Turkish - India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor was created and the plans for this were discussed, talked about and its establishment was announced. Unfortunately, Türkiye actually remained out of this due to wrong foreign policies.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)

Now, when we look at this IMEC, we are talking about a line that starts from India, passes through the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia, continues from Israel, enters the Eastern Mediterranean basin, and reaches Greece from the south of Cyprus; It is actually an extremely important trade and energy line. When we look at the conflicts and wars in the Middle East today, we see once again how important and critical this trade and energy line is, especially at a time when Israel continues its genocidal policies. Actually, Netanyahu talked about this line in his speech at the United Nations, he showed two maps, one is a map of curse and the other is a map of blessing. This line is essentially what he is trying to explain in the blessing map. Of course, the expansion of the war in the region, now the attacks on Lebanon and Iran continue, Israeli attacks on Syria continue; When we look at all this, it is clear that the region is going through an extremely critical period. Why am I saying these now? In fact, when we look at the first quarter of the 2000s, we saw that these discussions were being held internationally since the 2010s.

In terms of Turkey being both a regional power in economic, social, political and cultural terms and a country of peace and creating a model of peace; We truly think that it is of great importance for it to be a model country in terms of building democracy among countries with different identities, cultures and beliefs in the Middle East and to spend its efforts, opportunities and resources in this regard. We hope that in the coming days, steps will be taken internationally within the framework of this awareness and Turkey can truly become a country that not only ensures social peace within itself but also serves as an important peacekeeper and peace model in its region. Otherwise, we are in a period and region where the interests of global powers are colliding with each other. MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - I do not make any comments regarding the views expressed by the esteemed MPs, but in your speech you made a statement such as "economic distribution in the Kurdish geography" or "the policies implemented are more severe". Regarding this, Mr. Oluç, it is stated in the 3rd article of the Constitution that the state is an indivisible whole with its territory and nation. Therefore, there is no other definition in our laws. In this sense, I request all MPs to show the necessary sensitivity in their speeches in line with this provision of the Constitution.
Here you go.

Now, when you make that correction and deflate it with American inflation, it is actually calculated that the per capita income, which you said was 12,594 dollars in 2013 - I am talking about the Turkish average - decreased to 10,139 dollars in 2023, we see that when we take this with 2013 prices. In other words, since the value of 100 dollars of that day was not the same as 100 dollars of today, when we eliminate that inflation, there is actually something 20 percent below 2013, a little more below, there is a total loss. When we look at this by slices, in fact, the loss in the first slice is 20 percent in dollars; In the last decade, the income of people in the first bracket in real dollar terms has decreased by 20 percent, and there is no middle pole left. The average increase in the 2nd, 3rd and 4th segments, which we call the "middle pillar", is 24 percent; When we look at it in real terms, the increase in the top bracket is less than these, at 14.7 percent.
Now, we actually see this and similar things in the Gini coefficient; This is again from the Turkish Statistical Institute, and their sources are written below as much as possible. Gini coefficient, when this bar grows upwards, injustice increases, when it goes downwards, as you know, injustice decreases and inequality in income distribution. 2007; There is 2006, 2006 is the "outlier" in these statistics, that is, there are radical changes in all of them compared to 2007. Since it is the first year, we do not include it, even if you do, it does not change anything. Now, look, after 2007, when we look roughly until 2014, the Gini coefficient decreased, that is, there were some issues in Turkey regarding a more equitable distribution of income, but after 2014, there was no such thing as a fair distribution of income and it became increasingly unfair. Currently, the Gini coefficient at the current level is above the Gini coefficient of the year when this year started, that is, it is much more unfair and unfortunately, this injustice is increasing as an upward trend. See if this gets better from now on?
Now, this is the distribution of income. In fact, let's say that there is injustice in the distribution of two types of income, or rather, there is injustice in the financial situation of the citizens. First, you distribute the income more unfairly; Secondly, you miss your own income policy or justice based on citizens' expenses, and act more unfairly. Therefore, this is actually why people are crying out, I don't know how aware you are.
Now, when we look at the equivalent household disposable individual income distribution, you actually see the same thing.

Turkish Statistical Institute says: The share of retirees' income is worse than 2006, when this year started. It has fallen from 16.9 to 15.6, you should hear the cry of retirees. Because of the recent policies implemented. Orhan Bey is not here, but I will not spare time for him. But it does this with a current account deficit of only 0.6 of its national income, that is, by using foreign resources, that is, by using savings. Now, what to grow? You need financing. What is financing? It is savings. If your own savings are not enough, you use foreign savings, that is, if you use foreign savings well, you will do good things. I cannot say that the current account deficit is absolutely bad. Current account deficit occurs when that resource is not used well. If you use that resource to make cars, it's bad; You use it in housing construction, it is bad; If it leads to corruption, shamelessness and theft, it is bad, but if you use it to build factories or develop technology, it is good, it pays off many times over.
Now, look, during your period, the growth rate increased from 4 to 5.4, but when we look at it with developing countries, our performance is bad, but when we look at it on its own, yes, but while your predecessors were doing this with a current account deficit of 0.6, you did this with a current account deficit of 3.8, and this is the basis of today's problems. We have a chart called "Periods in which the current account deficit decreases and increases". Just growth. The story of the current account deficit in Turkey is this, friends: In periods when growth decreases, Turkey contracts or growth slows down, our current account deficit decreases. Other things are needed to transform this structurally. You need to transform your industry, you need to transform your agriculture. We've had these discussions here before. When it had a current account surplus for three months in 2021, the ministers said in unison that Türkiye would have a current account surplus. Right after that, Türkiye had the highest current account deficit in its history, and I said this. Well, I said three months, let alone fourteen months, there were periods when Turkey had a current account surplus, but since you did not make a structural transformation, when you start growing again and your economy enters a growth trend, your current account deficit increases. Now, here it is. This is my current story, but my friends must be very confident that - of course, we did not have the opportunity to review it on an annual basis - they no longer included the current account deficit issue in the macroeconomic objective of the 2005 program on page 81, that is, Türkiye corrected the current account deficit issue. Our friends did not include the issue of correcting the current account deficit in the macroeconomic purpose of the program. I am very sorry about this. Politicians say, "We are running a current account surplus, just like that." But if the bureaucrat also believes this, then woe to us. This would be a very sad thing.

There is nothing structural about the current improvement in Turkey's current account deficit. Oh, does it make us comfortable in terms of finance? That is a separate “case”, but you need to do something else to permanently reduce the current account deficit.

It is not right to look at the fundamental issues of our country from a daily or party affiliation perspective. We should all be able to act together on the principle that is right for the country and the nation.
Now, with your permission, I would like to start with a sentence about the budget. Reliability and transparency Dear Vice President, I mean this: We personally have very close relations with Mr. Cevdet Yılmaz or many of our friends here, we do not have a personal trust problem with anyone, but we are talking about a budget, in fact we are talking about a financial basis. I mean this country. In your presentation, you can include retirees, young, old, working people; You said many things and said that the target of the budget is their welfare and happiness. Now, in order to achieve such happiness, we must first discuss the reliability and transparency of the budget that we built on numbers; I think it's something we need to talk about a lot. I will say two things about this.
Reliability. First of all, what did we build the figures on? For example, regarding inflation, we would really like this, that is, after the elections in 2023, we would like the discussions about TURKSTAT to end. I hope we will talk about this with our Minister of Treasury and Finance. Well, open this inflation basket, let's put an end to these discussions, let's finish the double-headed inflation explanations. What's in this basket? How are these calculated?

How do those figures and prices found by TURKSTAT come about? Let people see this. Of course, we do this based on the figures announced by the state, but in a country where inflation and inflation figures are a matter of debate in terms of reliability, when we make a budget and base the interest on inflation figures, it is not possible for any of the figures in this budget to be realized.
The second issue of trust, Mr. Vice President - I expressed this to our Central Bank Governor - we need to put this issue of transparency on the back burner. Now, 2018-2021; As the opposition, we all talked about the 128 billion dollars, "What happened to 128 billion dollars, to whom did we sell it?" like. Well, now calculations are being made, it is said that this figure exceeds 400 billion dollars. Now, this is 400 billion dollars. If such a figure exists, it needs to be seen. Yes, due to exchange rate pass-through, let's take it from the beginning of the year, let's see how much the foreign exchange increased, let's look at inflation; There is something strange about this. Now, our Central Bank cannot provide transparency on this issue. Therefore, in an environment where we do not know who buys what and sells what in the exchange rate management of the Central Bank, in an environment where inflation figures are followed by the society and the people who follow this business, there is a very serious problem with the numerical reliability and transparency of this budget, because we will talk about its performance in the end, I will go into the figures in more detail a little later.
Of course, another important issue here, that is, the second dimension of reliability, in my opinion, is this: It was necessary to confront the nation, Mr. Vice President. Believe me, I am not saying this just as a party member, because if you remember our President, "What did you want that we did not give?" When he says, that's "FETO terrorist organization." What they said was that we lived through July 15 after that statement, but it came out, it was made good with the nation and the nation accepted it. Currently, this country, elected with 52 percent, has a President and a head of executive power. So what do I mean by saying goodbye?
Not only starting from 2018, but in 2021, we are talking about how we dragged this country into a financial collapse with those wrong steps we took during the periods when inflation and interest rates were at 18-20 percent. In fact, we are talking about the price of five or six years in the whole picture we have right now. Believe me, someone should have come forward and apologized. I would like to thank Mr. Mehmet Şimşek - I have told him many times here - that is, "It is necessary to return to rational policies." By saying this, a very serious criticism was actually made about the past. Therefore, if an apology and a confrontation had been made with the society on this issue, believe me, this budget would have been questioned, accepted by the nation and its credibility would have been much higher.
Now, of course, thank God we did not go to war, we did not experience any problems, but we have a worse budget picture than if we went to war. Now, I will tell you a little later, you already know some things numerically. Now, the current economic management has been really successful by using financial tools in tight monetary policy. So we have seen an improvement in the data on many issues. What happened? In other words, what happened in production, investment and employment?

There is a point of view on this issue: I think that a mistake has been made in the calculations made based on the gross national product in Turkey. This is something that is now also in the literature: We are growing, but how are we growing and who are we growing? How do we grow and who do we grow? What kind of growth is this? Is growth a fair distribution, a growth that ensures equitable distribution, income justice? To be honest, now is the time for us to ask these questions. Yes, monetary tightening has been made, what is being done in terms of fiscal policy, you have expressed them, we will probably listen to you and then our Minister of Treasury and Finance, but now our question is: How many factories did we open, how much employment did we provide? These are things we will talk about further.
Now, there is a World Economic Outlook Report recently announced by the OECD regarding this growth. Now, our Turkey grew in the top 3-5 places in the world for years, sometimes we even became champions in growth, and there were periods when we were truly a leader ahead of China. Now, in these announced figures, the world average is 3.2; The OECD brought us down to 3.2. In other words, the OECD is doing something like this: It is pulling down our growth, it is pushing the world average up because interest rates are falling in the world, there will be an expansion, there will be an increase in the world trade volume. Therefore, he talks about an increase and a growth in world trade, but when it comes to Turkey regarding the same growth, he says: "I am reducing Turkey's growth to 3.2 percent."
Of course, when we look at inflation in G20 economies, there is a very different picture. Again, it is shown as 3.3 percent in the OECD report and Turkey's inflation has been brought down to over 29 percent. We will talk more about this issue, the issue of inflation, in the budget of the Ministry of Treasury and Finance. As you know, we started with 33, then we put a band called 36-42, maybe there will be a realization between 42-44 this year, we will take your guess on that issue, but right now, medium-term programs are of course constantly revised, the latest is 17.5 percent. Now, there is an inflation forecast of 29.1 percent. Regarding the OECD's Türkiye, will we hear this from you for 2025? “17 and a half percent plus 5 percent, plus 10 percent.” So will we have such a margin inflation figure? Of course, in an environment where our President is so serious about geopolitical risks and threats to the country in the Grand National Assembly of Turkey, here is what we will do about what the price of a barrel of oil will be, whether it will rise above 100 dollars or not, and how this will affect Turkey; Of course, this is a separate issue. Therefore, measures are needed in this regard as well.
Now, “We have been growing continuously for 14 periods.” We said so much so that sometimes we became - as I just stated - one of the fastest growing economies in the world. Yes, we went down from 19th to 17th, you stated this in your presentation.
Now, speaking specifically about income distribution, I have the following objection to you in your presentation regarding purchasing power statistics: In other words, regarding electricity and natural gas prices, "We are the country that provides them cheapest in Europe." you said.

There was a similar statement in your presentation last year, it was a statement you took from EUROSTAT data and shared. Now, when we look at the purchasing power statistics of Turkey, we have a purchasing power problem that is 3rd and 4th from the bottom among 27 countries in Europe, that is, it cannot be compared with Europe, that is, it cannot be compared with Germany or France.
Therefore, I believe that we need to rethink the issue of who this growth causes and what kind of obese growth it causes. As I have just stated, in countries like ours in the world, where the rent and interest economy is ahead of the production and investment economy and is reflected in the budgets, there is now talk of an impoverishing growth concept. In the last seven years in Turkey, everyone should look at the budget and ask, "How is Türkiye really growing and who is it growing?" If he asks the question, Türkiye, unfortunately, has become an economy where a significant part of the society, the large masses of people, are impoverished and a very small minority thrives. In such a country, we cannot look at any data in terms of gross national product and say, "We are doing well." It wouldn't be the right approach to say that.
Look, we have a contraction issue here in the industrial and manufacturing sectors. Now, in June, July and August, our industrial sector shrank by almost 5 percent, and has there ever been a period in the history of the Republic of Turkey - we have financial advisor friends among us - when bankruptcies, concordats and enforcements were so intense?
Dear Vice President, how will we achieve this 4 percent growth, how will this 4 percent growth happen, while the industry is contracting so much - the OECD predicts 3.2 percent in 2025, we say 4 percent? If there is really going to be growth on the real economy side, we don't need to talk about these, hopefully these will happen.
There is something I noticed in your general presentations: You speak highly of the capacity to create employment. Now, “It is a success that the number of people employed is actually increasing every year, but is this enough?” TURKSTAT's latest answer to the question "We cannot answer this without looking at the idle workforce." He said and 27.2 percent of the working population in Turkey is currently not working, and our labor force participation rate is 54.3 percent. Now, Mr. Vice President, the labor force participation rate in Europe is around 75 percent, the EU average. Come on, we passed these; We look at Saudi Arabia, Mexico, these countries; We look at Russia and Brazil; The labor force participation rate in these is over 60 percent, so I want to say this: Yes, we ensure participation in employment, but when we take into account the increasing population in Turkey, we have a very high unemployment rate in terms of our employment rate and the issue of idle labor force, because, as you know, people are no longer considered unemployed in Turkey if they do not look for a job for a few weeks.
So why did I bring this here?

Again, when we look at the budget data, let's be clear, of course states are not going bankrupt, but if it continues like this, that is, if a Turkey that cannot increase the employment participation rate, grow the real economy, grow production and investment thinks that it grows in this way with financial instruments, interest and rent, and cannot increase the number of working people, the Social Security Institution will collapse in Turkey, God forbid, this will make Turkey pay a very heavy price. The numbers we currently allocate to the Social Security Institution in the budget are reaching appalling proportions.

MUSTAFA KALAYCI (Konya) - Mr. President, esteemed Commission members, Mr. Vice President, esteemed representatives of our public institutions and organizations, non-governmental organizations and the press; First of all, I would like to greet you all with respect and thank those who contributed to the preparation of the budget.
Our Republic is 101 years old. The Republic of Türkiye is a legacy that will last forever, a sacred trust that will be defended under all circumstances, of a just cause and a nationalist consciousness that does not bow to oppression. The Turkish nation and the Republic of Türkiye will exist as long as the world continues.
I once again celebrate the 101st anniversary of our Republic, and commemorate the founding heroes, especially the founder of our state, Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, our beloved martyrs and our ancestors, with mercy, gratitude and gratitude.
Mr. President, dear MPs; The world economy has recently been going through a difficult process due to pandemics, war, geopolitical risks and global crises. The post-pandemic recovery process of the global economy was disrupted due to the Russia-Ukraine war that started in 2022, trade disputes, intensified political struggles and inflationary pressures, and the massacre that turned into a genocide initiated by Israel in October 2023 further increased the tension and geopolitical risks in our region. While the world economy grew by 3.3 percent in 2023, the world trade volume increased by 8 percent. The world economy will grow by 3.2 percent in 2024; It is expected to maintain moderate growth at 3.3 percent in 2025.
In addition to the slowdown in the global economy, despite the costs imposed on the economy by the earthquakes experienced in our country in February 2023 and the elections that caused uncertainty, the Turkish economy became the fastest growing country among the OECD countries with a growth of 5.1 percent in 2023.

Growth has been positive on an annual basis for fourteen years. Our national income exceeded 1 trillion dollars for the first time in 2023, reaching 1 trillion 130 billion dollars, and our per capita national income increased to 13,243 dollars. Employment increased by 880 thousand people in 2023, reaching 31 million 632 thousand, and the unemployment rate decreased by 1 point to 9.4 percent. Our exports grew by 6 per thousand in 2023 and reached 255.6 billion dollars. Turkey's share in global goods exports increased to 1.07 percent in 2023 and reached its historical highest level. Annual inflation was 64.8 percent in 2023.
In May 2023, Turkey ended the expansionary monetary policy that it had been implementing since the third quarter of 2021 and joined the global trend and switched to a tight monetary policy to prevent rising inflation and ensure price stability. The interest rate, which was 8.5 percent in the middle of 2023, was first increased to 15 percent and then to 50 percent with gradual increases. Turkish economy will grow by 5.7 percent in the first quarter of 2024; In the second quarter, it grew by 2.5 percent annually, thus growth in the first half of the year was 3.8 percent. The contribution of private consumption expenditures to growth continued to decrease in the second quarter of the year, reaching 1.2 points, the lowest level in the last four years. In the same period, the net export item continued its positive contribution to growth with 1.3 points into the second quarter.
Machinery and equipment investments, which have been growing continuously for eighteen quarters, that is, four and a half years, contracted by 5.6 percent in the second quarter of 2024. Industrial production was 5.3 on an annual basis in August; It decreased by 1.6 percent on a monthly basis. The manufacturing industry capacity utilization rate was 74.9 percent in October. Manufacturing Industry Purchasing Managers' Index decreased to 44.3 in September, and a monthly slowdown was recorded in production for the 6th consecutive time. Although the contraction in industrial production shows the effects of tight monetary policy on production, these effects are estimated to be temporary. As a matter of fact, the seasonally adjusted Real Sector Confidence Index increased by 3 points compared to the previous month in October and reached 102.2, thus exceeding 100 after four months and shifting to an optimistic outlook. It is observed that the trend in favor of those reporting a decrease in production volume also turns in favor of those reporting an increase. Additionally, the Consumer Confidence Index increased by 3 percent in October and reached 80.6. Consumers' financial situation and general economic situation expectations have improved significantly.
The economic program is being implemented decisively and macro-financial indicators are improving significantly. The disinflation process continues, growth has started to stabilize, the current account deficit has narrowed, our country's risk premium has decreased, its credit rating has increased, its outlook has turned positive, the gray list has been removed, external resource inflows have increased, and the Central Bank reserves have improved.
Annual inflation decreased to 49.38 percent as of September. However, inflation and the high cost of living rightfully disturb all of our people. Our main duty is to eliminate complaints on this issue.

As of the second quarter of 2024, employment increased by 1 million 189 thousand people compared to the same period of the previous year and by 205 thousand people compared to the first quarter, and the unemployment rate decreased to 8.4 percent, the lowest level of the last twelve years. Erhan Bey is gone. Although Erhan Bey said, “Nobody should talk about the increase in employment.” he said, but I'm talking specifically about it. As of August, 1 million 100 thousand additional jobs were provided annually, and the unemployment rate decreased by 0.7 points compared to the same month of the previous year, falling to 8.5 percent.
Yes, the issue of unemployment is constantly brought up. It is obvious that there is an increase in employment, the data shows this. Now, when we look at the statistics, employment dropped to 26 million during the pandemic period, so to speak, but today it is 32 million. In other words, there is an increase of 6 million jobs in four years. I don't know if I want to ignore this, I leave the interpretation to you. Not only that, the labor force participation rate increased to a historical level, 54.3 percent, that is, labor force participation increased.
When we look at it, yes, there is an increase in the idle workforce rate. But when we look at its details, we see this: There is a decrease in those who are not normally included in the workforce. When we make a one-year comparison, there is a decrease of around 500 thousand people who are not included in the labor force. There is a significant increase in the number of those who have no hope of finding a job, whom we call "potential workforce", those who can do a job but are not looking for a job, and those who look for a job but cannot start working. In itself, there is also an increase in the idle workforce rate, as there is an increase in the number of people entering the workforce who were not included in the workforce before. Surely that's okay, right? Problem. But I will also say that this is not a new calculation, that is, the data of the Turkish Statistical Institute has been making these distinctions in this way for many years and in accordance with the standards in Europe. Now, those who can start work but are not looking for a job. This is debatable; Is he unemployed or not? Yes, he is a potential workforce, but he is not looking for a job, he is able to do it. For this reason, it is not right to only highlight the increase in the idle labor rate and, on the other hand, overshadow the success achieved in increasing employment. In other words, there is both a very significant increase in employment and a high increase in the labor force participation rate.
Another issue was brought to the agenda: "The share of these labor payments in the gross value added is constantly declining." It was said. In fact, an invoice was even issued to the Presidential government system on this issue. I have a series in front of me since 2009. The share of labor payments is increasing every year. Look, while it was 26.7 percent in 2009, it increased to 27 percent, 28 percent, 29 percent in the following years; It increased to 32 percent in 2016, 30 percent in 2017, 30 percent in 2018, 31 percent in 2019; Then there is a decrease, it decreased to 26 percent in 2021 and to 23 percent in 2022. We went through a pandemic period, that period definitely had a significant impact. In 2023, the labor force share increased to 32.8 percent. Yes, the EYT regulation was made in 2023, severance pay payments definitely have an impact, but when we look at this year's data - both the first quarter and the second quarter of this year - the share of labor payments increased to 42 percent in the first quarter and 40.8 percent in the second quarter, that is, it reached a historical level. So, is EYT the issue here this year?

No, so there is no severance pay payment. Here, we actually see an indicator of employment growth. In other words, the increase in employment also provides an increase in labor payments.
Yes, another issue is poverty. Yes, I agree, there is a deterioration in the inequality in income distribution, but when we look at poverty rates, we see that there is a decrease in TÜİK's relative poverty rate, material or social poverty rate, permanent poverty rate and the rate of those at risk of poverty or social exclusion. There is 2023 data.
Additionally, if you look at the World Bank's website, it has its latest assessment dated October 25, 2024 regarding the Turkish economy. The exact statement he used there is as follows: "The poverty rate, which was over 20 percent in 2007, decreased to 7.6 in 2021." says and 7 for 2022; It predicts a poverty rate of 6.6 for 2023. How does he calculate this? His calculation based on a daily income of $6.85 does this for the entire world. As you know, Turkey is a country listed in the upper middle income group by the World Bank, and we are close to being among the high income countries. Hopefully, we will transition to that this year or next year. When we look at other rates, we see that the rate in Turkey is gradually decreasing, including the World Bank's figures of 3.65 dollars per day or 2.15 dollars per day.
Yes, my dear friends, while exports increased by 3.2 percent in the nine months of this year, imports decreased by 7.9 percent. As of September, our annual exports increased by 3.4 percent to 261.6 billion dollars, and our annual imports decreased by 7.3 percent to 340.3 billion dollars. Foreign trade deficit increased by 31.5 percent to 27.6 billion dollars in the nine-month period of this year; The annual decrease was 31 percent, by 35.3 billion dollars. The current account deficit decreased by 75.2 percent by 29.3 billion dollars in the January-August period. As of August, the annualized current account deficit decreased by 40.6 billion dollars, at a rate of 78.3 percent, to 11.3 billion dollars.
Our tourism revenues increased by 9.3 percent in the six-month period of 2024, reaching 23.7 billion dollars. The Central Bank's gross reserves reached a new historical record level of 159.4 billion dollars. Turkey's EU-defined general government debt ratio decreased to 26.1 percent in the second quarter of 2024. As it is known, the Maastricht Criteria is 60 percent, and this rate is 86 percent on average in EU member countries. In our country, household debts increased from 12.6 percent to 10.2 percent compared to the previous year, debts of non-financial companies increased from 55.6 percent to 42.8 percent, public debts increased from 35.6 percent to 29.2 percent, debts belonging to the financial sector increased from 21.2 percent to 21.2 percent. It decreased to 16.7. In other words, Türkiye is among the least indebted countries in terms of public indebtedness, real sector indebtedness and household indebtedness.
The capital adequacy ratio of the banking sector is at 17 percent and it has a solid structure. The banking sector's non-performing loans ratio is 4.14 percent in 2020; 3.67 percent in 2021; 2.38 in 2022; While it was at 1.61 percent in 2023, it is at 1.69 percent as of August this year.

Mr. President, dear MPs; In 2023, the budget deficit was realized as 1 trillion 380 billion liras and the ratio of the budget deficit to the gross domestic product was 5.2 percent. 960 billion liras were spent in 2023 for the reconstruction and reconstruction of the earthquake area and the rapid elimination of the effects of the earthquake. Excluding earthquake expenses, the ratio of budget deficit to gross domestic product is 1.6 percent. The ratio of budget deficit to gross domestic product in 2024 is projected to be 4.9 percent. It is estimated that this rate will be 2.5 percent, excluding earthquake expenses. An allowance of 1 trillion 28 billion liras has been allocated in 2024 for the rapid elimination of earthquake damages and the needs of our citizens living in the region. While budget expenditures for 2024 were predicted to be 26.9 percent of the gross domestic product, this rate is expected to be 25.4 percent. This indicates that significant savings have been achieved in public expenditures thanks to the measures taken.
In the 2025 budget, expenses are 14 trillion 731 billion lira, revenues are 12 trillion 800 billion lira, and the ratio of the budget deficit to gross domestic product is expected to be 3.1 percent, excluding earthquake expenses, this rate will be 2.2 percent. An appropriation of 584 billion liras is foreseen in 2025 to improve the earthquake zone and increase resilience against disasters.
Mr. President, dear MPs; Türkiye has reached a level where its economic breakthroughs can be achieved in political stability until 2028. This period, in which separatist terrorism is ended and national unity and brotherhood are strengthened, should be a period in which efforts are made to achieve the goals of the Turkish and Turkish Century. Public resources should be used to create production structures that are globally competitive, have high R&D and innovation capabilities, have a strong capital structure, and can benefit from economies of scale to the maximum extent. An industry that carries out Digital and Green Transformation in a healthy way and has international competitiveness should be rapidly created. The ability and capacity to produce science and technology should be increased, and this should be transformed into development and social welfare. In this context, we consider and support the HIT-30 High Technology Investment Program, introduced by our President in August, as very important. With the HIT-30 High Technology Investment Program, 30 billion dollars of resources, including grants, taxes and market development supports, will be directed to high technology areas. Türkiye aims to become a global center in high technology production by 2030 with the HIT-30 Program.
With its national technology move, Türkiye has reached a point where it can develop and produce advanced technologies with its own means and capabilities. It should be aimed to fully mobilize the domestic and national production potential, further strengthen the giant breakthroughs in the defense industry, further increase the domestic contribution to energy, and obtain the advanced technology captured in the defense industry in other areas.

According to the 2024 Global Innovation Index announced in September, Türkiye achieved a significant success by rising to the 3rd place in the upper middle income group. In the general rankings, it ranked 37th, rising 2 places compared to 2023. Türkiye ranked 16th in the "creative outputs" category. It has risen to the first place worldwide in the field of trademarks and industrial designs. This situation shows that Turkey has successfully commercialized intellectual assets.
Undoubtedly, the production economy based on technological development, innovation, efficiency and employment should be expanded, and Turkey should be among the leading determinants of regional and global developments by integrating with the world economy in an open and competitive structure.

If Turkey gains a stronger economic structure, strengthens its social fabric, reinforces the culture of brotherhood and solidarity and mobilizes its means and capabilities in line with the goal of a greater Turkey, it will go beyond being a regional power and become a global power. Achieving this and reaching a new millennium requires reinterpreting the great and deep-rooted state tradition and historical accumulations in the conditions of the new century and bringing them together with contemporary developments by completing the development and democratization process by assimilating the bitter and sweet experiences of the past millennia.
Our only desire is for the Turkish nation to reach many more centuries as independent, unconnected and strong. I hope we will achieve this with the Presidential government system. Thanks to the presidential system of government, a strong Turkey that is at peace with its own national and historical values, has the ability to solve problems, has established a developed, effective state order and has become more respected in the community of states will have a say in world politics and economic life in the 21st century.
I wish the 2025 budget to be beneficial and fruitful, and I offer my respects.

PRESIDENT OF THE COURT OF AUDITORS METİN YENER - Mr. President and esteemed members of the Planning and Budget Commission; As I begin my speech, I greet you all with respect.
As it is known, in accordance with the 161st article titled "Budget and final account" of the Constitution of the Republic of Turkey and the 42nd article titled "Final account law" of the Public Financial Management and Control Law no. 5018, the final account law proposal was prepared by the Ministry of Treasury and Finance in accordance with the form of the Central Government Budget Law, according to the accounting records, and was prepared by the Ministry of Treasury and Finance in accordance with the form of the Central Government Budget Law, on June 28, 2024, together with the justification containing evaluations showing comparatively the results of one-year implementation. It was presented to the National Assembly and a copy was sent to the Court of Accounts.

As a result of the evaluation of the audits and activity reports carried out by the audit group directorates in accordance with Article 41 of the Court of Accounts Law No. 6085 titled "General compliance notification", it has been observed that there is no incompatibility between the results of the implementation of the 2023 Central Government Budget Law included in the 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal and the results determined based on the accounts of public administrations.
As is known, extra-appropriation spending and appropriation addition are different from each other. Over-appropriation expenditures are expenditures that exceed the appropriation amounts provided in the budget law. Appropriation addition is the addition of appropriations to the budgets of public administrations within the budget year, based on the authority given by law.
To date, in general compliance notifications, the Court of Accounts has presented the above-appropriation expenses included in the final account law proposal to the Grand National Assembly of Turkey, and in appropriation additions, it has examined whether the authority given by law and the limits, if any, are complied with and stated whether they are in compliance with the legislation.
In the 2023 general conformity notification, there is essentially no different method and practice from the general conformity notifications of previous years. In the 2023 general compliance notification, it is stated that "The appropriation additions were examined in terms of their compliance with the laws and limits authorizing them, and it was seen that they were in compliance with the legislation. As stated in the previous years, the general compliance notification - on page 12 - found that as a result of the examination of the Court of Accounts audit reports, there was no determination that caused a change in the amounts of appropriations and budget expenses." It was presented to the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye by saying: Since there is no over-appropriation expense in the 2023 Final Account Proposal, a table regarding this is not included in the general compliance notification.
In the 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal, page 33, general budget administrations; The total of the added appropriation columns in the summary tables of appropriations and budget expenses of special budget administrations on page 147 and page 229 of regulatory and supervisory institutions is 1 trillion 318 billion 264 thousand 519 liras and 82 kuruş, and the usage information of this amount on a program basis can be obtained when Table 9 and Table 13 of the 2023 General Compliance Declaration are examined together. The difference between the total appropriation amount in the 2023 central government budget expenditures and appropriations - in table-9 - on a program basis in the general compliance declaration is 7 trillion 290 billion 788 million 49 thousand 519 lira 82 kuruş and the initial appropriation of 4 trillion 808 billion 267 million 645 thousand lira, is 2 trillion 482 billion 520 million 404 thousand. 519 lira is 82 kuruş. When the amount given with the additional budget of 1 trillion 164 billion 520 million 140 thousand lira in the appropriation table added with the additional budget on a program basis in Table-13 is subtracted from this amount, we see in Table-9 how much of the remaining 1 trillion 318 billion 264 thousand 519 lira 82 kuruş additional appropriation was used on a program basis. The basis for the added appropriation in the amount of 1 trillion 318 billion 264 thousand 519 liras and 82 kuruş TL is included in the budget laws and various laws.

Provisional Article 1 of Law No. 7456 grants the President the authority to add funds for personnel expenses, state premium expenses to social security institutions, and premium expenses for universal health insurance. Apart from this, the executive branch is given the authority to add appropriations by Article 30 of the State of Emergency Law No. 2935, Budget Law No. 7427 and other laws.
In the examinations and audits carried out by the Court of Accounts regarding the appropriation additions in the 2023 Final Account Law Proposal, it was seen that the authority to add appropriations was used within the basis and limits of the relevant laws and this was stated in the general compliance notification.
The details of 1 trillion 318 billion 264 thousand 519 lira and 82 kuruş in the Final Account Law Proposal are as follows: 15 billion 275 million 839 thousand 96 lira 2 kuruş as the appropriation carried over from the previous year, 107 billion 589 million 201 thousand 523 lira as the appropriation added and deducted by internal and external reserve transfers, 1 trillion 195 TL. billion 135 million 223 thousand 900 lira 80 kuruş other added appropriations.
The details of the other added appropriation amount, 1 trillion 195 billion 135 million 223 thousand 900 lira and 80 kuruş, are as follows: 3 billion 602 million 793 thousand 250 lira, 43 kuruş special appropriation carried over from last year, 9 billion 797 million 718 thousand 68 lira, 72 kuruş special appropriation added during the year, 2 billion. 782 million 210 thousand 112 lira, 14 kuruş letter of credit surplus added allowance, 14 billion 874 million 361 thousand 445 lira, 38 kuruş loading surplus added allowance, 1 trillion 164 billion 104 million 269 thousand 29 lira, 23 kuruş added allowance during the year, 26 million 128 thousand 5 lira. 10 kuruş canceled allowance paid during the year.
The detailed figures and legal basis of the 1 trillion 164 billion 104 million 269 thousand 29 lira 23 kuruş appropriation added during the year are as follows: 692 billion 222 million 230 thousand lira. Appropriation additions made pursuant to the authority given by the temporary article 1 of Law No. 7456, 223 billion 749 million 100 thousand lira. The appropriation additions made in accordance with the 30th article of the State of Emergency Law No. 2935, 80 billion 871 million 85 thousand 828 lira and 40 kuruş are the appropriation additions made within the scope of the powers given by various articles of the Budget Law No. 7427, and 167 billion 261 million 853 thousand 200 lira and 83 kuruş are the appropriation additions made within the scope of the powers given by various other laws. appropriation additions.

Of course, the main problem here is actually this: I think the attitude of the administration here until the last few years was correct. This was a very disastrous thing in terms of usurping the budget right of the Parliament, we criticized them a lot here, but I don't know if it came in the last few years, at least in 2022, 2023 or even 2021 that I remember, I guess it came in 2021 too, at least it is coming from the budget with additional budget laws. Although the procedure is like a normal law, of course, if you are making a budget of 25-30 percent of the budget, it is necessary to change the procedure from the normal legal procedure to a budget law procedure. CHAIRMAN: Mehmet MUŞ (Samsun)

CHAIRMAN MEHMET MUŞ - Dear MPs, I am opening the Second Session of the 4th Meeting.
Now, on behalf of the AK PARTY Group, I give my word to Aksaray Deputy Mr. Hüseyin Altınsoy.
Here you go.
HÜSEYİN ALTINSOY (Aksaray) - Thank you, Mr. President.
Dear President, Dear Vice President, valuable members of our Planning and Budget Commission, representatives of our public institutions and organizations, valuable members of the press; I greet you all with respect, love and affection.
Before I begin my words, I congratulate our Republic Day, which celebrated its 101st anniversary yesterday, and wish God's mercy to our heroic martyrs and veterans who lost their lives, especially Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk.
I hope that the discussions on the 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal and the 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal will be beneficial for our country and nation.
As AK PARTY, we have worked to increase our service quality by producing services in every field from education to health, from transportation to energy, from industry to agriculture, from sports to social support. We continue to work to develop, grow and strengthen our country, increase the welfare of our citizens, make their lives easier, and also ensure their security.
The 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal has an approach that supports investment, employment, production and exports and increases social welfare. Our budget is based on sharing the opportunities provided by growth with all segments of society. The 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal has been prepared in line with our policies of maintaining fiscal discipline, ensuring economic stability and supporting sustainable growth.
There are many developments that threaten trust and stability in different parts of the world, especially in our nearby geography. The successive events that took place in this historical period indicate that humanity, societies and economies have reached a point of radical transformation. The current conjuncture dominated by increasing uncertainty and risks makes predictions about the future very difficult. This challenging process in the world and regional economy was carefully taken into account in the 2025 budget preparations, and our budget was created to minimize global risks for our country.
Dear MPs, growth slowed down in 2022 with the withdrawal of the extraordinary incentive policies implemented after the epidemic in the global economy, and the inflationary wave that occurred in this process brought about a financial tightening that spread throughout the world, especially in developed economies, and increased the expectations for a slowdown in economic activities. Since this date, the pressure of tight monetary policies on economic growth and risk appetite has become evident, while geopolitical tensions have maintained their weight on the agenda.

In the 2020-2023 period, the world economy grew by a cumulative 10.7 percent despite the difficult conditions, and compared to the world, the Turkish economy overcame these difficult conditions and displayed a more impressive growth performance, growing by a cumulative 25.9 percent. When evaluated in terms of average annual growth, while the world economy grew by 2.6 percent in this period, the Turkish economy managed to grow by 5.9 percent, more than twice the world average. These results clearly reveal Turkey's economic power and resilient structure. With the positive effects of our transparent and predictable policy steps that ensure strong coordination between monetary and fiscal policies, our top priority will be to reduce the inflation rate to single-digit levels during the program period and ensure that it stabilizes at these levels, in the continuation of the disinflation process that started as of June 2024.

We see that the 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal has been prepared in accordance with the Public Financial Management and Control Law No. 5018 and in line with the following objectives: Strengthening macroeconomic and financial stability, maintaining fiscal discipline, ensuring price stability by reducing inflation to single digits in the medium term, improving R&D and innovation capacity, ensuring technological transformation with a focus on green and digital transformation, strengthening human capital, further activating the labor market, improving the business and investment environment, and reducing informality in the economy; To continue the determined implementation of monetary, fiscal and income policies in a mutually supportive manner in the fight against inflation; To compensate for the damage caused by the earthquakes that occurred in February 2023 and continue to take the necessary measures to reduce disaster risks; To increase voluntary compliance, predictability and transparency in taxation, to strengthen tax justice, to redistribute income fairly and to increase revenues with an approach that spreads the tax to the base; To carry out the fight against informality with the active participation of all parties by making better use of technological opportunities and to continue auditing activities aimed at reducing informality; To act in line with the policies, measures and priorities determined in the basic policy documents in public resource use; To ensure that public administrations use their resources effectively, economically and efficiently, without exceeding the allocations allocated to them; To carry out public services with an understanding of efficiency and savings, to comply with and monitor savings measures; Implementing education and employment policies that will direct human capital to the jobs of the future in the medium term; To ensure the appropriate use of service levels by strengthening demand control and integration between service levels in health products and services; To support agricultural activities on the periphery of cities, which are important consumption centers, to ensure that consumers can access agricultural products at affordable prices by reducing logistics costs; To ensure that reserves are brought into the economy with high added value by increasing domestic and international exploration and production activities for oil, natural gas and mineral resources; It aims to achieve the goals I have mentioned above, especially to strengthen the financial sustainability of the system by implementing regulations that prioritize equity and actuarial balance, which encourage people to remain more employed in the social security system.
Budget appropriations are determined by taking into account the macroeconomic indicators, policies and priorities, measure targets, previous years' spending trends of the administrations, the important activities, projects and needs they carry out in the medium-term program; It is seen that budget revenue estimates are created by taking into account the revenue policy targets and priorities determined in the medium-term program, macroeconomic indicators, previous year revenue collections, the effects of revenue policies on budget revenues, and developments in national and international markets.

While 2025 central government budget income and expense forecasts were being made, it was assumed that the gross domestic product would grow by 4 percent in real terms, the year-end CPI would be 17.5 percent, exports would be 279.6 billion dollars, imports would be 369 billion dollars, and it is also envisaged to allocate sufficient reserve funds in order to carry out the urgent and priority services that will arise during the budget implementation.
Mr. President, dear members of parliament; There are budgets of 227 public administrations within the scope of the 2025 central government budget. I would also like to give some figures regarding the 2025 budget proposal: In the 2025 central government budget, it is anticipated that budget expenses will be 14 trillion 731 billion liras and budget revenues will be 12 trillion 800 billion liras. The ratio of the budget deficit to gross domestic product is expected to be 3.1 percent. After the increase in budget deficits due to the earthquake, it is seen that the budget deficit is approaching its average level again during our governments' period.
It can be seen that, while our governments continued to maintain fiscal discipline with determination, on the other hand, the budget was transformed from an interest budget into a service budget. Interest expenses, which were 14.3 percent of the national income in 2002, were reduced to very low levels, as a matter of fact, it is seen that interest expenses are at 3.2 percent in the 2025 budget.
In order to eliminate the damages caused by earthquakes in 2023, 960 billion TL was spent in the budget, and it is anticipated that 1 trillion 28 billion TL will be spent on earthquakes in 2024. In the 2025 budget, a total of 584 billion TL is allocated for the reduction of disaster risks, the rapid elimination of damage caused by earthquakes, the needs of our citizens living in the earthquake zone and the construction of earthquake-resistant cities. All authorities and institutions of our state continue to work uninterruptedly to ensure that life in the earthquake zone returns to normal quickly.
The distribution of 2025 budget appropriations according to economic classification is envisaged as follows: A total of 3 trillion 911 billion lira for personnel expenses, 1 trillion 24 billion lira for goods and service purchase expenses, 5 trillion 813 billion lira for current transfers, 1 trillion 102 billion lira for capital expenses, 338 billion lira for capital transfers, 306 billion lira for lending expenses, reserve appropriations. 287 billion lira, interest expenses are 1 trillion 950 billion lira.
Dear MPs, we have considered education, one of the most important elements of development, as the most important area in our budgets since 2002, and we have increased the investment made by the Ministry of National Education over the years. With our budget proposal, we increased our education budget to 2 trillion 181 billion lira in 2025. Thus, we allocate the largest share of the central government budget to education, which received a share of only 9.4 percent in 2002, with a rate of 14.8 percent in 2025. In addition, it is seen that the investment budget of the Ministry of National Education has been increased by 41.2 percent compared to the 2024 appropriation, from 100 billion lira to 142 billion lira.

Again, one of the issues we attach most importance to during our governments' term is health services. In order to ensure that our citizens benefit from health services in the best way, it is seen that 1 trillion 106 billion lira resources have been allocated for health services in the central government budget in 2025. Considering the health expenditures from the Ministry of Health, higher education institutions and the Social Security Institution, the total amount allocated from public resources to the field of health reaches 2 trillion 435 billion liras. Again, while our number of manpower in health services was 379 thousand in total in 2002, 257 thousand of which were within the Ministry of Health, it increased to 1 million 448 thousand in October 2024, 872 thousand of which were within the Ministry of Health.
The Turkish economy maintains its strong structure not only with investment, employment, exports and growth, but also with the solidity of its financial structure. We are determined to spread the prosperity of our country to all layers of society with the principle of social state. As one of the countries with the best functioning and most comprehensive social support system in the world, we are increasing the social aid budget to 650.9 billion lira in 2025; This amount corresponds to 4.4 percent of our budget.
I would like to briefly share other social resources we provide from the budget to increase the welfare of our citizens. A resource of 472 billion lira is foreseen in the 2025 budget so that our citizens can use electricity and natural gas more cheaply. In 2024, approximately 60 percent subsidy is applied for electricity and natural gas to residential subscribers. As of January 2022, we have kept the minimum wage exempt from tax, and all our employees benefit from this opportunity. In this context, a tax exemption of 810.4 billion lira is foreseen in 2025.
Dear MPs, the resource allocated to agriculture from our budget in 2025 is being increased to 705.6 billion lira. In this context, 135 billion liras are allocated for agricultural support programs, 166.3 billion liras for agricultural sector investment allowances, and 204.6 billion liras for agricultural credit subsidies, intervention purchases, agricultural kits and export supports. Agricultural sector investment allowances are also being increased significantly, and in 2025, the agricultural sector investment allowance will be increased to 166.3 billion lira, an increase of 64 percent compared to the previous year. While the agricultural irrigation investment allowance was at the level of 23.1 billion lira in 2002-2022, this figure is increased to 51.4 billion lira in 2023, to 60.3 billion lira in 2024, and to 108.1 billion lira in 2025, an increase of 79.3 percent compared to the previous year.
As of 2003, our railway line length of 10,959 kilometers has been increased to a total of 13,919 kilometers, 2,251 kilometers of which are high-speed lines, by the end of September 2024, our signaled line length of 2,505 kilometers has been increased to 8,046 kilometers, an increase of 221 percent, and our electrified line length of 2,122 kilometers has been increased to 7,274 kilometers, an increase of 243 percent.

An allowance of 561 billion lira is allocated from our budget for real sector supports, in this context, 222.8 billion lira for Social Security Institution employer premium support payment, 160 billion lira for agricultural loans subsidy support, 60 billion lira for Halk Bank tradesman loan subsidy support, 29.6 billion lira for state contribution paid within the scope of vocational training, and 56.9 billion lira for other real sector supports, especially exports. is leaving.
In addition, while only 62 defense projects were carried out in 2002, the number of projects has increased 16 times to over a thousand. With the contribution of the resources we allocate to defense and security, the localization rate in the defense industry has exceeded 80 percent. As of the end of 2023, the total defense and aviation sector turnover, which includes all defense and aviation sales of our defense companies and shows the size of the sector, reached 15.1 billion dollars. Our defense and aerospace exports, which were only 248 million dollars in the past, grew more than 22 times and reached 5.54 billion dollars in 2023, and are expected to reach 6.6 billion dollars by the end of 2024.
2024 has been a year in which a more positive picture was seen in foreign demand conditions and global trade in terms of our exports, and 2025 will also be the same.
We would like to thank our Vice President Mr. Cevdet Yılmaz and our Strategy and Budget Presidency, our Ministry of Treasury and Finance and all public institutions and organizations that contributed to the preparation of the budget proposal, non-governmental organizations and professional organizations that contributed with their opinions and suggestions, as well as our Commission members for their contribution to our work with their evaluations, and wish that the 2025 budget and all the work we will carry out with this budget will be auspicious for our country and our beloved nation. I wish.

Another important issue is that we have a very serious organized industry in Malatya - as Mr. Vice President knows very well. Now, I would like to express a few of my opinions about the budget. There are budget revenues and expenses, the way these funds are collected and how they are spent. First, let's look at the budget revenues section. We are probably one of the countries with the highest indirect taxes in the world. In 2023, the ratio of tax revenues to gross annual product was 19.3 percent, that is, 26 trillion 545 billion TL.

Among this, tax revenues amounted to 5.1 trillion, which seems to be 1 fifth of the gross national product. 1 fifth of production, exports, sales and all income. In other words, equal taxes are collected from the poor and the poor of the 85 million people, which unfortunately creates injustice in taxes. One of the most important issues that both unions and worker organizations have said is justice in this tax, justice must be ensured in this tax, that is, very rich people pay the same tax, and people with no income pay the same tax.
Unfortunately, on the expenditure side of budget expenditures, the situation is exactly the opposite. There is a serious distribution injustice in the budget. Unfortunately, the share allocated from the budget for the segment of the society that survives on wage income, retirement income, poverty pension or social aid is not comparable to the share allocated to the rich minority. Look, now you are saying new things, you have found new enemies of the economy. Now EYT members have come out and said, "The opposition deceived us and tricked us." Now you have put the burden on retirees and EYT members, as if retirees had ruined the economy, as if the theory of interest cause and inflation had no contribution in 2021. There is a social media phenomenon: "My only fault is my beauty." he says.
