Now, Cevdet Yılmaz is making a presentation to us here, trying to make us a part of the budget, but throughout August, you met with TOBB President Rifat Hisarcıklıoğlu, TİSK President Özgür Burak Akkol, the record-breaking Bayraktars, the Investment Environment Improvement Coordination Board of which he is the President, the US Chamber of Commerce, the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development, and first you submitted the budget to their approval; Now you are trying to get the budget of the capital approved by presenting it to us as "the budget of 85 million people". Now.

An AKP MP asked here and said, "Tell me about this wealth transfer so we can learn." I swear, let's say it openly: There is no good wealth transfer from this government's tax policy. Companies that make millions of dollars pay less taxes than a worker pays in three months in this country. Metal Workers' wages are reduced to dust every month by tax deductions. Now, look, you want to reduce the share of public services, services.

ELİF ESEN (Istanbul) - Thank you, Mr. President.
Dear Vice President, esteemed members of parliament, esteemed bureaucrats; I also hope that our budget will be beneficial for our country and our citizens.
I also wholeheartedly congratulate the 101st anniversary of our Republic; I hope to see better days and better years.
I also strongly condemn the TAI attack. I offer my condolences to the relatives of our martyrs and wish a speedy recovery to our injured.
Now, I want to make a speech in favor of this budget being child-focused because, yes, the citizens of the country are extremely troubled, extremely troubled, but the most vulnerable segments of the citizens are children and our elderly; They are wounded much more deeply. Now, going back to the child, “Why child?” If you say, 1 in every three children in Turkey lives within the poverty line. According to TUIK's 2023 data, more than 7 million of our children are forced to grow up under poverty conditions. This figure is equal to the total population of Slovenia, Lithuania, Estonia and Luxembourg. Moreover, this poverty is not just economic poverty; Our children have to grow up as victims and deprived of many rights in terms of education, health and social development, and this poverty now threatens the future of our children.
When we talk about the survival of the country, I say this in many of my speeches: The real survival of our country is actually our children. There used to be a classic saying: “Our children are our future.” Yes, our children are our future, but if those children live with hope, peace and in a happy environment and develop with equal rights and opportunities with their peers, we can only become qualified individuals and talk about the future of our country; Therefore, the future and survival of our country are our children. In order for these children to live a healthy life, continue their education, and look to the future with hope without being dragged into crime, the budget must be planned correctly and distributed fairly.
We found that the share allocated to capital expenses in the budget distribution of the Ministry of Family and Court of Accounts reports has increased rapidly. On the other hand, these data reveal that a decrease is observed in current transfers such as social aid. Policies specifically targeting child poverty should focus on social services rather than capital expenditures. It is extremely essential to improve the hygiene conditions in schools, provide poor children with at least one free meal a day, and support them with social support mechanisms.
In fact, I would like to say this here: When it comes to "social policies of the state budget", social aid and social services should also be differentiated. Social aids are cash transfers and in fact - I am a social politician - they are cash transfers that we prefer less and should be limited to a few months, maybe five or six months, for temporary periods - for example, earthquakes, floods, war times. What is important is social services. Turkey's women's society, Turkey's women's population, has been unethical in this sense, especially since the last decade, when the AKP government no longer cared about the support of other segments of society and consolidated its own base on the basis of polarization.

Dear friends, now I will give the floor to our Vice President, Mr. Cevdet Yılmaz, to answer your opinions, criticisms and questions.
Dear Vice President, you can respond in writing to questions that are not answered within the deadline within seven days.
Come on Mr. Yılmaz, your time is forty-five minutes.
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - Mr. President, dear members of parliament; I greet you all with respect.
In my presentation speech before the Planning and Budget Commission on October 22, I presented the 2025 Budget Law and the 2023 Final Account Law Proposal to your appreciation on behalf of the executive. Today, I listened to your evaluations on the overall budget and noted your questions and criticisms. I would like to thank all our MPs, especially the Chairman of the Planning and Budget Commission, Council members and Commission members, who contributed to the budget process with their constructive and guiding contributions.
I will make a general evaluation based on the areas where criticism and questions are concentrated and I will answer the questions as much as time allows.
Mr. President, dear MPs; On the 101st anniversary of our republic, we are taking firm steps forward with the goals of the Türkiye Century, and we are working with all our strength to carry our country to a brighter future. On this occasion, I once again congratulate the 101st anniversary of the founding of our republic, and I commemorate with mercy and gratitude all our martyrs who sacrificed their lives for the sake of their homeland, especially the founder of our republic, Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk and his friends, and our veterans who passed away to the afterlife.
On the other hand, I wish mercy to our martyrs and all our martyrs who lost their lives in the attack on TAI, the leading organization of our defense industry, recently. Defense and security are a matter of survival for all of us, that is, our 85 million citizens and our state, and at the same time, developments in the defense industry are an extremely important element in creating an economic structure with high added value and increasing social welfare. Therefore, we condemn this attack on our defense industry, this terrorist attack, in every respect.
It is also a fact that in periods when the basis for dialogue in politics has become stronger, terrorism has repeatedly been tried to haunt the fate of our nation. It is also obvious that these heinous attacks are being used as a tool to manipulate politics. Bloody terrorist organizations have not been able to divert our country from its direction so far, and they will not be able to divert it from now on.

Wherever they want to direct us, we will act in the opposite direction; We will further strengthen our unity, solidarity and brotherhood, we will support our defense industry determinedly as always, and we will continue our determined fight against terrorism.
Mr. President, dear MPs; I stated in my introductory speech last week that our 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal was prepared with a perspective that would make the gains sustainable and prioritize economic development and social welfare. As Türkiye, we are going through an important period, we are faced with a historical opportunity, and that is this: We are experiencing a period without elections that we have not experienced in a long time. Periods when elections are held intensively are inevitably periods when short-term issues occupy more of the agenda. Today, Türkiye is experiencing a period without elections; We are moving on, having left the general and local elections behind, and we are at a critical stage, and we are in an extremely challenging phase, period, and environment in the world. In this environment, we have the opportunity to make good use of this period without elections, together with the government and the opposition, and take our country to a different level, not only quantitatively but also qualitatively, and we can achieve some long-term changes and transformations in this period, and we should all take advantage of this opportunity together. In this sense, one of the main priorities is to save Turkey from the middle income trap and move it from the upper middle income league to the high income countries league. While doing this, our main goal should of course be to bring Turkey to the highest levels in terms of human development. Of course, this is not just about the economy here; I would like to express that we are going through a critical period in terms of increasing the capacity of our country in every field, from the justice system to the education system, from technology policies to many other areas, reaching a stronger tomorrow and building the Türkiye Century.
Within this general framework, our budget has an approach that supports investment, employment, production and exports and increases social welfare. It was prepared by taking into account the needs of all segments of society, including workers, tradesmen, retirees, young and old, and the needs of our 81 provinces were taken into consideration, without distinguishing between east and west, or north and south. The resources we allocate for social aid and support are one of the clearest indicators of this; showing the social dimension. Again, electricity and natural gas subsidies, the tax expenditures we make on the minimum wage, all these show that our budget actually has a significant social content.
One of the most distinctive features of our budget is that it is a reconstruction budget; We experienced the greatest disaster in our history and we are trying to heal its wounds. If we calculate the last year at today's prices and add it up, we are talking about a burden of around 2.6 trillion liras for our two-year expenditure, and this will continue next year.
“Will next year's decline affect construction work?” Our friends asked questions, and I would like to express this: This is absolutely out of the question.

We continue our work under three headings; construction of housing, repair of permanent residences and workplaces, infrastructure, revitalization of economic and social life. Our revival and development works continue under these three headings. Budget is an accrual-based document; Accrued expenses are shown in the budget, but their cash realization may occur over time. Therefore, some of this may be due to this timing difference, but when you look at it on an accrual basis, 2.6 trillion will have been accrued as of the end of this year, and as of next year - now, the exact number; Let me not say anything wrong - a figure of 580-odd will be allocated next year as well; Therefore, we will implement this action plan we have put forward. From here.
SELİM TEMURCİ (Istanbul) - Total 106 billion.
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - An initial calculation of 104 billion was made; To date, what we have made from the central government alone has reached approximately 70 billion dollars. Please don't look at it just as residences; Well, there are many expenditure items, from natural gas and transportation to hospitals, schools, tunnels, roads, airport repairs and some economic supports. When you look at these, if we can finish with 104 billion dollars, that would be quite good, we will probably exceed that. These are only the central government expenditures that I mentioned, there are also the expenditures of local governments, our Social Security Institution, the expenditures of institutions other than the central government budget, such as the Bank of Provinces, and we do not count them in these expenditures. So there is a big expense. “Why did debts, deficits and interest rates increase during this period?” This is one of the most important reasons for this. Earthquake expenditures have temporarily increased our budget deficit to a very high level. Our finances have to borrow to finance these deficits. Therefore - some of our friends also said - we have to finance our expenses, especially earthquake-related expenses, by borrowing more than we pay in this temporary period, but this is not a structural problem and after a while these expenses will be removed from our budget and we will have a more comfortable perspective. We already foresee this in our Medium Term Program. With this relief, I can say that a more positive perspective is ahead of us in the medium term, both in terms of our finances' borrowing and interest expenses.
Now, you have made very important comments, determinations and criticisms. With your permission, I would of course agree with some of them and disagree with some of them, but I would like to share my own evaluations with you.
Opinions regarding the presidential government system were expressed. I would like to underline this: It is a positive development that the Presidential government system, as a system that guarantees political stability at a time when risks are rising at the global level and in our region, came with the votes of our people, and we tested this during the pandemic period, we tested it in geopolitical developments, and we are also testing it in some economic decisions and policies. I leave it to your discretion what kind of risks the challenges we face might pose if such a structure did not exist and if our political stability did not continue.

On the other hand, I would like to express this: Both the parliamentary system and the presidential system are democratic systems. Some democracies have a presidential system, some have a parliamentary system; It is not right to describe one as democratic and the other as not. There are very good presidential systems, very good parliamentary systems, semi-presidential systems, and each system has its own sub-versions. Therefore, both are democratic systems. As long as you know how to operate them well, a good culture will be formed and these systems will develop together with democratic culture. These are not regimes, they are not alternatives to the democratic regime; These are management systems. We have moved to the presidential system. I would just like to underline this here: Of course, it will take some time for these systems to be established and fully institutionalized. In this process, I would like to state that we are always open to learning lessons from experience and working to identify and eliminate deficiencies, if any, socially. In fact, this is the inherent perspective put forward with this new constitution. In other words, a dynamic structure was envisaged in which the administration would eliminate the deficiencies it saw within itself through decrees, without even coming to our Parliament on some issues. Therefore, “Everything is perfect here, there is nothing to criticize.” We are not saying that, but I would like to express that the effort to present a total system as the source of all kinds of problems is extremely meaningless. During the period of the parliamentary system, Türkiye faced many issues in the context of economic, social, political and international relations, and of course we are dealing with many issues today. I believe it would be very easy to explain all of these with a system analysis. Therefore, I believe that the Presidential government system is a valuable system that ensures and guarantees stability. Of course, there are issues to be discussed here regarding legislative-executive relations, there are definitely steps that can be taken on other issues, I would like to express these without excluding them.
On the other hand, there was a debate started mostly by Mr. Erhan, whether the budget-related rate should be given or the absolute figure should be given. Of course, absolute figures can be given when necessary, but the main thing is proportional analyzes in economic analyses. We all give absolute numbers from time to time, we also state them, there may be places where it is useful. Especially in comparisons with the near future, absolute figures may be more meaningful. But the important thing is to make correct analysis here. Both the opposition and the government sometimes use absolute numbers, sometimes they use ratios, we all do it, but of course the correct thing is proportional numbers in the economy. Especially in periods of rising inflation, the difference between absolute figures may widen over time. Therefore, it is always healthier to use ratios; I would like to underline this. I believe it would be beneficial for all of us to be consistent here.
Of course, it is also important which year we use as reference. We, as the Government, started in 2002. Therefore, naturally, our reference year is 2002.

You can take another reference point and analyze from there, this is of course your right, I'm not saying anything. But I would also like to say this, especially in the context of the budget, again in the sense of a criticism of consistency; We are faced with a situation like this - especially our friends in the opposition: Let's say they make all kinds of expense-increasing proposals, and they keep their distance from all income-increasing proposals, and together with these two, they say: "The budget deficit should also decrease, the state should not borrow, and it should not pay interest." they say. Now, all these things cannot go together, so if you are offering an expense, you also have to show a source of income.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (İzmir) - You brought tax reform and said "No." Did we say Mr. Vice President?
ERHAN USTA (Samsun) - Income from movable capital, income tax.
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - You have to put these together and make them more concrete.
ERHAN USTA (Samsun) - Interest, stocks, repo, all instruments. Let's take income tax from those who make money from money, let's tax them.
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - We would be happy if we could hear them in more detail.
Dear friends, when we look at it in this sense, when we look at the budget figures in proportion rather than absolute numbers, we foresee a deficit of around 3.1% next year. This is a deficit approaching our historical averages, I say this as the AK Party period. If I remember correctly, our twenty-year average is around 2.7, we will reach a figure approaching it next year. We aim for something even better in the following years.
Mr. Türeli had examined the MTP-plan relationship. So here consistency, inconsistency and so on. Of course, it can be interpreted in different ways, but let me share my own interpretation. What is important in plans is the policy framework, the direction it provides and the framework it draws in basic policies. Plans, some call them "rolling plans", are renewed every year. You know, our plans are not like that, we renew them every five years. Therefore, we do not have the opportunity to revise the figures there every year, but the world and Türkiye are dynamic.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (İzmir) - It's only been a year, Mr. Vice President?
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - A year ago, these incidents did not exist in Gaza, now there are; I say this as an example. So when you make plans, you make assumptions. You draw a perspective with your assumptions by looking at the development of an economy in the world and the region, taking into account the situation in the world and the region with your assumptions of the day. More important than the numbers in the plans is the policy framework drawn by the plans and the general direction they draw, this should be taken as a basis. When you take this as a basis, I do not see a policy inconsistency between the MTP and the plan. There may be numerical differences because MTP is a document that is renewed every year and you have the opportunity to reflect the developments that took place during that year in the figures. Therefore, naturally, there may be a difference in terms of numbers, but our policy framework is no different. The focus of our plan and the focus of the MTP is "Green and Digital Transformation", there is stability, there is growing the economy, there is turning the blessings of the growing economy into permanent social welfare.

Therefore, there is no inconsistency in that sense.
Secondly, some of our friends made comments regarding this inflation-growth relationship. Is it growth or inflation? Is there a contradiction or an impasse here? Of course, there may be some difficulties between growth and the fight against inflation in the short term, and you may have to make some choices. But if we look at it theoretically and from Turkey's experience, we can easily say this: We do not see a contradiction between the fight against inflation and growth in the medium-long term, because an environment where you reduce inflation also means an environment where you increase predictability and improve the investment environment. We also know from historical experience that Turkey's growth is more stable and higher in periods when inflation is low. Therefore, we do not see a contradiction here. It may be short-term, there may be periodic short-term contradictions and some difficulties, but when you look at the medium term, there is no such contradiction; On the contrary, there is a positive relationship between low inflation and stable, sustainable growth. The same goes for income distribution. As you know, inflation is an event that distorts income distribution. The decline in inflation is in itself a positive development in terms of income distribution; I would like to underline this too. Reducing inflation is very important for a permanent increase in welfare, and it is also important for social balances. I will come back to social policy in a moment.
We had friends who brought up the issue of regional inequalities, Mr. Oluç brought it up more. I can easily say this about these issues: Regional inequalities have definitely decreased, not increased, in the last twenty years. First of all, we have invested heavily in infrastructure in all our regions; I can also tell you in quantitative terms that our more underdeveloped regions have received more investments in roads, airports, dams, irrigation facilities, as well as education and health facilities. I honestly have no hesitation there. For example, if double roads have increased, let's say, 3 times throughout Turkey, they have increased 10 times in the east and southeast, that is, when you compare it with the past, there has been a much higher increase in these regions since they were less in the past. On the other hand, we combined this with policies that encourage the private sector. On the one hand, we reinforced the environment of security and peace; I think this is the most important thing. Why couldn't these regions receive some services? Why did they fall behind? One of the most important reasons is security. Security is also related to the investment environment. You would guess that there will be no capital left in a place where there is no security. Some capitalists in the east and southeast moved their capital to other regions for security reasons, in the past qualified personnel did not go to the region for security reasons, the plateaus could not be used, and tourism remained weak; We know it all, I won't go into too much detail.
Terrorism has two types of damage: One is direct damage, loss of life, loss of property, direct damage. Secondly, it causes greater economic damage indirectly.

This means tourists who do not go because of terrorism, capital that does not go, qualified personnel that do not go, some social and economic activities that do not develop; The people living in the region suffered the most from all this. On the contrary, the decrease and end of terrorism will produce the greatest benefit to the people living in the region, the southeast and the east. There is only one difference, let me tell you that: The destructive effects of terrorism, the indirect damages I mentioned, appear in the shorter term, the constructive effects require a little longer time, the constructive effects do not develop immediately, it takes some time for people to normalize and for the security environment to attract and develop investors again. Therefore, the east and southeast are also in this process right now, and I believe that the east and southeast have a development perspective above the country average, especially with private sector investments, not only with public investments, but also with tourism, agriculture, and trade. We see this too.
Another element here - for the east and southeast - is trade with neighbors. No country or region in the world can develop without trade and economic relations with neighbors. Therefore, I would like to underline two projects in this context: One of them is the Development Road Project, I would like to express that it is a road that crosses Iraq, opens a new perspective for Turkey in the international arena, and is really valuable for the east and southeast. The other one is the Zengezur Corridor, which is very valuable in terms of the connection between Eastern Anatolia and Turkey with the Caucasus, the Turkish republics. With all this, and with the political stability in our region, I believe that the east and southeast will grow above Turkey's average because there is a potential that has not been used for a long time, and this potential will be activated much faster in an environment of peace and trust.
Many of our friends mentioned inflation issues, I am not mentioning names, but since many people mentioned them, let me give a general answer. One of the most important goals of this program, this budget, is undoubtedly to reduce inflation. We have said this from the beginning: We said there are three periods in this struggle, a transition period, a disinflation period, and then a permanent price stability period. We completed the transition period as of June and entered the disinflation process. There has been a decrease in the inflation rate of more than 26 points in the last four months, and we expect this to continue in the coming periods and we aim for an inflation rate below 20 percent by the end of next year, and a single-digit figure in 2026. There is a rigidity here in services, in fact it has reached a level close to 40 percent in goods, there is an inflation rate in basic goods that has fallen below 30 percent, but there is a rigidity especially in the service sectors, this rigidity will be broken over time and our citizens will feel it more strongly, our citizens have started to feel it in basic goods, some goods, but I would like to express that in a broader sense they will feel it better in the coming months.
What do I mean when I say our budget is compatible with disinflation? We are reducing budget deficits.

We are reducing our budget deficit, which is 4.9 this year, to 3.1 next year, which we estimate. Our expenditures are slightly higher than we predicted last year, but as you know, our deflator was also higher than predicted, our national income increased more, so when we look at it proportionally, last year we said that the ratio of public expenditures to national income would be 26.9 percent, now the realization rate we estimate is 25.4, that is, 1.5 points. I'm talking about the expenses proportionally, we just said what your rates are.
ERHAN USTA (Samsun) - We have an allowance, we need to use it this year.
Now you are using figures for 2002, Mr. Vice President.
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - I say ratio, I say ratio.
ERHAN USTA (Samsun) - No, you are using absolute numbers in 2002, you are using rates for the current year.
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - Which number did I use?
ERHAN USTA (Samsun) - Here, education expenses, I don't know what.
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - No, no, there is no such thing, look it up in the booklet if you want. We used ratios, there are always ratios in the booklet, I can show them.
Now, here - I'm doing exactly what you want - while our expenditure ratio is 26.9, we have made a public expenditure of around 1.5 points below what we predicted last year. As we say savings, careful spending and discipline are the best and most concrete indicators of this. We said it would be 26.9, but we say it will be 1.5 points below, 25.4 percent of public expenditures to national income; We aim to reduce this rate to 23.9 next year. Therefore, we can easily state that here too, there is a fiscal policy compatible with the disinflation process. I would like to express that our fiscal policy will follow a much more positive course, supporting both our general savings rates and the disinflation process, in a process where the ratio of expenditures and deficit to national income gradually decreases and the ratio of expenditures to national income decreases.
There has been quite a bit of discussion about taxes. “Is there justice in taxes or not? What did you do?” There were criticisms and discussions. It is not possible to summarize everything, but I would like to make some reminders. In terms of tax justice, we reduced the income tax burden in 2019, increased the tax rate of the lower bracket from 22 to 15 percent, and the upper income group from 35 to 40 percent; We did this in 2019. Secondly, as you know, we excluded all income up to the minimum wage from tax; This was truly a historic decision. We excluded not only minimum wage earners but all incomes up to minimum wage from tax. On the other hand, we brought tax exemption to tradesmen subject to simple procedures. We brought tax exemption to around 850 thousand tradesmen and small tradesmen. While the general VAT rate is 20 percent, we apply it as 1 percent for basic food. We apply 10 percent in health, education, food and beverage, tourism, clothing and residential electricity. We abolished VAT on feed and fertilizer - many of our friends emphasized how important agriculture is - and we abolished the income tax deduction for support payments to farmers. We increased the corporate tax by 5 points, increasing the general rate from 20 to 25.

We increased it to 30 percent for the finance sector and banking, we increased it to 30 percent for the profitability of public-private cooperation projects, and we set it as 20 percent for exporting companies; This is again a reflection of our policies on taxes.
SELİM TEMURCİ (Istanbul) - Despite all this, income justice is being disrupted.
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - I will come to him, he will come to justice.
We have removed some discounts and exclusions. We have determined the rate of interest and dividends obtained from KKM and participant accounts from zero to 5 percent and 7.5 percent depending on the maturity.

VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - Dear friends, friends also made comments about tax expenditure. 3 trillion 5 billion tax expenditure for 2025. A perception was created that it would all happen to companies; It's not like that at all. Now, I would like to present to you the details of these tax expenditures. I am telling you the details of this 3 trillion 5 billion: 536.1 billion TL to encourage investments, what you call capital to these companies, the incentives we give to investors to make investments. Secondly, these premium supports for the development of the labor market and promotion of employment - the premium supports we provide to workers to increase employment - amount to 876.8 billion liras. The support we provide to the disabled, the elderly, widows and orphans of martyrs for education, health and social purposes is 259.2 billion TL; These are the details of these 3 trillion.

147.3 billion TL to encourage R&D, innovation and design activities; 199.8 billion TL to encourage agricultural activities; 816.7 billion TL to increase investments through investment partnerships and funds and to encourage TL savings; We see that it is distributed as 169.3 billion TL for infrastructure, energy, transportation and other items. Again, one of the highest items in this amount is the minimum wage exemption that all our employees benefit from. The tax expenditure we made just for this is 810.4 billion lira, and this item alone corresponds to 27 percent of the total tax expenditure. When we include the amount not collected from stamp duty of 42.6 billion TL to this amount, it is estimated that a total of 853 billion TL of tax revenue will be given up. Therefore, this amount alone constitutes 28.4 percent of the total amount; I will not go into further detail, we especially request that this be understood in this way.
Debt-related issues came to the fore; Have debts increased or decreased? Again, we come to the debate between giving absolute numbers and giving ratios. If you give absolute numbers, you would say that debts in TL terms have increased, of course, but if you look at it as a ratio, what happened to this ratio in Turkey? Look, the ratio of the European Union-defined general government debt stock to gross domestic product - which was 71.5 percent in 2002 - decreased to 26.1 percent as of the second quarter of 2024. It decreased to 26.1; This is one of the historically very low levels in Turkey, the lowest level since 2001. The lowest level is currently the ratio of our total EU-defined public debt stock to national income. This rate will be 81 percent in EU member countries and 69 percent in developing countries at the end of 2023, meaning "You borrowed too much, you put the country in debt." Whether or not there is something to say, you decide. In this context, our country is among the least indebted countries in terms of general government debt stock when compared to both EU countries and developing countries.
In this context, we are faced with a similar situation regarding interest. You say absolute numbers about interest, you do not express rates, we need to talk about rates again. The ratio of interest expenditures to national income was 14.3 percent in 2002, and the expected realization in 2024 is 2.9 percent, so as you say this has increased, this is it. The average of the 2002-2023 period is 4.5 percent, and the average of the 2025-2027 period is 3.1 percent. I would like to express that as the temporary expenses related to this earthquake decrease and fiscal discipline continues, and if you take into account our current indebtedness levels, the share of interest expenditures in both the budget and the national income will tend to decrease again in the coming periods as our interest rates decrease - this takes a certain time, of course, we are going with an influence from the past.
Dear friends, we need to touch on income distribution issues and social justice issues because many of our friends have touched on them. Is there a deterioration in income distribution? Yes, there has been a deterioration, especially when we look at the 2022 figures, there is a deterioration in the Gini coefficient etc. Of course, this needs to be analyzed to see where it originates from.

Pandemic…
SELİM TEMURCİ (Istanbul) - Last year…
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - No, the number announced by TÜİK as 2023 is 2022 figures.
ERHAN USTA (Samsun) - Reference year.
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - In other words, the reference year is 2022, so "income distribution was disrupted in 2023." It should not be interpreted as, it is a number announced in 2023 but describing 2022. Therefore, when you look at the world here, we are going through a process where income distribution is deteriorating; wealth distribution and income distribution. Especially for the first time during the pandemic, the United Nations has Sustainable Development Goals, called SDGs. For the first time in the history of the United Nations, these figures have gone backwards. Unfortunately, there has been a regression in social indicators all over the world, let alone improvement. We were also affected by this, but we are determined to overcome this.
You gave figures relative to this national income, here is the share of the workforce in national income. Look, while the ratio of labor payments to national income was 33 percent in 2018, it decreased to 24 percent in 2022. True, we experienced a loss of employment, we experienced some difficulties, this rate increases to 29 percent in 2023 and 36 percent in 2024 and is the highest rate in our statistics to date. Therefore, this is a leading indicator, showing that there is a recovery and improvement in income distribution. Various friends of ours had commented on how much labor and capital receive. At the point we are at now, the ratio of net operating surplus to national income was 44 percent in 2018, it increased to 48 percent in 2022 - that's right, it increased - and fell to 42 percent in 2023, and decreased to 33 percent in the first half of 2024; This is the lowest level ever. Therefore, there is an improvement here, but this will be reflected in TURKSTAT figures and Gini figures with a delay.
I can summarize the general framework of our social policy as follows: We have a three-legged strategy from the very beginning, when you look at this twenty-year period. First, the macro level. To reduce inflation, improve the investment environment, sustain growth, increase employment; These are processes that reduce poverty and improve income distribution. First of all, without this macro level, you cannot achieve social balance only with micro interventions. We think that we have managed this macro dimension very positively in twenty years. The second stage is the sectoral stage, let's say sectoral and regional. Your investment in education, your investment in health, your support to SMEs, your support to regional development; These are investments that indirectly improve income distribution and social balance. By integrating these two, you provide social support and aid. We definitely do not have a perspective that consists only of social support and aid, we have a three-tiered perspective; in the form of macro, sectoral-regional and direct supports. We implemented these three in integrity, and thus, according to the calculations of the World Bank and international institutions, Türkiye has largely overcome absolute poverty. We have become a country that deals with relative poverty, which is generally the case with developed countries.

Absolute poverty figures lose their statistical meaning after a while, but relative poverty is of course an issue that we must always deal with and discuss, and we continue to work on this issue. Employment is very important here, and it is also very important in terms of income distribution. When you compare employment to population growth, we see a significant improvement in the last twenty years. Youth employment has been talked about a lot. I can also give the following figure for youth employment: While the employment participation rate of young people in EU countries increased by 1.6 points in the 2012-2023 period, it increased by 7.4 points in our country, and while it is currently 45.6 in our country, it is 41.8 in EU countries. We especially support youth employment and women's employment, and we will continue to do so.

VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - We are implementing programs for a more permanent transformation in foreign trade. We announced the program we called "HIT-30". This is a very important program; It is a 30 billion dollar project that aims to develop Turkey's domestic and national capabilities in critical technologies and attract investment. Again, we updated the advance loan with investment commitment, which we call "YTAK". Here too, we foresee a significant transformation, we foresee a technological transformation.

In the coming periods, as our fiscal space is created and our budget relaxes, we will use this area for social expenditures and the structural transformation of the real sector. In particular, we will strengthen and diversify selective finance mechanisms; We will strengthen the selective finance mechanisms that foresee and support this structural transformation.
In this structural transformation, we again place significant emphasis on vocational education and the transformation of the education system. We see the transformation in energy as very important in terms of the current account deficit. Our perspective of reducing our dependence on energy, from nuclear energy to domestic, renewable energy, to oil and gas exploration, is also directly related to the current account deficit. We want to carry the success we have achieved in sectors such as the defense industry to other areas. We evaluate that all these transformations, especially health industries and software, will contribute to a permanent decrease in our current account deficit and the construction of an economic structure with high added value.
There were many evaluations regarding agriculture. With your permission, I would like to say this about agriculture: The initial budget was increased by 47.4 percent compared to 2024 and direct supports reached 135 billion TL. While Treasury interest support was 23.3 billion in 2023, it became 86.1 billion in 2024 while providing these supports. At the same time, a question was asked, excuse me, let me answer it on this occasion; İsmail Bey asked. “Which supports count and don't?” Well, not only the direct support we provide, but also the investments we make in agriculture, the loans we subsidize, and the purchases of TMO; Of course, we list all these as support for agriculture. The OECD does more than we do. OECD also sees the support created through customs duties as agricultural support, that is, if you impose a customs tax and cause the citizen to pay more and the price of the agricultural product to increase, it assumes that the difference goes to the producer and includes it in the calculations. According to the OECD 2023 Agricultural Policies Monitoring and Evaluation Report, the ratio of our country's total resources allocated to agriculture to gross domestic product is estimated to be 1.51 percent for 2022, and the average of OECD countries is estimated to be 0.58 percent, meaning that according to this OECD methodology, the support we offer to agriculture is well above the OECD average. Of course, why shouldn't we do more? A friend of ours from the opposition said, I agree 100 percent; Agriculture is a strategic sector, a sector that will never go out of fashion. We are always on the side of agriculture and our farmers, and we will continue to be.
There was a lot of discussion about women and our women's policy. I can say that there is a similar framework here, in terms of time, only with titles. First of all, we have invested heavily in the education of our daughters and young girls; There has been a big transformation here. We will see the effects of this better in the long run. This transformation we have made may not have an immediate effect; This is the schooling rate of women and girls. We have a seriously educated female population coming from below, and we will see the social and economic consequences of this better in the coming periods.

We support women in employment and entrepreneurship, we provide bonuses, we make positive discrimination when necessary, but of course, we make some positive discrimination, provided that it is not abused. We are striving to increase the number of women in decision-making processes in many areas, especially in our Parliament. We implement multifaceted measures against violence against women. Because of the increase in penalties.

Regarding GAP - Mr. Tanal has left here - but let me say this: We prepared a new action plan.

We will announce the new GAP action plan in Şanlıurfa in the near future; We did this for DAP, we did this for KOP, we also did this for DOKAP. We will announce each of these new action plans in their respective regions, by gathering EKKs in those regions and inviting civil society and the business world; The first thing we will announce will be the GAP Action Plan. I think we can give detailed information about the projects again.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - We want realistic information about who owns the Oymapınar Dam.
PRESIDENT MEHMET MUŞ - Mr. President, thank you very much.
I'm turning on your microphone for greeting. I added an additional fifteen minutes. Therefore, please respond to the remaining questions in writing within seven days.
Please greet us and finish.
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - Thank you.
Mr. President, we thank you and all members for their contributions - whether we agree or not - for their criticisms. Of course, we will receive some ideas and suggestions that we will evaluate and shed light on the future, but of course we will clearly express those that we disagree with. What is important is to provide this democratic, quality discussion environment.
I would like to thank you and all the friends who contributed. I hope that our budget will be beneficial and bring blessings to our nation and country, and I greet you all with respect.
PRESIDENT MEHMET MUŞ - Thank you.
Dear friends, if we stop this activity, the Commission has not voted yet, there is nothing yet. Now we have listened and will decide.
Now, dear friends, today, of course, since there are general negotiations, we have been as lenient as possible with our group spokespeople, contrary to the decisions we have taken regarding the time period, we have allowed them to use the time as much as possible, but my request from you is that we have made a decision here and everyone should express their opinions on this decision in accordance with the decisions we have taken. Now, the Commission spoke for eight hours, the Government spoke for fifty minutes. Therefore, it should respond every fifty minutes to eight hours. Otherwise, if each of us wanted to talk as much as he did, we would not be able to get out of the situation.
Thank you dear friends.
Now, dear friends, the discussions on the 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal, the 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal and the Court of Accounts reports in general have been completed.
I submit to your vote the following articles of the 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal: Those who accept. Those who don't. The ayes have it.
Now, I submit to your vote the articles of the 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal: Those who accept. Those who don't. The ayes have it.
I hereby read the 1st and 2nd articles of the 2025 Central Government Budget and 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposals, respectively:
2025 CENTRAL GOVERNMENT BUDGET LAW PROPOSAL

PRESIDENT MEHMET MUŞ - Thank you.
I would like to thank our esteemed Vice President, our valuable members of parliament and all the participants.
Dear MPs, I am closing the meeting to meet on Thursday, October 31st at 10.00 to discuss the budget, final accounts and Court of Accounts reports of the Grand National Assembly of Turkey, the Ombudsman Institution and the Court of Accounts, which are in our program.
Closing Time: 20.57

fields.Subject: 2025 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Bütçe Kanunu Teklifi (1/278) ve 2023 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Kesin Hesap Kanunu Teklifi (1/277) ile Sayıştay tezkereleri münasebetiyle

records.originalTurkish:
AYHAN BARUT (Adana) - Sayın Bakanım, iyi niyetinizden hiç şüphem yok. Sizin de hassas olduğunuz iki konuda birer soru soracağım.
Ülkemizde yaklaşık 2.500 adet Tarım Kredi Kooperatifi marketi bulunmakta ancak son aldığımız haberlerde 2.500 şubede tabela değişikliği yapılacağı ve bu tabela değişikliği karşılığında da 500 milyon lira para harcanacağı duyumu alındı. 1,5 milyar görev zararı, 1,5 milyar da faiz ödeyen Tarım Kredi Kooperatiflerinin temel görevi çiftçiye destek çıkmaktır. Bu tabela değişikliği doğru mu?
İkinci sorumuz da Çanakkale Kaz Dağı eteklerinde Bayramiç ilçesinde Cengiz Holdinge ait olan Halilağa Bakır Madenindeki şirket 1 milyon ağaç kesimine başlamış. Bu ağaç kesimiyle birlikte Danıştayda yürütmeyi durdurmayı bekleyen bir dava var. Bu dava sonuçlanana kadar bekletemez misiniz?
Teşekkür ederim.
BAŞKAN MEHMET MUŞ - Teşekkür ediyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
AYHAN BARUT (Adana) - Dear Minister, I have no doubt about your good intentions. I will ask you one question each on two issues that you are sensitive about.
There are approximately 2,500 Agricultural Credit Cooperative markets in our country, but in the latest news we received, it was heard that the signboards will be changed in 2,500 branches and 500 million lira will be spent in return for this signboard change. The main duty of Agricultural Credit Cooperatives, which pay 1.5 billion in duty losses and 1.5 billion in interest, is to support farmers. Is this sign change correct?
Our second question is that the company in the Halilağa Copper Mine, which belongs to Cengiz Holding, in the Bayramiç district at the foot of Mount Kaz in Çanakkale, has started to cut down 1 million trees. With this tree cutting, there is a case waiting to stay the execution in the Council of State. Can't you wait until this case is concluded?
Thank you.
PRESIDENT MEHMET MUŞ - Thank you.

records.originalTurkish:
AYHAN BARUT (Adana) - Sayın Başkan, Sayın Bakan, değerli milletvekilleri; hepinizi saygıyla sevgiyle selamlıyorum.
Bugün burada Tarım ve Orman Bakanlığının bütçesini görüşmek için toplandık. Ulusal gıda egemenliğimiz gerçeğinden hareket edecek olursak bu bütçe teklifi sadece hesap kitaptan ibaret değildir. Bu bütçe teklifi, bu bütçe çok önemlidir. Çünkü ülke tarımını ve çiftçisini, halkın ekmeğini, gıdasını, kırsalın umudunu, milyonlarca yurttaşın bugününü ve geleceğini ilgilendirmektedir. Çünkü tarım bir ülkenin bağımsızlığının, güvenliğinin, halk sağlığının, aynı zamanda da üretim gücünün ve sosyal huzurunun da en temel unsurudur. Ancak üzülerek ifade etmek isterim ki karşımızdaki bu bütçe teklifi tarımsal üretimdeki gerilemeyi durdurmayı, çiftçimizin artan maliyetlerini ve bu maliyetler karşısında ezilmesini engellemeye yetmiyor. Gıda fiyatlarını istikrara kavuşturacak, kırsal kalkınmayı sağlayacak, yeterli bir bütçe değil. Sonda söyleyeceğimizi de başta söyleyelim, dolayısıyla bu bütçeye biz "hayır" diyoruz bunun için.
Sayın Bakan, değerli milletvekilleri; bu yıl gerçekten çiftçinin başına gelmeyen kalmadı. Neredeyse tabir yerindeyse gökten bir meteor taşı düşmediği kaldı. Bu yıl başta şubat ayında bölgemizde yaşanan aşırı don, daha sonra 10-12 Nisanda tüm ülkede yaşanan aşırı don, arkasından 9-10 Ağustosta yine 58,5 dereceyi gösteren aşırı sıcaktan dolayı meyve yanıklıkları, derken kuraklık maalesef tarımda ve üreticilere büyük zararlara neden olmuştur. Bu vesileyle ülke gıda egemenliği riske girerken aynı zamanda iktidarın uygulamış olduğu tarımsal ekonomi fiyatı da yok olan ürünlerin fiyatı da maalesef değerinde satılmamıştır.
Bugün bölgemizde limon ağaçları kesilmiştir. Bu sene pamuk, soğan, patates, karpuz, biber, hiçbiri para etmemiştir. Ayrıca erkenci mandalina şu anda 2 liraya, 1 liraya düşmüştür. Pamuk dedim Sayın Bakanım, bölgemizin, ülkemizin bir dönem simgesiydi, beyaz altın. Şu elimde gördüğünüz pamuk eskiden, AK PARTİ iktidarlarından önce 1 kilo pamuk sattığımızda, şu mazot elimde 1 litre... Bu mazot çiftçinin en temel girdilerinin başında geliyor, fiyatı 60 lirayı buldu. Eskiden 1 kilo pamuğa 2,5 litre civarında mazot alınırken bugün 2 kilo pamuk satacak ki 1 litre, belki de 2,5 kilo pamuk satacak çünkü 28 liralardan 25 liralara kadar düştü. Bu nedenle 2,5 kilo pamuğa 1 litre mazot alabiliyor.
İşte, bu yıla özgü acı gerçekler öyle büyük ki iktidarın pembe boyacısı olan TÜİK de bazı rakamları gizleyememiş ki... Çünkü açıkladıkları verilere göre bu sene toplamda meyve üretimi
yüzde 30,4 azalırken tahıllarda da yüzde 12,4 oldu. Yani rapora göre, 2024 yılında 74 milyon ton olan tahıllar ve diğer bitkisel ürünlerdeki rakam 67 milyona düştü. Yani buğdayda 20 milyon 800 bin ton üretilirken bu azalışla, maalesef 17 milyon 900 bin tona kadar inmektedir. Yağlı tohumlarda farklı mı? Yağlı tohumlarda da yine aynı; Türkiye'de üretilen soyanın yüzde 90'ı bölgemizde üretiliyor, sadece 200 bin ton kadardı, geçtiğimiz yıllarda 180 bin ton, bu sene, bu olumsuz koşullardan dolayı 148 bin tona; kütlü pamuk da 2 milyon 243 bin tondan 1 milyon 935 bin tona kadar düşüyor. Bu kara tabloya rağmen çiftçinin mazottan gübreye, zirai ilaçtan tüm maliyetlere kadar hepsi katlanarak artıyor. Burada, AK PARTİ sıralarından birçok milletvekili arkadaşım, çiftçinin çok iyi durumda olduğunu söylüyor; gerçekten çok şaşırdım, Sayın Bakanımızdan daha ateşli savundular. Ben onların hiçbirinin çiftçi olduğunu düşünmüyorum. Ben kendim hem ziraat mühendisiyim hem de Adana'da çiftçilik de yapmaktayım hem de tarım girdileriyle uğraşan bir işletmem de var. Âdeta çiftçiyle 7/24 teşrikimesaim var. Bugün, ovamızda ve ülkemizde çiftçilere bir dokunsan bin ah işitirsin. Gerçekten üreticinin hâli perişan, şu anda, hiçbir ürününden ya zirai don, kuraklıktan ya da AK PARTİ'nin yürütmüş olduğu politikalardan dolayı para kazanamamaktadır Sayın Bakan, değerli milletvekilleri. Dolayısıyla çiftçimizin acilen desteklere ihtiyacı vardır. Aynı zamanda, bugün, gün boyu neredeyse ülkemiz... Tarımda net bir şekilde ihracatçı olan arkadaşlarımıza da tarımsal ham madde bazında söylüyorum, gıda ürünleriyle ilgili değil, 2003 yılında AK PARTİ iktidarı 2,1 milyar dolar ihracat gerçekleştirirken karşılığında da 2,3 milyar dolar ithalat yaptı. 2023'e geldiğimizde 8,7 milyar dolar ihracata karşılık 13,3 milyar dolar ithalat yaptı. Yani 2003 ile 2025 yılları arasında toplamda 47 ile 49 milyar arasında tarımda net bir şekilde dış ticaret açığı bulunmaktadır.
Sayın Bakan, tarım desteklenirse yapılır, tarım korunmaya muhtaç bir sektör. Tarım tüm dünyada korunurken, desteklenirken acaba bizim ülkemizde destekleniyor mu? 2006 yılında çıkarılan bir Tarım Kanunu'nun 21'inci maddesine göre "Yüzde 1'inden aşağı olamaz." diye kesin, emredici hüküm var ancak hiçbir zaman yüzde 1'i verilmedi. Bu yılki gayrisafi millî hasılanın 77,3 trilyon olduğunu düşündüğünüzde bunun da yüzde 1'i 773 milyar destekleme olması gerekirken 168 milyar veriliyor, dolayısıyla şu anda bütçenin bile 0,9'u gayrisafi millî hasılanın beşte 1'ine kadar inmiş durumda.
Sayın Bakan, değerli meslektaşlarım; sektörümüzle alakalı tarımsal üretimin sürdürülebilirliği yalnızca bizle değil, çok sayıda meslektaşımız, çiftçiyle değil, aynı zamanda sahada çalışan teknik personelle mümkündür. Bu nedenle, ziraat mühendisinden gıda mühendisine, veteriner hekimden orman mühendislerine kadar çok sayıda meslektaşımız atama beklemektedir. Ziraat mühendisleri açısından söylemem gerekirse, ülkede 46 tane ziraat fakültesi var, her yıl 6 bin ziraat mühendisi mezunu veriyor. 2024 yılının KPSS sınavlarına 36 bin kişinin girdiğini düşündüğünüzde, yaklaşık 2 bin civarında alım yüzde 6'ya gelmektedir; bu da çok önemli rakam değildir. Sizden gençlerin ricası, bir an önce atamaları gerçekleştirmenizdir.
Sayın Bakan, değerli milletvekilleri; cumhuriyetin ilanından sonra Ulu Önder Mustafa Kemal Atatürk'ün öncülüğünde 1924 yılında Adana'da Tohum Islah ve Pamuk Üretim
İstasyonu kuruldu. Hacıali Mahallesi'nde Tarım ve Orman Bakanlığına bağlı Doğu Akdeniz Tarımsal Araştırma Enstitüsü olarak da hizmet veren yüz bir yıllık kurum arazilerinin mülkiyeti hazineye ait, tahsisi ise size, Tarım ve Orman Bakanlığına verildi ancak enstitü arazileri AKP iktidarında âdeta yağmalandı. 2002 yılında 10.160 dekar olan alanda faaliyet gösteren enstitünün önce 1.244 dekarı satıldı. 2004 yılında Millî Emlak Genel Müdürlüğü tarafından bir şirkete kiralandı 4.366 dekarı; daha sonra bu firma konkordato ilan etti, 2018'de başka bir firmaya verildi; akıbeti, kaç yıl olduğu belirsiz. 2025 yılı itibarıyla kullanılan arazinin 4.550 dekara kadar düştüğü, şimdi de bu arazilerden 4.172 dekarlık kısmı Sayın Cumhurbaşkanı Recep Tayyip Erdoğan'ın eşi Emine Erdoğan'ın himayesinde kurulan Sıfır Atık Vakfına devredilmek isteniyor. Sayın Bakanım, size bu belgeyi vereceğim. Tarım ve Orman Bakanlığı ve Çevre, Şehircilik ve İklim Değişikliği arasında olur yazısı şu anda sürüyor. Bu yazı resmî bir yazıdır, tarihini de söylüyorum size, 22 Eylül 2025 yılında...
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
TARIM VE ORMAN BAKANI İBRAHİM YUMAKLI - Yok.
AYHAN BARUT (Adana) - Yoksa teşekkür ediyorum ama bu resmî yazıyı size göndereyim.
records.englishTranslation:
AYHAN BARUT (Adana) - Mr. President, Mr. Minister, dear MPs; I greet you all with respect and love.
We gathered here today to discuss the budget of the Ministry of Agriculture and Forestry. If we start from the fact of our national food sovereignty, this budget proposal is not just about calculations. This budget proposal, this budget is very important. Because it concerns the country's agriculture and farmers, the people's bread and food, the hope of the countryside, and the present and future of millions of citizens. Because agriculture is the most fundamental element of a country's independence, security, public health, as well as production power and social peace. However, I regret to say that this budget proposal before us is not enough to stop the decline in agricultural production and prevent our farmers from being crushed by the increasing costs and these costs. It is not an adequate budget to stabilize food prices and ensure rural development. Let's say at the beginning what we will say at the end, that's why we say "no" to this budget.
Dear Minister, dear MPs; Nothing has happened to farmers this year. Almost no meteorite fell from the sky, so to speak. This year, the extreme frost experienced in our region in February, then the extreme frost experienced throughout the country on April 10-12, followed by fruit burns due to the extreme heat of 58.5 degrees on August 9-10, and drought unfortunately caused great damage to agriculture and producers. On this occasion, while the country's food sovereignty is at risk, the agricultural economy implemented by the government has unfortunately not been sold at its value.
Today, lemon trees have been cut down in our region. This year, cotton, onions, potatoes, watermelons and peppers did not make any money. In addition, early mandarin has now dropped from 2 lira to 1 lira. I said cotton, Mr. Minister, it was the symbol of our region and our country for a while, white gold. The cotton you see in my hand used to be 1 kilo of cotton, before the AK Party governments, but this diesel oil in my hand was 1 liter. This diesel fuel is one of the farmers' most basic inputs, its price reached 60 lira. While in the past, around 2.5 liters of diesel fuel was purchased for 1 kilo of cotton, today it will sell 2 kilos of cotton, which means 1 liter will sell, or maybe 2.5 kilos of cotton, because the price has dropped from 28 liras to 25 liras. For this reason, 2.5 kilos of cotton can buy 1 liter of diesel fuel.
Here, the painful facts specific to this year are so great that TÜİK, the government's pink painter, could not hide some figures. Because, according to the data they announced, total fruit production this year is
While it decreased by 30.4 percent, it also decreased by 12.4 percent in grains. In other words, according to the report, the number of grains and other plant products, which was 74 million tons in 2024, decreased to 67 million. In other words, while 20 million 800 thousand tons of wheat is produced, unfortunately this decrease goes down to 17 million 900 thousand tons. Is it different for oilseeds? It's the same with oilseeds; 90 percent of the soybean produced in Türkiye is produced in our region, it was only about 200 thousand tons, in the past years it was 180 thousand tons, this year, due to these negative conditions, it increased to 148 thousand tons; Unseeded cotton also decreases from 2 million 243 thousand tons to 1 million 935 thousand tons.

Despite this dark picture, everything from diesel to fertilizer, from pesticides to all costs of farmers is increasing exponentially. Here, many of my MP friends from the AK PARTY ranks say that farmers are in a very good situation; I was really surprised, they defended it more passionately than our Minister. I don't think any of them are farmers. I am an agricultural engineer myself, I am a farmer in Adana, and I also have a business dealing with agricultural inputs. I literally have 24/7 communication with the farmer. Today, if you touch the farmers in our plains and in our country, you will hear a thousand sighs. The situation of the producer is really miserable, right now, he cannot make money from any of his products either due to agricultural frost, drought or the policies carried out by the AK Party, Dear Minister, dear MPs. Therefore, our farmers urgently need support. At the same time, today, it is almost our country all day long. I am telling our friends who are clear exporters in agriculture on the basis of agricultural raw materials, not food products. In 2003, the AK Party government exported 2.1 billion dollars and imported 2.3 billion dollars in return. By 2023, it imported 13.3 billion dollars against 8.7 billion dollars of exports. In other words, between 2003 and 2025, there is a net foreign trade deficit in agriculture between 47 and 49 billion in total.
Dear Minister, if agriculture is supported, it can be done, agriculture is a sector in need of protection. While agriculture is protected and supported all over the world, is it supported in our country? According to Article 21 of an Agricultural Law enacted in 2006, "It cannot be less than 1 percent." There is a definitive, imperative provision saying, but 1 percent has never been given. When you consider that this year's gross national product is 77.3 trillion, 1 percent of this should be 773 billion support, but 168 billion is given, so even 0.9 of the budget has now decreased to 1 fifth of the gross national product.
Dear Minister, dear colleagues; The sustainability of agricultural production related to our sector is possible not only with us, many of our colleagues and farmers, but also with the technical personnel working in the field. For this reason, many of our colleagues, from agricultural engineers to food engineers, from veterinarians to forest engineers, are waiting for appointment. In terms of agricultural engineers, there are 46 agricultural faculties in the country, producing 6 thousand agricultural engineer graduates every year. When you consider that 36 thousand people will take the KPSS exams of 2024, approximately 2 thousand recruitments come to 6 percent; This is not a very significant number either. The young people's request is that you make the appointments as soon as possible.
Dear Minister, dear MPs; After the declaration of the republic, Seed Breeding and Cotton Production was established in Adana in 1924 under the leadership of the Great Leader Mustafa Kemal Atatürk.
The station was established. The ownership of the lands of the one hundred and one-year-old institution, which also serves as the Eastern Mediterranean Agricultural Research Institute affiliated with the Ministry of Agriculture and Forestry in Hacıali District, belongs to the treasury, and the allocation was given to you, the Ministry of Agriculture and Forestry, but the lands of the institute were literally plundered under the AKP government. 10th in 2002.

Operating on an area of 160 decares, 1,244 decares of the institute were sold first. 4,366 decares were leased to a company by the General Directorate of National Real Estate in 2004; Later, this company declared concordat, and it was given to another company in 2018; Its fate and how many years are unknown. As of 2025, the land used has decreased to 4,550 decares, and now 4,172 decares of these lands are wanted to be transferred to the Zero Waste Foundation, established under the auspices of Emine Erdoğan, wife of President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan. Dear Minister, I will give you this document. The agreement between the Ministry of Agriculture and Forestry and Environment, Urbanization and Climate Change is currently ongoing. This article is an official article, I am also telling you the date, September 22, 2025.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FORESTRY İBRAHİM YUMAKLI - None.
AYHAN BARUT (Adana) - Otherwise, I thank you, but let me send you this official letter.

records.originalTurkish: AYHAN BARUT (Adana) - Atatürk'ün mirası Atatürk Orman Çiftliği'nde AOÇ markalı güvenilir gıdaların özel bir firma tarafından fason üretildiği doğru mudur? Kamuoyuna yansıyan bu iddialar ışığında Atatürk'ün adını taşıyan bir markanın özel çıkarlar için kullanılmasına ilişkin idari ve hukuki bir inceleme başlatılacak mıdır?
records.englishTranslation: AYHAN BARUT (Adana) - Is it true that AOÇ branded reliable foods are subcontracted by a private company in Atatürk Forest Farm, Atatürk's legacy? In light of these allegations reflected in the public, will an administrative and legal investigation be initiated regarding the use of a trademark bearing Atatürk's name for private interests?

records.originalTurkish:
AYHAN BARUT (Adana) - Sayın Başkan, Sayın Bakan, değerli milletvekilleri; hepinizi saygıyla sevgiyle selamlıyorum.
Bugün burada Tarım ve Orman Bakanlığının bütçesini görüşmek için toplandık. Ulusal gıda egemenliğimiz gerçeğinden hareket edecek olursak bu bütçe teklifi sadece hesap kitaptan ibaret değildir. Bu bütçe teklifi, bu bütçe çok önemlidir. Çünkü ülke tarımını ve çiftçisini, halkın ekmeğini, gıdasını, kırsalın umudunu, milyonlarca yurttaşın bugününü ve geleceğini ilgilendirmektedir. Çünkü tarım bir ülkenin bağımsızlığının, güvenliğinin, halk sağlığının, aynı zamanda da üretim gücünün ve sosyal huzurunun da en temel unsurudur. Ancak üzülerek ifade etmek isterim ki karşımızdaki bu bütçe teklifi tarımsal üretimdeki gerilemeyi durdurmayı, çiftçimizin artan maliyetlerini ve bu maliyetler karşısında ezilmesini engellemeye yetmiyor. Gıda fiyatlarını istikrara kavuşturacak, kırsal kalkınmayı sağlayacak, yeterli bir bütçe değil. Sonda söyleyeceğimizi de başta söyleyelim, dolayısıyla bu bütçeye biz "hayır" diyoruz bunun için.
Sayın Bakan, değerli milletvekilleri; bu yıl gerçekten çiftçinin başına gelmeyen kalmadı. Neredeyse tabir yerindeyse gökten bir meteor taşı düşmediği kaldı. Bu yıl başta şubat ayında bölgemizde yaşanan aşırı don, daha sonra 10-12 Nisanda tüm ülkede yaşanan aşırı don, arkasından 9-10 Ağustosta yine 58,5 dereceyi gösteren aşırı sıcaktan dolayı meyve yanıklıkları, derken kuraklık maalesef tarımda ve üreticilere büyük zararlara neden olmuştur. Bu vesileyle ülke gıda egemenliği riske girerken aynı zamanda iktidarın uygulamış olduğu tarımsal ekonomi fiyatı da yok olan ürünlerin fiyatı da maalesef değerinde satılmamıştır.
Bugün bölgemizde limon ağaçları kesilmiştir. Bu sene pamuk, soğan, patates, karpuz, biber, hiçbiri para etmemiştir. Ayrıca erkenci mandalina şu anda 2 liraya, 1 liraya düşmüştür. Pamuk dedim Sayın Bakanım, bölgemizin, ülkemizin bir dönem simgesiydi, beyaz altın. Şu elimde gördüğünüz pamuk eskiden, AK PARTİ iktidarlarından önce 1 kilo pamuk sattığımızda, şu mazot elimde 1 litre... Bu mazot çiftçinin en temel girdilerinin başında geliyor, fiyatı 60 lirayı buldu. Eskiden 1 kilo pamuğa 2,5 litre civarında mazot alınırken bugün 2 kilo pamuk satacak ki 1 litre, belki de 2,5 kilo pamuk satacak çünkü 28 liralardan 25 liralara kadar düştü. Bu nedenle 2,5 kilo pamuğa 1 litre mazot alabiliyor.
İşte, bu yıla özgü acı gerçekler öyle büyük ki iktidarın pembe boyacısı olan TÜİK de bazı rakamları gizleyememiş ki... Çünkü açıkladıkları verilere göre bu sene toplamda meyve üretimi
yüzde 30,4 azalırken tahıllarda da yüzde 12,4 oldu. Yani rapora göre, 2024 yılında 74 milyon ton olan tahıllar ve diğer bitkisel ürünlerdeki rakam 67 milyona düştü. Yani buğdayda 20 milyon 800 bin ton üretilirken bu azalışla, maalesef 17 milyon 900 bin tona kadar inmektedir. Yağlı tohumlarda farklı mı? Yağlı tohumlarda da yine aynı; Türkiye'de üretilen soyanın yüzde 90'ı bölgemizde üretiliyor, sadece 200 bin ton kadardı, geçtiğimiz yıllarda 180 bin ton, bu sene, bu olumsuz koşullardan dolayı 148 bin tona; kütlü pamuk da 2 milyon 243 bin tondan 1 milyon 935 bin tona kadar düşüyor. Bu kara tabloya rağmen çiftçinin mazottan gübreye, zirai ilaçtan tüm maliyetlere kadar hepsi katlanarak artıyor. Burada, AK PARTİ sıralarından birçok milletvekili arkadaşım, çiftçinin çok iyi durumda olduğunu söylüyor; gerçekten çok şaşırdım, Sayın Bakanımızdan daha ateşli savundular. Ben onların hiçbirinin çiftçi olduğunu düşünmüyorum. Ben kendim hem ziraat mühendisiyim hem de Adana'da çiftçilik de yapmaktayım hem de tarım girdileriyle uğraşan bir işletmem de var. Âdeta çiftçiyle 7/24 teşrikimesaim var. Bugün, ovamızda ve ülkemizde çiftçilere bir dokunsan bin ah işitirsin. Gerçekten üreticinin hâli perişan, şu anda, hiçbir ürününden ya zirai don, kuraklıktan ya da AK PARTİ'nin yürütmüş olduğu politikalardan dolayı para kazanamamaktadır Sayın Bakan, değerli milletvekilleri. Dolayısıyla çiftçimizin acilen desteklere ihtiyacı vardır. Aynı zamanda, bugün, gün boyu neredeyse ülkemiz... Tarımda net bir şekilde ihracatçı olan arkadaşlarımıza da tarımsal ham madde bazında söylüyorum, gıda ürünleriyle ilgili değil, 2003 yılında AK PARTİ iktidarı 2,1 milyar dolar ihracat gerçekleştirirken karşılığında da 2,3 milyar dolar ithalat yaptı. 2023'e geldiğimizde 8,7 milyar dolar ihracata karşılık 13,3 milyar dolar ithalat yaptı. Yani 2003 ile 2025 yılları arasında toplamda 47 ile 49 milyar arasında tarımda net bir şekilde dış ticaret açığı bulunmaktadır.
Sayın Bakan, tarım desteklenirse yapılır, tarım korunmaya muhtaç bir sektör. Tarım tüm dünyada korunurken, desteklenirken acaba bizim ülkemizde destekleniyor mu? 2006 yılında çıkarılan bir Tarım Kanunu'nun 21'inci maddesine göre "Yüzde 1'inden aşağı olamaz." diye kesin, emredici hüküm var ancak hiçbir zaman yüzde 1'i verilmedi. Bu yılki gayrisafi millî hasılanın 77,3 trilyon olduğunu düşündüğünüzde bunun da yüzde 1'i 773 milyar destekleme olması gerekirken 168 milyar veriliyor, dolayısıyla şu anda bütçenin bile 0,9'u gayrisafi millî hasılanın beşte 1'ine kadar inmiş durumda.
Sayın Bakan, değerli meslektaşlarım; sektörümüzle alakalı tarımsal üretimin sürdürülebilirliği yalnızca bizle değil, çok sayıda meslektaşımız, çiftçiyle değil, aynı zamanda sahada çalışan teknik personelle mümkündür. Bu nedenle, ziraat mühendisinden gıda mühendisine, veteriner hekimden orman mühendislerine kadar çok sayıda meslektaşımız atama beklemektedir. Ziraat mühendisleri açısından söylemem gerekirse, ülkede 46 tane ziraat fakültesi var, her yıl 6 bin ziraat mühendisi mezunu veriyor. 2024 yılının KPSS sınavlarına 36 bin kişinin girdiğini düşündüğünüzde, yaklaşık 2 bin civarında alım yüzde 6'ya gelmektedir; bu da çok önemli rakam değildir. Sizden gençlerin ricası, bir an önce atamaları gerçekleştirmenizdir.
Sayın Bakan, değerli milletvekilleri; cumhuriyetin ilanından sonra Ulu Önder Mustafa Kemal Atatürk'ün öncülüğünde 1924 yılında Adana'da Tohum Islah ve Pamuk Üretim
İstasyonu kuruldu. Hacıali Mahallesi'nde Tarım ve Orman Bakanlığına bağlı Doğu Akdeniz Tarımsal Araştırma Enstitüsü olarak da hizmet veren yüz bir yıllık kurum arazilerinin mülkiyeti hazineye ait, tahsisi ise size, Tarım ve Orman Bakanlığına verildi ancak enstitü arazileri AKP iktidarında âdeta yağmalandı. 2002 yılında 10.160 dekar olan alanda faaliyet gösteren enstitünün önce 1.244 dekarı satıldı. 2004 yılında Millî Emlak Genel Müdürlüğü tarafından bir şirkete kiralandı 4.366 dekarı; daha sonra bu firma konkordato ilan etti, 2018'de başka bir firmaya verildi; akıbeti, kaç yıl olduğu belirsiz. 2025 yılı itibarıyla kullanılan arazinin 4.550 dekara kadar düştüğü, şimdi de bu arazilerden 4.172 dekarlık kısmı Sayın Cumhurbaşkanı Recep Tayyip Erdoğan'ın eşi Emine Erdoğan'ın himayesinde kurulan Sıfır Atık Vakfına devredilmek isteniyor. Sayın Bakanım, size bu belgeyi vereceğim. Tarım ve Orman Bakanlığı ve Çevre, Şehircilik ve İklim Değişikliği arasında olur yazısı şu anda sürüyor. Bu yazı resmî bir yazıdır, tarihini de söylüyorum size, 22 Eylül 2025 yılında...
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
TARIM VE ORMAN BAKANI İBRAHİM YUMAKLI - Yok.
AYHAN BARUT (Adana) - Yoksa teşekkür ediyorum ama bu resmî yazıyı size göndereyim.

Because agriculture is the most fundamental element of a country's independence, security, public health, as well as production power and social peace.
